entries.title: #272: Suggestion: Word Add-In and DOCX Editing Feature — AI-Powered Document Redlining with Live Tracked Changes
entries.text:
Suggestion: Word Add-In and DOCX Editing Feature — AI-Powered Document Redlining with Live Tracked Changes ## Feature Suggestion: Word Add-In & DOCX Editing

This issue proposes a **Chat & Edit Legal Docx** feature — AI-powered document editing with live redlines, designed as both a web editor and MS Word Add-In.

### What This Is

A Vault + Document Editor chat surface where lawyers can:
- Upload matter bundles and chat with AI about documents
- Draft new documents or propose redlines against precedent
- Review AI-generated edits as **live tracked changes** (Word native or web redlines)
- Confirm writes before any mutation (human-in-the-loop)
- Export clean DOCX without compromising privilege

### Draft Documents

Two documents outlining the feature (drafts for discussion and refinement before a proper PR):

1. **[PRD — Product Requirements Document](https://files.098020.xyz/13c6e641-77d6-4ae4-8438-30974a2f4afb/prd-template-revised.md)** — 84 functional requirements, 9 non-functional requirements, 10 user stories. Covers scope, UX design, AI-specific requirements, governance compliance, competitive context, and edge cases.

2. **[Technical Reference](https://files.098020.xyz/481e79c4-e38a-4162-a738-3cb098a28c0a/technical-spec-template.md)** — Non-binding technical guidance covering architecture, data models, API contracts, web editor options (ProseMirror + Adeu), Word Add-In integration (Adeu-driven OOXML mutations via Office.js shim), and implementation task breakdown.

### Status

These are **drafts** — intended as a starting point for discussion and refinement before a proper PR. The PRD identifies clear in-scope/out-of-scope boundaries for v1. The technical reference is guidance, not a contract.

### Parent Epic

DE-287 (Word Add-In Feature Surface) — OAuth plumbing, manifest, and version handshake are already shipped at v0.3.0.

entries.title: #271: Add EUIPO Trademark Register as a WS-E authority source
entries.text:
Add EUIPO Trademark Register as a WS-E authority source ### Prerequisites

- [x] I have searched existing issues and the PRD §9 deferred-enhancements list.

### Use case

As a trademark attorney using LQ.AI for IP matters, I want the platform to look up EU trademark register entries (via the EUIPO Trademark Search API) as a citable authority source, so that I can get live, verified answers about EU trademark registrations (status, owner, filing date, Nice classes) instead of relying on the model's training data or a separate manual search.

### Why this can't be met today

The current WS-E sources (GovInfo, SEC EDGAR, EUR-Lex) cover US federal law, US corporate filings, and EU legislation/case law — none of them cover trademark register data. Today, a user has to leave the platform and search the EUIPO register manually (e.g., via TMview), then copy the result back in. That workaround loses the citation/ledger/fiduciary-grade verification WS-E already provides for its other sources: there is no audit trail, and the agentic loop cannot independently verify a trademark claim (e.g., "is this mark registered and by whom") the way it can for a statute or a filing — reintroducing exactly the fabrication risk WS-E is designed to close for other authority types.

### Proposed approach

Follow the same registry pattern already shipped for GovInfo/SEC EDGAR/EUR-Lex (ADR 0021, D1–D7), adding EUIPO as a fourth source rather than a new mechanism:

- **Gateway layer:** a new `tool_providers` entry for EUIPO in `gateway.yaml`, same shape as the existing sources. One difference to flag: the EUIPO API uses OAuth2 client-credentials auth (client ID + secret exchanged for a token), not a single API key — the adapter would need to handle the token exchange/refresh, similar to how any OAuth2-based provider would fit the existing auth fields.
- **Backend layer:** a `registry.py` entry with `jurisdiction: eu-trademark`, and a new `content_type` tag (`trademark-register`) alongside the existing statutes/regulations/caselaw/filings tags — since a register entry (mark, owner, status, filing date, Nice classes) is a structurally different content type from any of the three shipped sources.
- **Retrieval:** reuses the existing generic `retrieve_authority` intent (D2) — no new intent needed.
- **Citation verification:** reuses the existing shared `verify()` core via an in-memory authority target (per the D3 refinement) — no new verifier, same as GovInfo/SEC EDGAR/EUR-Lex.
- **Reference implementation:** I have a working, OAuth2-based EUIPO connector (covering both Trademark Search and Goods & Services APIs), verified live against the EUIPO production API, built together with a developer collaborator — happy to use it as the basis for the adapter's parsing logic.

Happy to write a fuller PR1-style spec (mirroring how WS-E PR1a/PR2a/PR2b were scoped) if there's appetite for this.

### Related DE-### entry (if any)

None found — new source proposal within the existing WS-E workstream (ADR 0021), not yet tracked as its own DE-### item.

### Affected subsystem

Backend (API)

### Alternatives considered

- **Leave EUIPO lookups as a manual, out-of-platform workaround.** Rejected — this reintroduces exactly the citation/audit gap WS-E is designed to close for other authority types.
- **Build EUIPO as a standalone MCP tool-provider outside the WS-E registry pattern.** Rejected — would duplicate the verification/ledger machinery WS-E already provides (D3's reuse-not-fork principle), and would lose the honest-availability/coverage reporting of D5.
- **Rely on operators bringing their own EUIPO connector via BYO-MCP only.** Rejected as the sole path — the EUIPO API is free-of-licensing-cost, like the other WS-E sources, so it fits the "free-source expansion" thesis rather than being licensed BYO content.
- **Ship a full EU IP practice module (connector + several skills) in one PR.** Rejected for this issue — too large a first step. Better to land the source/adapter first (per D6's phased-build approach), with skills consuming it afterward as separate contributions.

### Additional context

For track record in this domain: I've contributed three EU trademark skills to `lq-skills` (clearance search under Art. 8(1)(b) EUTMR with a CJEU case-law corpus, goods & services classification, and homoglyph/look-alike detection), all built and verified against the EUIPO production API. Happy to follow up with a fuller technical spec if there's interest in taking this forward.

entries.title: #258: [Feature] Inline chat reference call @document and from dropdown
entries.text:
[Feature] Inline chat reference call @document and from dropdown ### Prerequisites

- [x] I have searched existing issues and the PRD §9 deferred-enhancements list.

### Use case

Allow a user to reference specific documents from the current matter inside a chat message by typing an inline @filename mention or picking files from a multi-select dropdown. User can ask questions about the specific documents, getting an answer with verified citations that deep-link into the doc panel. Both interfaces feed the same authoritative referenced-files set sent on the message.

### Why this can't be met today

1. Attaching a file via the chat interface or as a file lets the model read it but can never produce a citation. The per-message file_ids channel injects each file's full text verbatim (chats.py:415/1129/1494) but citations are minted only from retrieved_chunks — attached-file contexts are never passed to _persist_message_citations (chats.py:2560/2604). 

2. No GET /api/v1/files list route to populate a picker/@-dropdown, and no composer picker/@ UI exists.

### Proposed approach

 - Add a new referenced_file_ids field (don't overload file_ids); route referenced files through retrieval as chunks so they land in retrieved_chunks and flow to the existing Citation Engine + deep-link viewer.
  - Composer multi-select picker + @-mention both feed one deduped set; disable un-parsed rows.
  - Reuse the shipped Citation Engine, verification cascade, and doc-panel viewer unchanged.
  - Phase: picker MVP → @-mention

### Related DE-### entry (if any)

DE-296

### Affected subsystem

Backend (API), Web (chat UI)

### Alternatives considered

Decisions to surface

1. New referenced_file_ids field vs. repurposing file_ids (recommend new — non-breaking).
2. Embedding of non-KB referenced files: KB-only MVP vs. embed-on-reference.
3. Picker scope: matter-scoped vs. owner-scoped.
4. Retrieval strategy: per-file budget vs. global top-k.

### Additional context

_No response_

entries.title: #256: [Feature] DE-201 / DE-202 / DE-208 / DE-209: agree WorkspaceEvent, MCP email, Today View, and Email Triage architecture
entries.text:
[Feature] DE-201 / DE-202 / DE-208 / DE-209: agree WorkspaceEvent, MCP email, Today View, and Email Triage architecture ### Prerequisites

- [x] I have searched existing issues and the PRD §9 deferred-enhancements list.

### Use case

I would like to contribute a phased M5 Workflow Intelligence slice covering Today View, a WorkspaceEvent / Signal Aggregation substrate if maintainers agree this is the right boundary, MCP-backed read-only email ingestion, and advisory Email Triage.

The user-facing goal is a chronological Today View that can initially surface internal LQ.AI work signals, and later display email-derived signals from an operator-approved mailbox connector.

Email Triage should classify incoming messages and propose next steps, but not perform mailbox mutations.

### Why this can't be met today


DE-200 appears to provide the MCP foundation this work should build on: gateway-brokered MCP discovery, OAuth, governed tool invocation, audit, and confirmation support.

However, the M5 workflow-intelligence layer is not yet present. The current gaps appear to be:

* No WorkspaceEvent / Signal Aggregation persistence and query layer.
* No MCP-backed email connector contract or sync lifecycle.
* No Today View route or Today API.
* No Email Triage skill/service implementation.
* No connector-derived data retention/export/delete rules.
* No email-specific connector admin visibility.
* No provenance contract linking source → event → triage → Today item.

This issue proposes resolving the architecture and contracts first, before implementation PRs.

### Proposed approach

I propose opening with a docs-only ADR before implementation. The ADR would lock the WorkspaceEvent / Today View / MCP-backed email connector boundary, then implementation would proceed in separate reviewable slices:

1. **Architecture/contracts ADR** — define WorkspaceEvent, TodayItem, email connector trust boundary, retention/export/delete rules, audit expectations, operator controls, and non-goals.
2. **Internal WorkspaceEvent substrate** — create the backend event layer using internal LQ.AI sources only.
3. **Read-only Today View** — add `/lq-ai/today` as a chronological, explainable internal-signal view.
4. **MCP-backed read-only email connector** — consume email through an operator-configured MCP server and the existing gateway/governed tool path.
5. **Advisory Email Triage** — classify normalized email events and produce structured, explainable results.
6. **Proposed actions / mutations** — future separate issues only; no auto-actions in this proposal.

The detailed architecture, principles alignment, likely files, tests, docs, risks, and non-goals are in Additional context below.

### Related DE-### entry (if any)

DE-200, DE-201, DE-202, DE-208, DE-209

### Affected subsystem

Skills, Documentation, Inference Gateway, Backend (API), Web (chat UI), Compliance / Procurement, Other / unsure

### Alternatives considered

### Alternatives considered

**Direct provider-specific integration** — rejected for this proposal. This would mean adding Gmail, Microsoft Graph mailbox, Exchange, or IMAP clients directly inside `api/` or `web/`. That appears inconsistent with the existing MCP/gateway connector direction because it would bypass the gateway egress boundary, operator-controlled tool enablement, MCP audit/governance path, and connector portability.

**Single combined implementation PR** — rejected. Combining WorkspaceEvent, Today View, email connector, triage, proposed actions, and mutations would be too large to review safely and would hide architecture decisions inside code.

**Today View without WorkspaceEvent** — possible, but needs maintainer input. A frontend-composed Today View using existing endpoints may be quicker for v1, but a backend WorkspaceEvent substrate appears cleaner if email/calendar/task signals are expected later.

### Additional context

#### Revalidated architecture

Correct flow:

```text
Operator-configured MCP server
→ Gateway MCP adapter
→ governed_tool_invocation
→ connector sync service
→ WorkspaceEvent normalization
→ Today projection
→ Email Triage skill
→ advisory ProposedAction
```

Forbidden flow:

```text
api/web → Gmail
api/web → Microsoft Graph mailbox
api/web → IMAP/Exchange
user-supplied MCP URL
connector-specific governance fork
triage → automatic mutation
```

#### Architecture decisions needed from maintainers

1. Should the first PR be a docs-only ADR locking the WorkspaceEvent / Today / Email connector boundary?
2. Should the reference email MCP server live in a separate repo/package rather than the core repo?
3. Should WorkspaceEvent be the canonical substrate before Today View, or should Today v1 start with a lighter projection?
4. Should Today use a backend `/api/v1/today` endpoint, or compose existing endpoints client-side for v1?
5. Should connector sync be manual/operator-triggered first, with scheduled polling deferred?
6. What minimum email fields may be retained in product tables, if any?
7. Should raw email bodies be fetched only on demand rather than retained by default?
8. What is the disconnect behaviour: retain derived data, purge derived data, or user choice?
9. What is the default retention period for connector-derived events, triage results, cursors, and dedupe identifiers?
10. How should shared/team mailbox ownership be represented?
11. Should connector admin visibility extend `/admin/mcp`, or should there be a separate `/admin/connectors` surface?
12. Should Email Triage v1 use full body, bounded excerpt, or metadata/snippet only?
13. Should reply drafting be deferred from Email Triage v1?
14. Should HONEST-STATE only be updated after each shipped slice rather than after the architecture PR?

#### Proposed data contracts

##### WorkspaceEvent

Minimum candidate fields:

```text
id
user_id
source_kind
source_ref
event_type
status
occurred_at
observed_at
project_id?
provenance
sensitivity
dedupe_digest
created_at
updated_at
```

##### SourceProvenance

Candidate fields:

```text
source_kind
source_id or opaque reference
connector_name
provider_name
tool_name
observed_at
produced_at
last_synced_at
stale_as_of
routed_tier
audit_or_receipt_ref?
```

##### TodayItem

Candidate fields:

```text
id
workspace_event_id
title
summary
status
source_kind
source_ref
occurred_at
display_at
stale_as_of?
project_id?
reason_codes
confidence/status
provenance
available_actions
```

##### EmailSignal

Candidate fields:

```text
workspace_event_id
message_ref
thread_ref?
received_at
mailbox_label_or_folder?
has_attachments
snippet_or_excerpt?
sender_ref?
recipient_refs?
subject_ref_or_encrypted_subject?
```

Open question: which email fields can be retained in product tables, and under what encryption/retention/export/delete rules?

##### EmailTriageResult

Candidate fields:

```text
event_id
classification
legal_relevance
confidence
reason_codes
evidence_refs
suggested_next_step
skill_name
skill_version
routed_tier
status: classified | needs_review | failed
created_at
```

##### ProposedAction

Candidate fields:

```text
id
event_id
triage_result_id
kind
label
rationale
requires_confirmation: true
status: pending | dismissed | confirmed
created_at
updated_at
```

#### Guiding-principles alignment

This issue is intended to agree the architecture before implementation. The answers below apply to the proposed architecture and should become acceptance criteria for later PRs.

1. **Egress — Yes.** External email access would go through an operator-configured MCP server, the existing gateway MCP adapter, and the governed tool path. This proposal rejects direct Gmail, Microsoft Graph mailbox, Exchange, or IMAP clients inside `api/` or `web/`.

2. **Closed set — Yes.** Users and models would only be able to use operator-enabled connectors and MCP tools. The design does not allow arbitrary user-supplied MCP URLs, provider URLs, provider names, or unrestricted tool names.

3. **Payloads — Yes, subject to domain-data decision.** Audit rows, operational logs, traces, metrics, exception text, and test snapshots would contain only IDs, counts, types, digests, source kinds, tiers, and outcomes. They must not contain email bodies, subjects, sender/recipient addresses, headers, attachment names/content, OAuth material, tool arguments/results, or raw MCP payloads. Any retention of email-derived content in user-owned product tables is a separate user-data / data-minimisation decision and should be approved before the first persistence PR.

4. **Fail direction — Yes.** Missing configuration, revoked or expired OAuth, unknown or disabled providers/tools, malformed payloads, unavailable MCP servers, invalid cursors, and uncertain triage results would fail into explicit unavailable, failed, stale, or `needs_review` states. They would not fall back to direct provider access, stale-token use, permissive classification, or inferred successful sync.

5. **Atomicity — Yes, for local state changes.** Connector configuration changes, enable/disable, disconnect/revoke, cursor advancement, WorkspaceEvent persistence, Today dismissals, EmailTriageResult creation, ProposedAction creation/dismissal, and any future confirmed mutation should write payload-free audit records in the same DB transaction as the local state change. External MCP reads cannot be part of a DB transaction, so cursor state should advance only after the response is validated and local persistence succeeds.

6. **Reuse the governance path — Yes.** Connector discovery, sync, triage input collection, and later actions should reuse the existing gateway MCP adapter, API gateway client, and `governed_tool_invocation` path. This proposal does not introduce a second provider-tier, cost, OAuth, rate-limit, audit, or tool-policy path.

7. **Irreversibility — Yes.** Email Triage v1 would be advisory only. It may classify, explain, and propose next steps, but it must not send, archive, delete, move, label, forward, or create tasks. Any later mutation should be a separate operator-enabled governed tool with explicit user confirmation, idempotency, and atomic audit.

8. **Operator control — Yes, with maintainer decision needed on surface.** Email connector availability, enabled tools, auth mode, scope summary, health/last-sync outcome, stale state, and disable/revoke controls should be visible to the operator. Secrets and mailbox payloads should never be shown. Maintainers need to decide whether this extends `/admin/mcp` or becomes a connector-status projection.

9. **User data — Yes, but lifecycle decisions are blocking.** Connector-derived WorkspaceEvents, EmailSignals, EmailTriageResults, ProposedActions, cursors, dedupe identifiers, and connector tokens should be treated as user-owned data. Export, account deletion, retention, disconnect, and purge behaviour must be defined before persistence ships. Account deletion should remove derived content and credentials while preserving only payload-free audit history with anonymised actor identity.

10. **Contract & docs — Yes.** Each shipped code PR should update the relevant OpenAPI contract, DB schema documentation, ADR/PRD/architecture material, deployment/configuration guidance, and security documentation. HONEST-STATE should be updated only for the exact slice actually shipped, not for the whole M5 workflow.

11. **Tests as part of the change — Yes.** Later PRs should include invariant tests that fail if core code adds direct provider clients, accepts arbitrary connector URLs, bypasses governed invocation, records sensitive payloads, advances cursors after partial failure, permits disabled/revoked connectors, executes triage mutations, breaks user scoping/export/deletion, or emits provenance-free Today items.

12. **No black box — Yes.** Today items and triage results should carry enough provenance for a user or admin to understand why the item appeared: source kind, connector/provider, tool, skill/version, relevant timestamps, freshness/staleness, reason codes, confidence/status, routed tier, and linked matter/project where applicable. Today v1 should be chronological and explainable, not a hidden ranking system.

#### Domain-data clarification

The no-payload rule forbids raw payloads in audit rows, operational logs, traces, metrics, exceptions, and snapshots. Any retention of email-derived content in user-owned product tables remains separately subject to data minimisation, encryption, export, deletion, residency, and bounded-retention decisions.

Generic raw MCP payload persistence is prohibited. The first implementation PR is blocked until maintainers approve the minimum retained email fields.

#### Phase-by-phase likely files

##### Phase 0 — ADR/contracts

Likely files:

```text
docs/adr/00xx-workspace-events-and-email-connectors.md
docs/PRD.md
docs/architecture.md
docs/ROADMAP.md
docs/security/threat-model.md
docs/security/audit-logging.md
```

Patterns to copy:

```text
docs/adr/0014-*
docs/adr/0015-*
docs/adr/0016-*
docs/proposals/legal-research-and-mcp.md
```

Non-goals:

```text
No code
No migration
No endpoint
No UI
No connector deployment
No HONEST-STATE shipped claim
```

##### Phase 1 — WorkspaceEvent backend

Likely files:

```text
api/app/models/workspace_event.py
api/app/schemas/workspace_events.py
api/app/workspace_events/service.py
api/app/api/workspace_events.py
api/alembic/versions/00xx_workspace_events.py
api/app/api/__init__.py
docs/api/backend-openapi.yaml
docs/db-schema.md
```

Patterns to copy:

```text
api/app/api/notifications.py
api/app/api/projects.py
api/app/api/chats.py
api/app/models/*
api/app/audit*
api/tests/test_openapi.py
api/tests/test_endpoints.py
```

Non-goals:

```text
No external connector
No email
No ranking
No triage
No UI
```

##### Phase 2 — Today View

Likely files:

```text
api/app/api/today.py
api/app/today/service.py
api/app/schemas/today.py
web/src/routes/lq-ai/today/+page.svelte
web/src/lib/lq-ai/api/today.ts
web/src/lib/lq-ai/components/today/*
web/src/lib/lq-ai/types.ts
web/cypress/e2e/*
```

Patterns to copy:

```text
web/src/routes/lq-ai/autonomous/*
web/src/routes/lq-ai/matters/*
web/src/lib/lq-ai/api/*
web/src/lib/lq-ai/components/*
```

Non-goals:

```text
No email
No calendar
No ranking
No LLM prioritisation
No mutations
No Word add-in parity
```

##### Phase 3 — Email MCP connector

Likely core repo files:

```text
mcp.yaml.example
api/app/connectors/*
api/app/mcp/*
api/app/tools/governance.py
api/app/clients/gateway.py
api/app/api/admin_mcp.py
gateway/app/providers/tool/mcp.py
gateway/app/providers/tool/egress.py
docs/deployment*
docs/security/*
docs/api/backend-openapi.yaml
```

External package:

```text
reference Gmail/Microsoft 365 MCP server
```

Patterns to copy:

```text
api/app/mcp/service.py
api/app/mcp/oauth.py
api/app/api/mcp_oauth.py
api/app/tools/governance.py
gateway/app/providers/tool/mcp.py
gateway/app/api/tools.py
```

Non-goals:

```text
No direct Gmail/Graph/IMAP clients in api/web
No send/move/archive/delete
No attachments
No full inbox mirror
No arbitrary MCP URLs
```

##### Phase 4 — Email Triage

Likely files:

```text
skills/email-triage/SKILL.md
skills/email-triage/examples/*
api/app/email_triage/service.py
api/app/schemas/email_triage.py
api/app/models/email_triage.py
api/alembic/versions/00xx_email_triage.py
docs/skill-authoring-guide.md if needed
```

Patterns to copy:

```text
skills/CONTRIBUTING.md
existing built-in skills
api/app/skills*
gateway inference path
structured output validation patterns
```

Non-goals:

```text
No automatic actions
No reply sending
No folder routing execution
No task creation
No background autonomous triage
```

##### Phase 5+ — Proposed actions and mutations

Separate issue/PR sequence only.

Likely patterns:

```text
chat pending tool confirmation
governed_tool_invocation
audit_action
tool metadata requires_confirmation
```

Non-goals for this issue:

```text
No mutation implementation
No autonomous grants for destructive tools
```

#### Required tests

##### Architecture/invariant tests

* Static failure if `api/` or `web/` adds Gmail, Graph mailbox, Exchange, IMAP, or direct MCP SDK clients outside the approved gateway path.
* Failure if user-facing connector schemas accept arbitrary provider URLs or MCP URLs.
* Failure if connector sync bypasses `governed_tool_invocation`.
* Failure if sensitive sentinel email/OAuth values appear in logs, spans, audit rows, exceptions, or snapshots.
* Failure if disabled/revoked connector can still be invoked.
* Failure if unknown/disabled provider or tool falls back permissively.
* Failure if triage has access to mutation-capable tools.
* Failure if Today item or triage result lacks provenance.

##### Backend tests

* WorkspaceEvent user isolation.
* Dedupe/idempotency.
* Time filtering and chronological query.
* Cursor rollback on partial ingestion failure.
* Atomic WorkspaceEvent/cursor/audit transaction.
* Export/delete coverage for connector-derived tables.
* Connector disconnect/revoke behaviour.
* Triage schema validation.
* ProposedAction persistence/dismissal when that phase arrives.

##### Gateway tests

* MCP discovery/invocation against mock email MCP server.
* OAuth token passthrough.
* SSRF/HTTPS/host allowlist refusal.
* DNS rebinding / public IP validation where existing patterns support it.
* Rate-limit/timeout/malformed MCP payload handling.
* No token/body/tool payload leakage.

##### Web tests

* Today loading/empty/error/populated states.
* Chronological sorting.
* Timezone display.
* Source/provenance display.
* Stale/unavailable connector display.
* Dismiss/confirm UI only when corresponding backend phase exists.
* Responsive/accessibility checks.

##### E2E tests

* Internal-only Today route works with no connectors configured.
* Mock MCP email server syncs messages into WorkspaceEvents.
* Email triage produces advisory result.
* Proposed action appears but does not execute.
* Disable/revoke connector prevents next sync/invocation.
* No action occurs before explicit confirmation.

#### Documentation required

* New ADR for WorkspaceEvent / Today projection / MCP email connector boundary.
* `docs/PRD.md`.
* `docs/architecture.md`.
* `docs/ROADMAP.md`.
* `docs/HONEST-STATE.md` only when a slice actually ships.
* `docs/api/backend-openapi.yaml`.
* `docs/api/gateway-openapi.yaml` only if gateway transport changes.
* `docs/db-schema.md`.
* Deployment/config docs.
* `mcp.yaml.example`.
* Connector authoring/security guide.
* Threat model / audit logging docs.
* Export/delete/retention docs.

#### Risks to flag

* Ingestion of privileged/confidential email.
* OAuth overscoping.
* Prompt injection inside email bodies, headers, HTML, or attachments.
* Cross-user or cross-matter leakage.
* Logging/tracing/audit leakage of sensitive mailbox data.
* Retention mismatch after connector disconnect.
* Stale cursor or stale token reuse.
* Duplicate/replayed messages and cursor loss.
* External MCP server compromise or supply-chain risk.
* Malicious MCP tool annotations.
* Rate-limit storms and expensive full-mailbox sync.
* Attachment malware or hidden payloads.
* Misleading triage confidence.
* Today silently becoming opaque ranking.
* Transactional gap between external mailbox read and local cursor commit.
* Shared mailbox ownership ambiguity.
* Data residency and inference-tier violations.

#### Non-goals

* Direct Gmail, Microsoft Graph mailbox, Exchange, or IMAP clients in the core repo.
* Arbitrary user-supplied MCP URLs.
* Full inbox mirror.
* Attachment ingestion.
* Sending, archiving, deleting, moving, labelling, or forwarding email.
* Task creation.
* Autonomous/background triage.
* Ranked Today priorities.
* Calendar connector.
* Word add-in Today parity.
* Mobile-native app.
* Provider-specific UI.
* One combined implementation PR.
* HONEST-STATE milestone-level shipped claim before actual shipped slices.

#### Suggested first PR

The safest first PR is:

```text
Docs-only ADR: WorkspaceEvent, Today projection, and MCP email trust boundary.
```

This PR should not implement code. It should lock the architecture, contracts, data lifecycle, audit expectations, operator control, and phased implementation sequence.

#### First PR acceptance criteria

* ADR reviewed by maintainers, and security reviewer if required.
* P1–P12 alignment defined.
* Direct Gmail/Graph/IMAP clients explicitly rejected.
* Operator-controlled closed connector set defined.
* WorkspaceEvent and provenance fields defined.
* Domain-data versus audit/log payload boundary defined.
* Failure and stale-data semantics defined.
* Every auditable state change listed.
* Export/delete/disconnect/retention decisions recorded or explicitly left as maintainer-blocking decisions.
* Today v1 defined as chronological and explainable.
* Email Triage v1 defined as advisory only.
* `mcp.yaml` versus admin DB ownership clarified.
* Phased PR sequence and non-goals pinned.
* No code, migration, endpoint, UI, connector, or shipped-state claim.

#### Later acceptance criteria

* WorkspaceEvent layer is scoped, deduplicated, auditable, exportable, and deletable.
* Today View is chronological, responsive, and provenance-visible.
* Email connector is MCP/gateway-only, read-only, scope-limited, immediately disable-able/revocable, and resumable.
* Email Triage is structured, versioned, explainable, uncertainty-aware, and mutation-free.
* ProposedActions are inert until separately confirmed.
* Mutations, if ever added, are separate governed tools with explicit human approval and atomic audit.

entries.title: #244: Acceptance tests for skill-creator skill
entries.text:
Acceptance tests for skill-creator skill ## Intent
Claiming the acceptance-test pass for the **skill-creator** meta-skill, per the
[skill-acceptance-tests mini-PRD](https://github.com/LegalQuants/lq-ai/blob/main/docs/contribute/mini-prds/skill-acceptance-tests.md)
(tracked as [DE-051](https://github.com/LegalQuants/lq-ai/blob/main/docs/PRD.md#de-051--acceptance-testing-for-the-m1-skill-set-against-real-documents) / [DE-236](https://github.com/LegalQuants/lq-ai/blob/main/docs/PRD.md#de-236--acceptance-tests-for-the-10-starter-skills)).
No `acceptance/` directory exists for this skill yet.

## Who I am
Senior Privacy Counsel at a global technology company; licensed in Arizona. My practice
often requires distilling legal text into concise, plain, and practical language for many different
audiences daily. I'll provide the practicing-attorney attestation
([skills/CONTRIBUTING.md §3](https://github.com/LegalQuants/lq-ai/blob/main/skills/CONTRIBUTING.md))
at PR time and sign off commits per the DCO.

## Corpus-sourcing
Per [skills/skill-creator/test-plan.md](https://github.com/LegalQuants/lq-ai/blob/main/skills/skill-creator/test-plan.md),
I'll author 5–8 skill-request scenarios covering the required range: a simple review skill, a
perspective-branching skill, an optional-input skill, a transformation skill, an extraction skill,
a fork-an-existing-skill request, and at least one deliberately ambiguous request.

## What I'll grade
For each generated skill: frontmatter validity, adherence to the conventions in
[docs/skill-authoring-guide.md](https://github.com/LegalQuants/lq-ai/blob/main/docs/skill-authoring-guide.md),
operational elicitation questions and sensible defaults, conservative posture (defers
enforceability, invents no citations, enumerates "what this skill does not do"), out-of-scope
refusal, and appropriate scope - whether the skill-creator produces lean, well-scoped skills and
avoids overbuilding (bloated frontmatter, unnecessary inputs, over-broad workflows).

## Run method
Assemble the skill and run each scenario across two models - **Claude Sonnet 4.6** (extended
thinking) and **Gemini 3.5 Flash** (thinking enabled). The exact resolved model IDs are recorded in
`results.md` at run time.

## Deliverable
A new `skills/skill-creator/acceptance/` directory: `inputs/` (the scenarios), `expected/`,
`outputs/` (per scenario × model, including the generated SKILL.md files), and `results.md`.

entries.title: #243: Acceptance tests for comms-improver skill
entries.text:
Acceptance tests for comms-improver skill ## Intent
Claiming the acceptance-test pass for **comms-improver**, per the
[skill-acceptance-tests mini-PRD](https://github.com/LegalQuants/lq-ai/blob/main/docs/contribute/mini-prds/skill-acceptance-tests.md)
(tracked as [DE-051](https://github.com/LegalQuants/lq-ai/blob/main/docs/PRD.md#de-051--acceptance-testing-for-the-m1-skill-set-against-real-documents) / [DE-236](https://github.com/LegalQuants/lq-ai/blob/main/docs/PRD.md#de-236--acceptance-tests-for-the-10-starter-skills)).
No `acceptance/` directory exists for this skill yet.

## Who I am
Senior Privacy Counsel at a global technology company; licensed in Arizona. My practice
often requires distilling legal text into concise, plain, and practical language for many different
audiences daily - the core of what this skill does. I'll provide the practicing-attorney
attestation ([skills/CONTRIBUTING.md §3](https://github.com/LegalQuants/lq-ai/blob/main/skills/CONTRIBUTING.md))
at PR time and sign off commits per the DCO.

## Corpus-sourcing
Per [skills/comms-improver/test-plan.md](https://github.com/LegalQuants/lq-ai/blob/main/skills/comms-improver/test-plan.md),
I'll assemble 6–10 sanitized samples spanning the required categories - contract clauses, legal
memos / analysis, regulatory / compliance passages, an authority-preservation sample, and a
technical-term sample - drawn from my own practice, EDGAR, court proceedings, and regulator
inquiries. Public-record sources need no redaction; own-practice samples get a first pass with
OpenAI Privacy Filter (open-weight, on-device PII detector) plus a manual review for missed spans
and matter-specific terms.

## Run method
Assemble the skill and run each input across two models - **Claude Sonnet 4.6** (extended thinking)
and **Gemini 3.5 Flash** (thinking enabled). The exact resolved model IDs are recorded in
`results.md` at run time.

## Deliverable
A new `skills/comms-improver/acceptance/` directory: `inputs/`, `expected/` (structural
expectations), `outputs/` (per input × model), and `results.md` (structural pass/fail grid +
calibration notes).

entries.title: #222: [Feature] DE-296 — Tabular wizard: Project + free-pick document sources
entries.text:
[Feature] DE-296 — Tabular wizard: Project + free-pick document sources ### Prerequisites

- [x] I have searched existing issues and the PRD §9 deferred-enhancements list.

### Use case

I'd like to take [DE-296](https://github.com/LegalQuants/lq-ai/blob/main/docs/PRD.md#de-296--tabular-review-document-source-surface-project-scoped--free-pick-deferred-from-m3-c3): extend tabular wizard Step 1 (`/lq-ai/tabular/new`) beyond KB-only to Project-scoped and free-pick file selection.


### Why this can't be met today

Tracked in DE-296 / [ROADMAP §2.4](https://github.com/LegalQuants/lq-ai/blob/main/docs/ROADMAP.md): wizard Step 1 is KB-only; `GET /api/v1/files` is not implemented (only POST/GET-by-id/DELETE). `MatterRailFiles.svelte` already calls a non-existent list route.

### Proposed approach

**Scope:** Wizard Step 1 only — how `document_ids` are collected. `POST /tabular/preview-cost` and `POST /tabular/execute` unchanged.

### Backend — `GET /api/v1/files` 
- **Pagination:** `cursor` + `limit` → `{ items, next_cursor }` envelope, modeled on `list_chats` (`api/app/api/chats.py`) / `ChatListResponse`. No offset/page-number pagination.
- **Filters:** `search` (filename); optional `project_id` (join `project_files`) for Project-scoped picker.
- **Auth:** caller-owned files; admins see all; soft-deleted excluded.
- **Correctness:** each row to include `document_id` via LEFT JOIN `documents` (same pattern as `get_file` and `list_kb_files`). Wizard disable-state and final `document_ids` depend on this — without it every row looks “Not yet parsed.”
- **OpenAPI / tests:** add `get` under existing `/api/v1/files` in `docs/api/backend-openapi.yaml`; add `("GET", "/api/v1/files")` to `IMPLEMENTED_ROUTES`. No `EXPECTED_PATHS` count change (paths, not operations).
- **In scope:** wire `MatterRailFiles.svelte` to new `listFiles()` — fixes existing broken `GET /files?owner_id=me` raw `apiRequest` call. Drop `owner_id` query param; scope is implicit from JWT.
**Fork for maintainer:** Project tab could use N× `getFile(attached_file_ids)` (PRD literal) **or** `listFiles({ project_id })` (preferred — avoids N+1, one list surface for Project + free-pick + MatterRail). Happy to follow your call.

### Frontend — wizard Step 1
- Source tabs: **Knowledge base** (unchanged: `listKnowledgeBaseFiles`) | **Project** | **Files**.
- Project / Files tabs: `listFiles({ project_id?, search?, cursor?, limit? })` in `web/src/lib/lq-ai/api/files.ts` + unit tests.
- Unified selection keyed by `document_id`; dedupe across tabs; enforce `TABULAR_MAX_DOCS` (200) in wizard (server already enforces).
- Disable rows without `document_id` (“Not yet parsed”) in all pickers.
**Touch:** `web/src/routes/lq-ai/tabular/new/+page.svelte`, `page-helpers.ts`; patterns in `PlaybookExecuteModal.svelte`, `list_kb_files` / `MatterRailFiles.svelte`.

### Tests & docs
- Backend integration tests for list endpoint (cursor, search, `project_id`, authz, `document_id` populated).
- Extend `page-helpers` tests + `web/cypress/e2e/m3-c-tabular-review.cy.ts`.
- Update `docs/tabular-review.md` when shipped.
**Delivery:** One PR or two (backend + MatterRail fix first, then wizard tabs) — happy to follow maintainer preference.

### Acceptance criteria (DE-296)
- [ ] KB / Project / Files combinable in one run
- [ ] `TABULAR_MAX_DOCS` enforced in wizard + server
- [ ] Unparsed files disabled in all pickers (requires `document_id` on every list row)


### Related DE-### entry (if any)

DE-296

### Affected subsystem

Web (chat UI), Backend (API)

### Alternatives considered

_n/a — implementing per DE-296._


### Additional context

_No response_

entries.title: #212: Default chat tool-call cap (8) is low for multi-call-per-case research skills (#207 finding 1)
entries.text:
Default chat tool-call cap (8) is low for multi-call-per-case research skills (#207 finding 1) Split out from #207 (finding 1).

### Summary
`settings.chat_tool_call_cap` defaults to **8**. Skills that make several tool calls *per item* — e.g. `case-law-research` does `search_case_law` → `get_cluster` → `read_opinion`/`find_in_case` for each case — exhaust the cap after only a handful of cases, forcing the loop into its final no-tools synthesis round earlier than the user expects.

### Context
- The empty-answer-at-cap bug is already fixed (#208) — hitting the cap now produces a synthesized answer rather than a blank one. This issue is about the cap *value/tunability*, not correctness.
- The operator override now works correctly via `LQ_AI_CHAT_TOOL_CALL_CAP` (or the bare `CHAT_TOOL_CALL_CAP`) after #210 fixed the missing env alias.

### Options to weigh
1. **Raise the global default** (e.g. 8 → 16/24) — simplest, but increases worst-case per-turn tool spend/latency for all skills.
2. **Per-skill cap override** — let a skill's frontmatter declare a higher cap for research-heavy skills; the loop uses `max(default, skill_cap)` bounded by a hard ceiling.
3. **Document per-deployment tuning** — keep the default, document `LQ_AI_CHAT_TOOL_CALL_CAP` prominently for operators running research-heavy skills.

A combination (sensible default bump + documented override, with per-skill as a later enhancement) is likely the right call. Tracked as **DE-357** in PRD §9.

Refs #207

entries.title: #207: Chat tool-loop persists an empty answer when a research turn exhausts the tool-call cap
entries.text:
Chat tool-loop persists an empty answer when a research turn exhausts the tool-call cap ### Prerequisites
- [x] I have searched existing issues for this bug.
- [x] This is not a security vulnerability.

### What happened?
A chat turn using the `case-law-research` skill runs its CourtListener tool calls, collects sources, then persists a **zero-length assistant message** — the turn appears to "just stop" with no answer.

Confirmed in the data on a reproduction run: **25 tool calls executed, 121 sources collected**, but the final model round returned `tokens_out=9` with **`length(content)=0`**, and the row was **not** `refused` and **not** truncated.

### Expected behavior
When the loop reaches `chat_tool_call_cap` and issues its final no-tools round, the model should **synthesize a final answer from the tool results already gathered** — not return empty.

### Reproduction steps
1. Enable the CourtListener tool provider (reproduces fastest at the default `chat_tool_call_cap = 8`).
2. New chat; ask a broad case-law question that needs more tool calls than the cap, e.g. *"Find federal cases on the reliability of AI-generated expert testimony under Rule 702."*
3. Let it run **without** re-prompting. Observe a blank assistant message.

### LQ.AI version
v0.5.x (`main` @ `1548b9b`, migration head 0055)

### Deployment mode
Mode 1 — self-hosted with cloud LLM keys

### LLM provider (if applicable)
Anthropic, `claude-opus-4-7`

### Environment
Docker Compose on Windows 11 (Docker Desktop)

### Relevant logs / data
- `inference_routing_log` final round: `tokens_in=37646, tokens_out=9`, `refused=f`
- `tool_egress_log`: 25 `courtlistener-prod` calls (search_case_law / get_cluster / find_in_case / read_opinion)
- `message_tool_sources`: 121 rows
- `messages`: assistant `length(content)=0`

### Root cause
`run_chat_tool_loop` (`api/app/chat/tool_loop.py`) fires the final round with `tools=None, tool_choice="none"` but **re-sends the message list verbatim**, with no instruction to stop researching and write. The model — cut off mid-tool-use and forbidden from calling tools — emits ~nothing. A fix (append an explicit synthesis directive to a copy of the messages before the no-tools round) is in the linked PR; verified live (cap-hit turn went from 0 chars to an 8,305-char report).

### Additional context — related, lower-priority findings
1. **Default cap (8) is low** for multi-call-per-case skills like `case-law-research` (`search_case_law` → `get_cluster` → `read_opinion`/`find_in_case` per case). Worth reconsidering the default or documenting per-skill tuning.
2. **Wrong env-var name in docs:** `config.py` says the cap is overridable via `LQ_AI_CHAT_TOOL_CALL_CAP`, but the `Settings` class has no `env_prefix`, so the real var is `CHAT_TOOL_CALL_CAP`. (Fix the docstring, or add `validation_alias=AliasChoices("LQ_AI_CHAT_TOOL_CALL_CAP")`.)
3. **`tool_call_log.chat_id` is NULL:** `tool_loop.execute_tool` is called without `chat_id=`, so chat-scoped audit queries can't find the rows.
4. **Skill drops on follow-up turns:** `applied_skills` is populated on message 1 but empty on subsequent turns in the same chat.

Happy to split (2)–(4) into separate issues if preferred.

entries.title: #184: PR5b: governed chat tool-loop + confirmation gate (WS4)
entries.text:
PR5b: governed chat tool-loop + confirmation gate (WS4) ## Summary

Implements **PR5b** of WS4 (legal-research + MCP): the **governed chat tool-loop + human confirmation gate**, building on the merged PR5a substrate (#181) per the merged design spec `docs/superpowers/specs/2026-06-18-pr5-governed-chat-tool-loop-design.md`.

Picks up the work declared "next" in the #182 handoff. Claiming here so it doesn't collide with that line.

## Scope (backend-only, per locked decision L2)

Shipping as **two security-gated PRs**:

- **PR5b-i — gateway `tools`/`tool_choice` passthrough + Anthropic `tool_use` bridge** (`gateway/**`). The spec assumed the adapters already map tool calls; the Anthropic adapter actually defers `tool_use` ↔ `tool_calls` translation in both directions, so that bridge is built here. Spec's recommended first task (§125).
- **PR5b-ii — chat tool-loop executor + persist-and-resume confirmation gate** (`api/app/chat/tool_loop.py`, integrated into `chats.py`; new `POST /api/v1/chats/{chat_id}/tool-calls/{pending_call_id}` + migration 0054). Reuses `governed_tool_invocation` (origin="chat") unchanged.

## Honors

- Closed-set posture (operator allowlist; gateway tier-enforced), L3 persist-and-resume, L4 per-turn cap 8, L6 shared governance helper.
- No raw payloads in `tool_call_log` (args digested; pending args Fernet-encrypted at rest).

All `web/` UI is out of scope (PR6/WS5). Both PRs will `Closes #<this>`.

entries.title: #183: PR5b: governed chat tool-loop + confirmation gate (WS4)
entries.text:
PR5b: governed chat tool-loop + confirmation gate (WS4) ## Summary

Implements **PR5b** of WS4 (legal-research + MCP): the **governed chat tool-loop + human confirmation gate**, building on the merged PR5a substrate (#181) per the merged design spec `docs/superpowers/specs/2026-06-18-pr5-governed-chat-tool-loop-design.md`.

Picks up the work declared "next" in the #182 handoff. Claiming here so it doesn't collide with that line.

## Scope (backend-only, per locked decision L2)

Shipping as **two security-gated PRs**:

- **PR5b-i — gateway `tools`/`tool_choice` passthrough + Anthropic `tool_use` bridge** (`gateway/**`). The spec assumed the adapters already map tool calls; the Anthropic adapter actually defers `tool_use` ↔ `tool_calls` translation in both directions, so that bridge is built here. Spec's recommended first task (§125).
- **PR5b-ii — chat tool-loop executor + persist-and-resume confirmation gate** (`api/app/chat/tool_loop.py`, integrated into `chats.py`; new `POST /api/v1/chats/{chat_id}/tool-calls/{pending_call_id}` + migration 0054). Reuses `governed_tool_invocation` (origin="chat") unchanged.

## Honors

- Closed-set posture (operator allowlist; gateway tier-enforced), L3 persist-and-resume, L4 per-turn cap 8, L6 shared governance helper.
- No raw payloads in `tool_call_log` (args digested; pending args Fernet-encrypted at rest).

All `web/` UI is out of scope (PR6/WS5). Both PRs will `Closes #<this>`.

entries.title: #174: GDPR as a statutory graph + MCP, starting with Spain
entries.text:
GDPR as a statutory graph + MCP, starting with Spain ### Prerequisites

- [x] I have searched existing issues and the PRD §9 deferred-enhancements list.

### Use case

GDPR work needs answers you can trace back to a specific article and a specific version of the law — the way PrivacyQuant already does for US state privacy. Anyone building GDPR skills on LQ.AI (EU/Spain practitioners first) benefits from a citation-grounded backbone to verify against.

### Why this can't be met today

PrivacyQuant explicitly lists GDPR under scope exclusions, so there's no EU equivalent. The GDPR work already in flight in lq-skills runs against general patterns rather than versioned statutory text — useful, but nothing underneath it is pinned to an article + official source + version.

### Proposed approach

 Same shape as PrivacyQuant, but for GDPR, starting with Spain (GDPR + the Spanish specifics in LOPDGDD, plus AEPD sanctions as the precedent source).

  - YAML obligation nodes for the articles in scope, each pinned to an official source (EUR-Lex / BOE) and a git hash, with exceptions, applicability and links between them.
  - A small MCP server with deterministic tools over the graph (fetch a requirement, check applicability, find an AEPD precedent, resolve GDPR-vs-national conflicts).
  - Wired the DE-264 way: community skills first, first-party MCP later.

I'm not a lawyer myself — I come at this from the legaltech side — but I have a privacy specialist lined up to review and attest the legal content before any PR, per skills/CONTRIBUTING.md. A good chunk of the Spanish-corpus plumbing is already done.

### Related DE-### entry (if any)

 DE-264, DE-002

### Affected subsystem

Skills

### Alternatives considered

Mainly looking for a steer on where you'd want this to live before I build at scale: a PrivacyQuant-style sibling repo, an `eu/` tree inside PrivacyQuant, or purely as lq-skills skills. And what namespace makes sense, since `pq-*` reads as US-only.

### Additional context

Happy to share a schema and a few sample nodes to make it concrete.

entries.title: #173: [Feature] DE-279: name-match verification to catch fabricated case citations
entries.text:
[Feature] DE-279: name-match verification to catch fabricated case citations ### Prerequisites

- [x] I have searched existing issues and the PRD §9 deferred-enhancements list.

### Use case

DE-279 already commits to this capability — catching the "model fabricated a case" failure mode, citing the *Mata v. Avianca* sanctions. This issue is an **offer to implement DE-279** (an api-side first slice), plus one finding worth recording: the gap DE-279 anticipated is now **live in shipped code**.

In practice the failure is rarely an invented reporter slot (those simply fail to resolve) — it's a **real reporter slot under a wrong or invented case name**, e.g. a model emits `Smith v. Jones, 576 U.S. 644 (2015)` when `576 U.S. 644` is actually *Obergefell v. Hodges*. That should be caught; today it passes as "resolved" — leaving anyone using lq-ai for litigation or legal-research work exposed to a fabricated cite slipping into a filing.

### Why this can't be met today

DE-279 was written before the CourtListener lookup tool shipped (PRs [#159](https://github.com/LegalQuants/lq-ai/pull/159)–[#161](https://github.com/LegalQuants/lq-ai/pull/161)).

Now that it has, the anticipated gap is concrete: the tool **resolves** reporter citations but never checks the cited **name**. Confirmed in current `main` — `verify_citations` ([`gateway/app/providers/tool/courtlistener.py`](https://github.com/LegalQuants/lq-ai/blob/main/gateway/app/providers/tool/courtlistener.py)) and [`api/app/research/service.verify_citations`](https://github.com/LegalQuants/lq-ai/blob/main/api/app/research/service.py) return the resolved cluster's `case_name`, but nothing compares it to the asserted name:

| Asserted in text | `/citation-lookup/` resolves | Today | Should be |
|---|---|---|---|
| `Smith v. Jones, 576 U.S. 644` | *Obergefell v. Hodges* | returned as resolved | flagged: resolves to a different case |
| `Fink v. Gomez, 239 F.3d 989` | *David M. Fink v. James H. Gomez…* | no check | verified (abbreviated, same case) |

### Proposed approach

DE-279 already scopes the module (`api/app/citation/case_resolver.py`). It sits **api-side, layered over the existing gateway-brokered `verify_citations`** — no new CourtListener egress (ADR 0014 preserved).

**What `verify_citations` already does:** sends the text to CourtListener, which extracts each citation and resolves it, returning the matched cluster's *canonical* case name (`case_name`), id, and url. Citation detection and resolution are done — this proposal does not touch them.

**What it does *not* do — and all this adds:** it never compares the resolved name to the name the author *asserted*. CL reports "`576 U.S. 644` is *Obergefell v. Hodges*"; it doesn't notice if the author called it "Smith v. Jones." `case_resolver` adds that one missing comparison:

1. **Read the asserted name** — for each citation, pull the `Name v. Name` the author wrote next to it out of the source text (a small local read; the lookup returns the citations but not the surrounding names).
2. **Compare** it to the resolved `case_name` with a lenient surname-containment matcher. Lenient *by design*: the citation is already confirmed real, so the check only rejects clear fabrications while tolerating that briefs abbreviate — `Fink v. Gomez` still matches *David M. Fink v. James H. Gomez, Director…*.
3. **Verdict** — emit `verified` / `wrong_case` / `unresolved` / `unverifiable` (the last when the resolver is unavailable — it degrades, never blocks).

Surface: `POST /api/v1/research/validate-citations` (stateless), one verdict per citation. The added code is a stdlib-only name matcher (`re` + `difflib`) — **no new dependency**. I've scoped it against the `case_resolver.py` shape and current `main` and can move quickly, but I'd rather align with you on the open questions below before locking an approach.

*Provenance: the goal and module name are DE-279's; what I'm adding is the name comparison itself, the live-code finding above, and the design reasoning below (DE-279 weighs no alternatives of its own).*

### Related DE-### entry (if any)

DE-279 — [PRD §9](https://github.com/LegalQuants/lq-ai/blob/main/docs/PRD.md#de-279--case-citation-validation-bluebook-resolution-via-courtlistener)

### Affected subsystem

Document Pipeline / Citation Engine

### Alternatives considered

- **Point lq-ai's (forthcoming) MCP client at the CourtListener MCP server, whose `analyze_citations` already name-checks** (it warns when the asserted name differs from the cluster's canonical name). Rejected for the citation *engine*: that match would run server-side inside a third-party MCP — a black box, counter to lq-ai's principle that verification logic is open and inspectable — and it depends on the unbuilt MCP client plus an external server each operator must allowlist and trust. Its existence does confirm the feature is worth having; the question is only whether lq-ai's own open, in-boundary engine does it natively (this proposal) or outsources it opaquely.
- **Port `Legal-Week-Cite-Checker` directly, as DE-279 suggests.** LWCC is a sound reference for the *approach* (extract → resolve → fall back to a name search), and is what DE-279 points at. But it's a Swift / iOS app, whereas DE-279's target is a Python module (`api/app/citation/case_resolver.py`) — so it isn't a line-for-line port; any implementation is a Python rewrite regardless. `citation-verifier` — an independent tool, not derived from LWCC — is already Python, covers the same flow with tuned name-matching and a regression corpus, so it's the more direct source for the logic, while LWCC remains a useful design reference.

### Additional context

**Deliberately scoped as a first slice.** Out of scope for this PR: the chat-pipeline auto-run + `message_case_citations` persistence + web chip/Cypress E2E that complete DE-279's own acceptance criteria (a natural second PR). Also beyond DE-279, as possible later enhancements (none tracked yet): being more thorough when a citation doesn't resolve by its exact reporter number — first looking the case up by name, court, and date in CourtListener's opinion database, and, failing that, in the free federal court-docket archive (RECAP). I'd open follow-ups as you prefer.

**Prior art / what I'd port.** I build and maintain [`rlfordon/citation-verifier`](https://github.com/rlfordon/citation-verifier) — a Python tool that checks legal citations against CourtListener to catch AI-hallucinated and misattributed case citations. (It also checks quoted passages and whether a case supports the proposition it's cited for — surfaces on lq-ai's own roadmap, e.g. DE-280 — but I'm scoping *this* PR to the DE-279 name-check.) It runs against real briefs with a regression corpus of known-real and known-fabricated citations, so the logic this PR ports is already exercised against real and adversarial inputs.

A bit more on who's offering: I'm a law librarian and former bankruptcy attorney with a lot of CourtListener-API mileage across side projects. I also serve on the Free Law Project board — FLP runs CourtListener — but to be clear, this is a personal project, not an FLP initiative. As a LegalQuants org member (read-only on this repo) I'm happy to implement the api-only first slice and iterate in review.

**Open questions (happy to shape before opening a PR):**
1. **Start standalone, or go straight to chat integration?** This proposal builds the check as a standalone endpoint (`POST /research/validate-citations`) that's called on demand — the smallest first step. DE-279's end state is bigger: the check runs automatically on every AI chat response and shows inline. Is it OK to land the standalone engine first and wire it into the chat flow as a follow-up, or would you rather it go into chat from the start?
2. Does a deterministic name-match layer fit the thin-tool model, or would you prefer it shaped differently?
3. Verdict vocabulary — reuse the existing Citation Engine's chip states (the green / yellow / grey inline badges it already shows for document-quote citations), or a distinct set for case citations?

**References:** legal-research mini-PRD ([`docs/proposals/legal-research-and-mcp.md`](https://github.com/LegalQuants/lq-ai/blob/main/docs/proposals/legal-research-and-mcp.md)), [ADR 0014](https://github.com/LegalQuants/lq-ai/blob/main/docs/adr/0014-gateway-egress-boundary-for-tool-providers.md), [`docs/citation-engine.md` §Scope](https://github.com/LegalQuants/lq-ai/blob/main/docs/citation-engine.md), CourtListener tool PRs [#159](https://github.com/LegalQuants/lq-ai/pull/159)–[#161](https://github.com/LegalQuants/lq-ai/pull/161), my implementation: [`rlfordon/citation-verifier`](https://github.com/rlfordon/citation-verifier).

entries.title: #99: [Bug] docker compose --profile local up fails — legalquants/paddleocr-vl image is not pullable from any public registry
entries.text:
[Bug] docker compose --profile local up fails — legalquants/paddleocr-vl image is not pullable from any public registry ### Prerequisites

- [x] I have searched existing issues for this bug.
- [x] This is not a security vulnerability (those go through the disclosure process in SECURITY.md).

### What happened?

Following the README's air-gapped / local-inference instructions fails because one of the two sidecar images the `local` profile requires cannot be pulled.

Running the documented command:

```
docker compose --profile local up -d
```

produces:

```
✘ Image legalquants/paddleocr-vl:latest  Error  pull access denied for legalquants/paddleocr-vl, repository does not exist or may require 'docker login'
! Image ollama/ollama:latest             Interrupted
Error response from daemon: pull access denied for legalquants/paddleocr-vl, repository does not exist or may require 'docker login'
```

The `ollama/ollama:latest` image is public and pulls fine, but `legalquants/paddleocr-vl:latest` is not available on Docker Hub (or any registry Docker reaches by default). Because that image fails, Compose interrupts the whole `local` profile startup — including the Ollama sidecar, which is the component actually needed for local (Tier 1) inference.

The README's "Providers and air-gapped deployments" section presents `docker compose --profile local up -d` as a complete, self-contained step with no `docker login` or registry-authentication prerequisite mentioned.

### Expected behavior

Per the README, `docker compose --profile local up -d` should add the Ollama and PaddleOCR sidecars and bring up a fully local, air-gap-capable deployment. Either the `legalquants/paddleocr-vl` image should be publicly pullable, or the README should document the registry/authentication step (or build instructions) required to obtain it.

### Reproduction steps

1. Complete the standard Quick Start (clone, configure .env, docker compose up -d) — the seven core services come up healthy.
2. Run the documented local-inference command: docker compose --profile local up -d
3. Observe the pull failure for legalquants/paddleocr-vl:latest, which also interrupts the ollama image pull.


### LQ.AI version

v0.3.0 — commit 8b8e5496e7464d3e15fb8890ad9ebdde4257e724

### Deployment mode

Mode 2 (local Ollama / vLLM, air-gap-capable)

### LLM provider (if applicable)

Ollama (local) — though the failure occurs before any inference, at image-pull time.

### Environment

- OS: macOS Tahoe 26.4
- Docker version 29.4.3, build 055a478
- Docker Compose version v5.1.4
- Failure occurs at docker compose image-pull time, before containers start

### Relevant logs

```shell
✘ Image legalquants/paddleocr-vl:latest  Error  pull access denied for legalquants/paddleocr-vl, repository does not exist or may require 'docker login'
! Image ollama/ollama:latest             Interrupted
Error response from daemon: pull access denied for legalquants/paddleocr-vl, repository does not exist or may require 'docker login'
```

### Additional context

Confirmed not a registry-auth issue: `docker pull legalquants/paddleocr-vl:latest` fails with the same error even while authenticated via `docker login`, and https://hub.docker.com/r/legalquants/paddleocr-vl returns 404. The image is genuinely unpublished.

PaddleOCR is the scanned-PDF OCR fallback in the ingestion pipeline; it is not required for local inference itself. As a workaround, starting only the Ollama sidecar by name —

  docker compose up -d ollama

— brings up local inference successfully and sidesteps the missing image. But the documented `--profile local` path is broken for any operator following the README as written.

Suggested resolution (one of):
- Publish the legalquants/paddleocr-vl image to a public registry (Docker Hub or GHCR); or
- Add a build context for it in docker-compose.yml so it builds locally like the other first-party services (api, gateway, web); or
- Document the registry-authentication or build step the local profile requires, in the README's air-gapped deployment section.

entries.title: #92: [Bug] `docker compose up` fails on a default install — profile-gated bridge services use `${VAR:?}` required-variable checks
entries.text:
[Bug] `docker compose up` fails on a default install — profile-gated bridge services use `${VAR:?}` required-variable checks ### Prerequisites

- [x] I have searched existing issues for this bug.
- [x] This is not a security vulnerability (those go through the disclosure process in SECURITY.md).

### What happened?

A fresh install following the README Quick Start fails at `docker compose up`.

The `slack-bridge` and `teams-bridge` services are correctly gated behind Compose profiles (`profiles: ["slack"]` / `["teams"]`), but their `environment:` blocks use the `${VAR:?error}` required-variable form, e.g.:

```yaml
SLACK_CLIENT_ID: ${SLACK_CLIENT_ID:?SLACK_CLIENT_ID is required when slack profile is enabled}
```

Compose interpolates the entire compose file *before* applying profile filtering, so these `:?` checks fire even when the `slack`/`teams` profiles are inactive. A default `docker compose up -d` therefore aborts:

```
error while interpolating services.slack-bridge.environment.SLACK_CLIENT_ID: required variable SLACK_CLIENT_ID is missing a value
```

Resolving each variable just surfaces the next one in turn.

`grep -oE '\$\{[A-Z_]+:\?' docker-compose.yml | sort -u` returns 12 variables using the `:?` form. Four are legitimate core secrets (`POSTGRES_PASSWORD`, `MINIO_ROOT_PASSWORD`, `LQ_AI_GATEWAY_KEY`, `JWT_SECRET`). The other eight belong to the opt-in bridge services and should not block a default install: `SLACK_CLIENT_ID`, `SLACK_CLIENT_SECRET`, `SLACK_SIGNING_SECRET`, `LQ_AI_BRIDGE_TOKEN`, `LQ_AI_BRIDGE_PUBLIC_URL`, `MICROSOFT_APP_ID`, `MICROSOFT_APP_PASSWORD`, `LQ_AI_TEAMS_BRIDGE_PUBLIC_URL`.


### Expected behavior

The `.env.example` header states the four core variables are what "must be set before `docker compose up` will succeed," and the Slack/Teams sections say their variables can be left blank when those integrations are unused.

Following that, a plain `docker compose up -d` with only the four core variables set should bring up the seven core services without touching the opt-in bridges.

### Reproduction steps

1. `git clone --recurse-submodules https://github.com/LegalQuants/lq-ai.git`
2. `cd lq-ai && cp .env.example .env`
3. Set only the four documented required variables in `.env`: POSTGRES_PASSWORD, MINIO_ROOT_PASSWORD, LQ_AI_GATEWAY_KEY, JWT_SECRET. Leave the Slack/Teams variables blank as `.env.example` instructs.
4. Run `docker compose up -d` with no `--profile` flag.
5. Observe the interpolation error for SLACK_CLIENT_ID.

### LQ.AI version

v0.2.0 116012bfae9782cacc701c55ef1fec398985d23f

### Deployment mode

Mode 1 (cloud LLM keys)

### LLM provider (if applicable)

_No response_

### Environment

- OS: macOS Tahoe 26.4
- Docker version 29.4.3, build 055a478
- Docker Compose version v5.1.4
- Bug occurs at `docker compose` interpolation time, before any container starts


### Relevant logs

```shell
There are no container logs — the failure happens during compose-file interpolation, before any service starts. The complete error output:

error while interpolating services.slack-bridge.environment.SLACK_CLIENT_ID: required variable SLACK_CLIENT_ID is missing a value: SLACK_CLIENT_ID is required when slack profile is enabled
```

### Additional context

Suggested fix: change the eight bridge variables from `${VAR:?...}` to the default form `${VAR:-}` in `docker-compose.yml`, and move the presence check into each bridge container's own entrypoint/startup script — which only runs when the profile is active. This preserves the "required when the profile is enabled" guarantee without breaking the default install path.

Alternatives: ship the eight bridge variables with placeholder values in `.env.example`, or document that they need non-empty placeholders even when unused. The `:-` change is cleaner since it requires no action from operators who don't use the bridges.

Workaround for affected users until fixed: set the eight bridge variables to any non-empty placeholder value in `.env`. The bridge services stay off because their profiles remain inactive.

entries.title: #281: docs: document native frontend dev flow with Vite HMR
entries.text:
docs: document native frontend dev flow with Vite HMR ## What this PR does

Adds documentation to enable frontend dev with Vite HMR.

## Why

Per documentation, dev environment hard rules, the web container serves a pre-built static bundle (no HMR) — rebuild web before debugging a UI change that isn't appearing. Add documentation to enable HMR during dev without code changes. 


## What this PR does *not* do

<!-- Optional but encouraged for non-trivial PRs. Naming the explicit
non-goals prevents reviewer scope creep and makes follow-up PRs cleaner. -->

## Type of change

<!-- Check all that apply -->

- [ ] Bug fix (non-breaking change that fixes an issue)
- [ ] New feature (non-breaking change that adds capability)
- [ ] Breaking change (fix or feature that would change existing behavior)
- [x] Documentation update
- [ ] Skill contribution (see attestation below)
- [ ] Refactor (no behavioral change)
- [ ] Test / CI / tooling

## Testing

<!-- How did you verify the change? -->

- [ ] Unit tests added/updated
- [ ] Integration tests added/updated (if applicable)
- [ ] Manually tested against a real deployment (describe scenario below)
- [ ] Acceptance test plan updated (if changing skill behavior)

<details>
<summary>Manual testing notes (if applicable)</summary>

<!-- What you ran, against what configuration, and what you observed. -->

</details>

## Documentation

- [x] PRD updated (if user-facing behavior changes)
- [ ] README updated (if quickstart or capability list changes)
- [ ] Skill-authoring guide updated (if skill conventions change)
- [ ] Deployment cookbook updated (if deployment changes)
- [ ] Compliance documentation updated (if compliance posture changes)
- [ ] OpenAPI schema regenerated (if API endpoints change)

## Transparency & governance invariants

<!-- The posture that *is* the product (ADR 0016). Check each box or mark it
n/a. If you can't, stop and either fix the change or surface the decision to a
maintainer. The cheap-to-check ones are enforced by
api/tests/test_transparency_invariants.py — but the judgment ones are on you. -->

- [ ] **Egress (P1):** any outbound third-party call goes through the gateway only — no direct egress from `api/`.
- [ ] **Closed set (P2):** any new model/autonomous-invokable capability is a bounded, operator-controlled allowlist entry, not an open hook.
- [ ] **No raw payloads (P3):** no row or log line I add carries raw content (message bodies, tool args/results, fetched text, keys) — counts, types, ids, digests, outcomes only.
- [ ] **Fail restrictive (P4):** missing/broken config fails closed, and I tested that path, not just the happy path.
- [ ] **Atomic audit (P5):** state changes get an audit row committed in the same transaction (helpers flush, the handler commits).
- [ ] **One governance path (P6):** I reused the shared governance/audit helpers rather than re-deriving tier/cost/audit logic.
- [ ] **Human gate (P7):** anything destructive/irreversible is behind the confirmation gate and excluded from unattended/autonomous execution.
- [ ] **Operator control (P8):** any new capability has an operator enable/disable and appears on an admin surface.
- [ ] **User owns data (P9):** outbound matter context is anonymized; the change respects export/delete and data-residency guarantees.
- [ ] **Contract is truth (P10):** OpenAPI sketch / DB schema doc / relevant ADR updated in this PR; any architectural/authz decision is recorded or surfaced, not made silently.

## DCO sign-off

- [ ] All commits in this PR are signed off per the [DCO](../CONTRIBUTING.md#sign-off-developer-certificate-of-origin)

<!-- Verify with: git log --show-signature -->

## Related issues

<!-- Closes #123, Refs DE-013, etc. -->

---

<!-- ============================================================
SKILL CONTRIBUTION ATTESTATION (uncomment if this PR contributes
or substantively modifies a skill containing legal substance)

## Skill attestation

I have reviewed the substantive legal content of this skill and
certify that, to the best of my knowledge as a [practicing
attorney / legal professional / specific role], the patterns,
severity calibrations, recommended language, and reference
material reflect accurate and reasonable legal practice in
[jurisdiction(s)]. I understand that this skill will be used in
real practice and that errors could affect real legal work; I
have authored this skill with the same care I would apply to my
own client work.

Signed: [your name and any relevant qualifications]

If you are not a practicing attorney, follow the alternative
attestation paths in skills/CONTRIBUTING.md (pair with a
practicing-attorney co-author, or have a practicing attorney
review the skill before submission).
============================================================ -->

## Reviewer notes

<!-- Anything the reviewer should know — areas where you'd specifically
appreciate scrutiny, design decisions you considered alternatives for,
known limitations of this PR. -->

entries.title: #280: fix: stop deriving dark mode from OS color-scheme preference
entries.text:
fix: stop deriving dark mode from OS color-scheme preference Dark mode isn't a supported theme in this deployment, but the 'system' theme option (default for every new visitor) still resolved to the OS's prefers-color-scheme setting in five places: app.html's boot script, the Settings > General theme dropdown, the Electron theme-update bridge, the toast notification theme, and the flowchart viewer's color mode. Most LQ.AI-authored components have no dark: styling at all, so a visitor with OS dark mode on ended up with a half-light/half-dark UI wherever a component still carried dark: classes inherited from upstream OpenWebUI (e.g. the assistant message bubble). All five now resolve 'system' to light unconditionally; a matching dead CSS media query in app.css is removed.

## What this PR does

Fixes mixing of light mode and dark mode when the system is set to dark mode

## Why

Creates visual rendering errors through out the application that make visibility difficult. 

## What this PR does *not* do

<!-- Optional but encouraged for non-trivial PRs. Naming the explicit
non-goals prevents reviewer scope creep and makes follow-up PRs cleaner. -->

## Type of change

<!-- Check all that apply -->

- [x] Bug fix (non-breaking change that fixes an issue)
- [ ] New feature (non-breaking change that adds capability)
- [ ] Breaking change (fix or feature that would change existing behavior)
- [ ] Documentation update
- [ ] Skill contribution (see attestation below)

## Testing

- [ ] Integration tests added/updated (if applicable)

- [ ] Acceptance test plan updated (if changing skill behavior)

<summary>Manual testing notes (if applicable)</summary>

</details>

## Documentation

- [ ] Skill-authoring guide updated (if skill conventions change)
- [ ] Deployment cookbook updated (if deployment changes)

- [ ] OpenAPI schema regenerated (if API endpoints change)

## Transparency & governance invariants

<!-- The posture that *is* the product (ADR 0016). Check each box or mark it
n/a. If you can't, stop and either fix the change or surface the decision to a
maintainer. The cheap-to-check ones are enforced by
api/tests/test_transparency_invariants.py — but the judgment ones are on you. -->

- [ ] **Egress (P1):** any outbound third-party call goes through the gateway only — no direct egress from `api/`.
- [ ] **Closed set (P2):** any new model/autonomous-invokable capability is a bounded, operator-controlled allowlist entry, not an open hook.
- [ ] **No raw payloads (P3):** no row or log line I add carries raw content (message bodies, tool args/results, fetched text, keys) — counts, types, ids, digests, outcomes only.
- [ ] **Fail restrictive (P4):** missing/broken config fails closed, and I tested that path, not just the happy path.
- [ ] **Atomic audit (P5):** state changes get an audit row committed in the same transaction (helpers flush, the handler commits).
- [ ] **One governance path (P6):** I reused the shared governance/audit helpers rather than re-deriving tier/cost/audit logic.
- [ ] **Human gate (P7):** anything destructive/irreversible is behind the confirmation gate and excluded from unattended/autonomous execution.
- [ ] **Operator control (P8):** any new capability has an operator enable/disable and appears on an admin surface.

entries.title: #279: docs: document native frontend dev flow with Vite HMR
entries.text:
docs: document native frontend dev flow with Vite HMR ## What this PR does

docs: document native frontend dev flow with Vite HMR.  Modifies .env.example to show how to enable HMR on /web 

```enab

## Why

The web container only builds a static SvelteKit bundle with no source bind-mount, so iterating on web/src required a full image rebuild per change. Vite's dev server already works, but nothing documented the two extra pieces it needs: the LQ.AI api's CORS origins must include Vite's port, and the dockerized web container's embedded OpenWebUI backend (which Vite hardcodes an expectation for at <hostname>:8080) must stay running, republished on that port, instead of the default :3000.

## What this PR does *not* do

No functional changes.

## Type of change

</details>

- [ ] Deployment cookbook updated (if deployment changes)
- [ ] Compliance documentation updated (if compliance posture changes)
- [ ] OpenAPI schema regenerated (if API endpoints change)

## Transparency & governance invariants

<!-- The posture that *is* the product (ADR 0016). Check each box or mark it
n/a. If you can't, stop and either fix the change or surface the decision to a
maintainer. The cheap-to-check ones are enforced by
api/tests/test_transparency_invariants.py — but the judgment ones are on you. -->

- [ ] **Egress (P1):** any outbound third-party call goes through the gateway only — no direct egress from `api/`.
- [ ] **Closed set (P2):** any new model/autonomous-invokable capability is a bounded, operator-controlled allowlist entry, not an open hook.
- [ ] **No raw payloads (P3):** no row or log line I add carries raw content (message bodies, tool args/results, fetched text, keys) — counts, types, ids, digests, outcomes only.
- [ ] **Fail restrictive (P4):** missing/broken config fails closed, and I tested that path, not just the happy path.
- [ ] **Atomic audit (P5):** state changes get an audit row committed in the same transaction (helpers flush, the handler commits).
- [ ] **One governance path (P6):** I reused the shared governance/audit helpers rather than re-deriving tier/cost/audit logic.
- [ ] **Human gate (P7):** anything destructive/irreversible is behind the confirmation gate and excluded from unattended/autonomous execution.
- [ ] **Operator control (P8):** any new capability has an operator enable/disable and appears on an admin surface.
- [ ] **User owns data (P9):** outbound matter context is anonymized; the change respects export/delete and data-residency guarantees.
- [ ] **Contract is truth (P10):** OpenAPI sketch / DB schema doc / relevant ADR updated in this PR; any architectural/authz decision is recorded or surfaced, not made silently.
