entries.title: #278: fix(compose): forward LQ_AI_GATEWAY_MASTER_KEY in the gateway service (Donna #3 follow-up)
entries.text:
fix(compose): forward LQ_AI_GATEWAY_MASTER_KEY in the gateway service (Donna #3 follow-up) ## Ask (from Donna-CC)
Forward `LQ_AI_GATEWAY_MASTER_KEY: ${LQ_AI_GATEWAY_MASTER_KEY:-}` in the root `docker-compose.yml` gateway `environment:` block, beside `LQ_AI_GATEWAY_KEY`, so a plain `docker compose up` enables runtime encrypted-at-rest key storage (in-app provider/research key setting) without a per-consumer compose override.

## Verified premise
- Root `docker-compose.yml` forwarded `LQ_AI_GATEWAY_KEY` + `COURTLISTENER_API_TOKEN` to `gateway` but **not** `LQ_AI_GATEWAY_MASTER_KEY`. So with a source `docker compose up`, `MASTER_KEY_ENV` (`gateway/app/secrets.py` = `LQ_AI_GATEWAY_MASTER_KEY`) was never present → the runtime key store was off → `POST /api/v1/admin/{provider-keys,tool-providers}` with a key returned **400 `failed_precondition`**.
- `docker-compose.release.yml` **already** forwards it (line 117) — this just brings the root file to parity.

## Change
- `docker-compose.yml`: add `LQ_AI_GATEWAY_MASTER_KEY: ${LQ_AI_GATEWAY_MASTER_KEY:-}` in the gateway env (right after `LQ_AI_GATEWAY_KEY`), with a comment covering both the Provider keys and the new Research sources key setters.
- `.env.example`: add a settable, documented `LQ_AI_GATEWAY_MASTER_KEY=` beside `LQ_AI_GATEWAY_KEY` (optional; empty = runtime key storage disabled; mint with Fernet). Otherwise operators forward an empty var and the feature stays off.

## Security note
Forwards a secret env var, exactly mirroring the existing `LQ_AI_GATEWAY_KEY` forward and the release compose. Defaults empty (`:-`), so no behavior change unless an operator sets it. The value is a Fernet master key (ADR 0011) — never logged, never returned.

## Validation
`docker compose -f docker-compose.yml config` interpolates `LQ_AI_GATEWAY_MASTER_KEY` into the gateway service cleanly.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

entries.title: #277: fix(desktop): pull images before 'up' so the launcher picks up releases (v0.6.2)
entries.text:
fix(desktop): pull images before 'up' so the launcher picks up releases (v0.6.2) ## Problem
The desktop launcher runs `docker compose up -d` with **no pull**, and `docker-compose.release.yml` has **no `pull_policy`**. So it reuses whatever `:latest` images are already cached locally and never fetches a new release — you install an update but the UI never changes. Concretely: the v0.6.1 **Research sources** admin tab stays invisible on any stack whose `:latest` was cached before v0.6.1 (all four images were 12 days stale on a live install).

## Fix
`startStack` now runs a **best-effort `docker compose pull` before `up -d`**:
- `runDocker` never throws, so a failed pull (offline / transient registry error) is **non-fatal** — the launch proceeds with cached images (offline still starts the last-known-good stack).
- `release.yml` only moves `:latest` on a version tag, so `:latest` == the latest **release** — pulling it is a safe auto-update, not bleeding-edge.
- `up -d` recreates only the containers whose image actually changed.

No config migration needed — existing users keep their persisted `imageTag: "latest"` and simply start getting refreshes.

## Scope
**Desktop-only release.** Bumps `desktop/package.json` 0.6.1 → **0.6.2**. The api/gateway/web GHCR images are unchanged — the fix ships in the `.dmg`, which then pulls the existing **v0.6.1** images (that already contain the Research sources card). No `vX.Y.Z` GHCR tag needed; release step is `git tag desktop-v0.6.2` → signed `.dmg`.

## Tests
- `compose.ts`: `pullArgs` builder + unit test.
- `orchestrator.ts`: `startStack` pulls before up (injectable runner); tests assert (1) pull-then-up order, (2) a failed pull still brings the stack up and returns the `up` result.
- `npx vitest run` → 63 passed; `tsc --noEmit` clean.

Not security-gated (desktop launcher only).

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

entries.title: #273: feat: runtime tool/authority-provider admin API + Research sources card (Donna #3)
entries.text:
feat: runtime tool/authority-provider admin API + Research sources card (Donna #3) ## Summary

Gives operators a **runtime, in-app path** to enable/disable the four fiduciary-grade authority sources (CourtListener, GovInfo, SEC EDGAR, EUR-Lex) and manage their keys — no `gateway.yaml` hand-edit, no restart — plus the API contract Donna's BFF consumes (upstream request #3). Closes a real gap in LQ.AI's own UI: the headline v0.6.0 authority-sources milestone was previously reachable only by editing YAML + setting an env var + restarting the gateway.

Approach: **mirror the proven inference-BYOK provider-key path one layer down into the gateway's `tool_providers:` block.** The **gateway owns a default registry** (`base_url` / `allowlist` / `egress_tier` / `rate_limit` / `key_required` per type, seeded from `gateway.yaml.example`), so the api can never inject an arbitrary egress target (SSRF-safe, ADR 0014). The api layer is a thin `AdminUser`-gated proxy; secrets are write-only and never returned. Hot-apply reuses the in-place `app.state.tool_adapters` swap the gateway Router already shares by reference.

## ⚠️ Security-gated — review focus

Touches `gateway/**` + secrets + admin authz. Requested reviewer checks (all verified green in a whole-branch review, but please confirm):

- **SSRF / gateway-owned egress:** the api sends only `{type, api_key?, enabled?}` (both request models are `extra="forbid"`); `base_url`/`allowlist` come exclusively from the gateway's `TOOL_PROVIDER_DEFAULTS`. No api path sets an egress target.
- **Secret write-only:** no plaintext key or Fernet ciphertext appears in any response or log — gateway status endpoint, the sanitized `GET /admin/v1/config` (now strips `api_key_encrypted`), the api proxy, or the web card. Status carries `has_key: bool` only (no `last4`).
- **Admin authz:** gateway `/admin/v1/*` is gateway-key-guarded; api `/api/v1/admin/*` is `AdminUser`-gated (403 for non-admin).
- **Audit:** every api write (POST/PATCH/DELETE) writes exactly one `audit_log` row after the gateway call succeeds.
- **Encryption:** reuses ADR 0011 Fernet (`encrypt_value`, `LQ_AI_GATEWAY_MASTER_KEY`) — no new secret-storage mechanism.

## API contract (§5 — the deliverable for Donna)

All `AdminUser`-gated; secrets write-only; every write is audit-logged.

| Verb | Path | Body | Success | Errors |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| GET | `/api/v1/admin/tool-providers` | — | `200` `[{type, enabled, name, has_key, key_required, egress_tier}]` (one per registered type) | 403 |
| POST | `/api/v1/admin/tool-providers` | `{type, api_key?}` | `200` single status row (enable/create from gateway defaults; store key if given; keyless types enable with no key) | 400 no master key, 404 unknown type, 403 |
| PATCH | `/api/v1/admin/tool-providers/{type}` | `{api_key?, enabled?}` | `200` single row (rotate key and/or toggle) | 400, 404, 409 env-key, 403 |
| DELETE | `/api/v1/admin/tool-providers/{type}` | — | `204` (remove entry + retire live adapter) | 404, 409 env-key, 403 |

- `type` ∈ `{courtlistener, govinfo, edgar, eurlex}`. `courtlistener`/`govinfo` are key-bearing; `edgar`/`eurlex` are keyless (User-Agent only).
- **Hot-apply proof:** after `POST {type: courtlistener, api_key: …}`, `GET /api/v1/research/sources` shows that source `enabled=true` with no restart.
- **409** protects operator env-configured entries (`api_key_env`) from runtime removal.

## What's in the branch

- **Gateway:** `TOOL_PROVIDER_DEFAULTS` registry + `upsert_tool_provider`/`remove_tool_provider` config-writer (encrypt-at-rest, atomic write + reload-with-rollback); `/admin/v1/tool-providers` endpoints; hot-apply (rebuild adapter + in-place swap into the Router's shared `app.state.tool_adapters`); `_sanitized_config_payload` now strips `api_key_encrypted`.
- **API:** `GatewayClient` tool-provider methods; `AdminUser`-gated audited `/api/v1/admin/tool-providers` proxy (thin, secrets write-only, `SOURCE_REGISTRY` 404 pre-check, DELETE-204 recipe).
- **Web:** "Research sources" admin card (`/lq-ai/admin/research-sources`) mirroring the Provider keys card — Available/Unavailable badge, masked write-only key input for key-bearing sources, enable/disable, hot re-fetch.
- **Guards/OpenAPI:** collision guards bumped (path count 137→139), `backend-openapi.generated.yaml` regenerated.
- **Deferred enhancements filed:** DE-381 (backfill provider-key audit), DE-382 (tool rate-limiter hot-refresh), DE-383 (`resolve_available_sources` honors `enabled`), DE-384 (mark runtime-managed entries so keyless env entries get the same 409 protection).

## Testing

- Gateway: full suite **770 passed / 3 skipped** at HEAD; new unit + endpoint + hot-apply tests (incl. a zero-boot regression test proving the Router shares the adapter dict).
- API: full suite green at HEAD; endpoint tests cover all verbs, non-admin 403, 400/404/409, secret-never-returned, one audit row per write, DELETE-204 empty body, and the 404 short-circuit-before-gateway.
- Web: `svelte-check` clean.
- OpenAPI drift-guard regenerated + green.

## Notes for the reviewer

Built via spec → plan → subagent-driven-development with a two-stage review per task and a final whole-branch security review. One Critical was caught and fixed mid-build: the gateway Router's `tool_adapters or {}` discarded an empty-but-real dict on a zero-tool-provider boot, so hot-apply never reached the live dispatch path — fixed in `7fc862e6` with an identity-assertion regression test.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

entries.title: #269: docs(DE-365 sub-3): vendor-neutral evidence-linked comparison (completes DE-365)
entries.text:
docs(DE-365 sub-3): vendor-neutral evidence-linked comparison (completes DE-365) ## DE-365 sub-3 — vendor-neutral, evidence-linked comparison (final DE-365 sub-project)

Adds the honest, evidence-linked comparison positioning LQ.AI against the proprietary "fiduciary-grade"-claiming legal-tech **category** (no named vendor). **On merge, all three DE-365 sub-projects are complete** (sub-1 docs honesty audit #260, sub-2 fiduciary playgrounds #262, sub-3 here) — which unblocks the fiduciary-grade release ritual.

### The core thesis
The comparison axis is **verifiability, not capability.** We never claim the proprietary category *lacks* a feature (unprovable strawman). We claim: **ours is inspectable / forkable / demonstrable — here's the artifact; a closed-source product's equivalent claim is not independently verifiable by the user.** That asymmetry is architecturally true, and it *is* the argument. Honest but unflinching, never a dig.

### What's here
- **`docs/comparison.md`** — canonical 14-row matrix; every LQ.AI cell links its proof (ADR + code path + one of the 24 interactive playgrounds). "They *assert* a citation ledger; here's ours — click to run it."
- **`/lq-ai/learn/compare`** — in-app Learn page: the three fundamental truths + a condensed 8-row highlight matrix with playground links + a pointer to the canonical doc; plus a 4th Learn-landing card.
- **Vendor-neutral cleanup** — genericized the DE-365 PRD entry, resolved **DE-377** (README "Streamline" reference), added README comparison links.

### Honesty guardrails (the whole point)
- Every "proprietary category" cell states **only the verifiability/forkability gap** — verified across all 14 doc cells + all 8 page cells (a final-review pass caught + fixed the one data-residency cell that had drifted into a capability claim, and softened the intro's own "every row links ADR+source+playground" promise to "and/or" since rows 12–14 have no playground).
- **Every partial LQ.AI capability carries its caveat inline** — DE-370/371 gate parity, EUR-Lex get-by-CELEX-only (DE-374/375), no matter-intake UI, anonymization recall unmeasured, treatment "not a citator."
- **No named vendor** on any public surface (README / comparison / Learn) — grep-clean.

### Verification
- Built via subagent-driven TDD (3 tasks, each a spec+quality gate) + a final Opus whole-branch honesty review + a consolidated fix wave.
- Every proof link in `comparison.md` resolves on `main` (7 ADRs, 8 code paths, 11 playground slugs — independently verified). Vendor-neutral grep clean. `svelte-check` reports **zero** problems in the new page/landing; prettier + eslint clean. No new API routes.

Spec: `docs/superpowers/specs/2026-07-03-de365-sub3-comparison-design.md` · Plan: `docs/superpowers/plans/2026-07-03-de365-sub3-comparison.md`

Refs DE-365 DE-377

entries.title: #267: feat: reference matter documents in chat
entries.text:
feat: reference matter documents in chat ## What this PR does

  Adds a `referenced_file_ids` chat-send channel so users can reference matter documents in a message and get answers grounded in retrieved chunks with verified citations. The web composer supports both inline `@` mentions and a multi-select document picker, converging on one deduped referenced-documents set.

  ## Why

  The existing `file_ids` channel injects full file text for a turn but does not produce citations. This adds a separate path for “answer over these matter documents” using file-scoped retrieval and the existing Citation Engine.

  ## What this PR does *not* do

  This does not change the existing `file_ids` verbatim attachment behavior.

  This does not add embed-on-reference or automatic ingestion retries for non-ready documents. Non-ready files are disabled in the UI and rejected fail-restrictive by the backend.

  ## Type of change

  - [ ] Bug fix (non-breaking change that fixes an issue)
  - [x] New feature (non-breaking change that adds capability)
  - [ ] Breaking change (fix or feature that would change existing behavior)
  - [x] Documentation update
  - [ ] Skill contribution (see attestation below)
  - [ ] Refactor (no behavioral change)
  - [x] Test / CI / tooling

  ## Testing

  - [x] Unit tests added/updated
  - [x] Integration tests added/updated (if applicable)
  - [ ] Manually tested against a real deployment (describe scenario below)
  - [ ] Acceptance test plan updated (if changing skill behavior)


  ## Documentation

  - [x] PRD updated (if user-facing behavior changes)
  - [ ] README updated (if quickstart or capability list changes)
  - [ ] Skill-authoring guide updated (if skill conventions change)
  - [ ] Deployment cookbook updated (if deployment changes)
  - [ ] Compliance documentation updated (if compliance posture changes)
  - [ ] OpenAPI schema regenerated (if API endpoints change)

  ## Transparency & governance invariants

## P1 Egress: checked

 The feature does not add a new outbound third-party call from `api/`. Backend retrieval is DB-local through `hybrid_search_files` in `api/app/knowledge/retrieval.py`. The only model/provider path remains the existing gateway send path in `api/app/api/chats.py`, where the existing `GatewayClient` flow is reused. The frontend only sends `referenced_file_ids` to the backend API in `web/src/lib/lq-ai/components/ChatPanel.svelte`.

## P2 Closed set: checked / effectively N/A

This PR does not add a new model, autonomous tool, MCP hook, or provider-invokable capability. It adds a bounded message field, capped at 16 in `api/app/schemas/chats.py`. The referenced files must already be in the user's matter KB set; `_validate_referenced_file_ids` enforces caller-owned, ready, project-attached KB files in `api/app/api/chats.py`. So there is no open-ended capability surface.

## P3 No raw payloads: checked

The new audit row stores ids and counts only: `file_ids`, `referenced_count`, `chunk_count`, `chunk_ids` in `api/app/api/chats.py`. The integration test explicitly asserts the audit row has no content/query payload keys in `api/tests/integration/test_referenced_files_send.py`. The retrieved text is sent to the model as context, but not added to the new audit details.

## P4 Fail restrictive: checked

Invalid references fail closed before gateway call. The validator returns `404` for malformed/non-owned/not-ready/not-in-matter/projectless cases in `api/app/api/chats.py`. Tests cover random foreign id returning `404` and assert the mocked gateway route was not called in `api/tests/integration/test_referenced_files_send.py`, plus projectless chat `404` in the same file. Non-ready and deleted file exclusion are covered in `api/tests/test_retrieval_files.py`.

## P5 Atomic audit: partially checked, caveat

"New retrieval audit event is written via shared audit helper and committed by the handler; no new durable referenced-file state is added." If the checklist is interpreted strictly, mark this **N/A/qualified**, not unconditionally checked.

## P6 One governance path: checked

The PR reuses existing gateway, retrieval-context injection, citation verification, and audit helpers. It does not create a separate citation verifier or custom routing/governance path. 

## P7 Human gate: N/A / checked as no new destructive path

This feature is not destructive or irreversible. It only selects documents for one chat turn. It does not add autonomous execution or a tool action requiring confirmation. 

## P8 Operator control: unchecked

 no new admin setting or operator enable/disable surface for referenced-file chat grounding. It is available as part of chat once matter KB files exist. There is bounded validation and KB readiness, but not an operator-facing feature flag/admin control.

## P9 User owns data: 

anonymization posture follows existing retrieved-context design."

## P10 Contract is truth: checked

This PR updates the API/schema contract and design docs. Schema adds `referenced_file_ids` and `applied_referenced_file_ids` in `api/app/schemas/chats.py`. OpenAPI has those fields in `docs/api/backend-openapi.yaml`. PRD adds the user/API/citation behavior in `docs/PRD.md`, and ADR 0022 records the design in `docs/adr/0022-referenced-file-ids-chat.md`.

  ## DCO sign-off

  - [x] All commits in this PR are signed off per the [DCO](../CONTRIBUTING.md#sign-off-
  developer-certificate-of-origin)

  ## Related issues

  N/A

entries.title: #266: feat(auditor): cross-user auditor/compliance role (read-only, audit-logged)
entries.text:
feat(auditor): cross-user auditor/compliance role (read-only, audit-logged) ## Cross-user auditor / compliance role

Adds a read-only, deployment-wide **`auditor`** RBAC role so a supervising partner or compliance officer can review another user's audit trail. The **privileged-reader set `{admin, auditor}`** may now read another user's citation ledger, message sources, per-message citations, autonomous-session ledger (+ the embedded fiduciary gate), and chat receipts — with every privileged cross-user read audit-logged. Ordinary owner-scoping for `member`/`viewer` is unchanged.

> ⚠️ **Security-gated (authorization change).** Requires a human security reviewer per CODEOWNERS. This PR was built via subagent-driven TDD with a per-task spec+quality gate and a final Opus whole-branch security review (verdict: ready-to-merge with fixes, all applied). Direct attention to the two items called out below.

Addresses Donna upstream request `lq-ai-cross-user-auditor-role`.

### The authorization contract (failure-mode matrix)
| Caller | ledger / sources / citations / session-ledger | receipts (read + export) |
|---|---|---|
| Owner | 200, no audit row | 200, no audit row |
| `admin` or `auditor`, non-owner | **200 + one audit row** | **200 + one audit row** |
| `member`/`viewer`, non-owner | **404** (existence-safe, unchanged) | **403** (unchanged) |
| nonexistent id | 404 | 404 |

- **Existence-safety:** a non-privileged non-owner gets a 404 on the ledger-family endpoints **indistinguishable** from a nonexistent id (same exception/message/shape). Verified identical across all three ledger endpoints.
- **Audit-the-auditor:** every privileged cross-user read writes one `audit_log` row (`auditor.{ledger,sources,citations,session_ledger,receipts}_viewed` / `receipts_exported`), `details.viewed_user_id` = the owner, with request IP/UA/request-id captured. Owner reads write nothing.
- **Least privilege:** `auditor` gains only READ; `is_admin` stays `False`; writes remain owner-scoped (an auditor cannot mutate another user's data). Granted via the existing `PATCH /api/v1/admin/users/{id}/role`. No JWT change.

### Two items for the security reviewer to consciously sign off
1. **`/citations` was folded into the privileged-read set** (maintainer-approved) — the auditor can now read the quoted `source_text` of another user's citations for a reviewed matter, consistent with `/ledger` + `/sources`.
2. **`MutatingUser` is unwired** (pre-existing) — so "read-only role" is enforced by owner-scoping on writes, not by the role gate. Documented honestly; **DE-378** filed to wire `MutatingUser` across mutating endpoints for true global read-only enforcement.

### Migration
`0065` widens the `chk_users_role_enum` CHECK constraint to include `auditor` (reversible; no data migration).

### Verification
- Full api suite green; per-endpoint cross-user triads (non-privileged 404/403 + no audit; privileged 200 + correct audit; owner 200 + no audit) for all five endpoint families; existence-safety and the receipts-export refactor independently verified behavior-preserving.
- OpenAPI conformance + DE-373 generated-export drift-guard both green.

Spec: `docs/superpowers/specs/2026-07-02-cross-user-auditor-role-design.md` · Plan: `docs/superpowers/plans/2026-07-02-cross-user-auditor-role.md` · Donna contract note: `docs/integration/2026-07-01-donna-fiduciary-auditability-integration.md` §2.6a

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

entries.title: #265: feat(gateway): Amazon Bedrock provider adapter via Bedrock Mantle Endpoint (DE-035)
entries.text:
feat(gateway): Amazon Bedrock provider adapter via Bedrock Mantle Endpoint (DE-035) ## What this PR does

Implements DE-035: a `BedrockMantleAdapter` provider adapter, giving the gateway access to Anthropic and OpenAI current-generation models (plus a growing set of others — Gemma 4, Grok 4.3) through the operator's own AWS account via AWS's **Bedrock Mantle** endpoint. This supersedes the original InvokeModel/SigV4 plan.

## Why

The original DE-035 plan targeted Bedrock's `bedrock-runtime` InvokeModel API — SigV4 request signing plus a hand-rolled AWS Event Stream binary frame parser, ~300 LOC combined, scoped to Anthropic-on-Bedrock only. Live testing against a real AWS account (2026-07-01) found AWS's newer **Bedrock Mantle** endpoint (`bedrock-mantle.{region}.api.aws`) serves the same models over three wire protocols that mirror existing, non-AWS-specific APIs this gateway already speaks elsewhere: **Chat Completions** (OpenAI's own format — reused verbatim), **Messages** (Anthropic's own format — reused via direct import of `app/providers/anthropic.py`'s translation functions), and **Responses** (OpenAI's newer format, genuinely new translation code in this PR). All three are authenticated with a single Bearer token instead of per-request SigV4 signing. This eliminates the signer and the binary frame parser entirely.

Mantle also carries AWS's documented [Zero Operator Access (ZOA) guarantee](https://aws.amazon.com/blogs/machine-learning/exploring-the-zero-operator-access-design-of-mantle/), hardware-attested via EC2 instance attestation + NitroTPM: no AWS operator, customer operator, or model provider has any technical means to access customer prompts/completions processed by Mantle. This PR does not change how the gateway itself authenticates or handles the credential — the ZOA guarantee is a property of AWS's infrastructure this PR routes through, not something implemented in this codebase.

`"bedrock"` (InvokeModel) stays in `ProviderType` as a fallback for operators who need it; `"bedrock_mantle"` is the new, preferred type for new deployments.

## What this PR does *not* do

- Does not remove or deprecate the existing `"bedrock"` (InvokeModel/SigV4) provider type — both coexist.

## Type of change

- [x] New feature (non-breaking change that adds capability)

## Testing

- [x] Unit tests added/updated
- [x] Manually tested against a real deployment (describe scenario below)

`gateway/tests/test_bedrock_mantle_adapter.py` — 23 unit tests (respx-mocked httpx, following `azure_openai.py`'s test pattern) covering construction, per-request protocol routing, all three wire-format tiers' happy paths (unary + streaming), tool-call mapping, the Responses tier's per-model URL selection and runtime fallback, dropped/out-of-scope item types, the server-side-tool-calling egress-boundary guard (FR3.7/ADR 0014), and the three live-verified error classes (entitlement-403, entitlement-401, wrong-API-for-model-400) — plus 1 `@pytest.mark.provider` live-provider test, matching CONTRIBUTING.md's "new provider adapter" requirement.

<details>
<summary>Manual testing notes</summary>

Live-tested against a real AWS account across all three tiers:

- **Chat Completions** — `openai.gpt-oss-20b` and `zai.glm-5`: both 200, real content, both directions of the adapter (unary confirmed; live provider test uses this tier).
- **Responses** — `openai.gpt-oss-20b`, `google.gemma-4-31b`, `xai.grok-4.3`: all 200, real content — Grok needed a larger `max_tokens` budget the first time (its model card documents always-on reasoning that consumes budget before visible output; same pattern already known for `gpt-oss-20b`, not a bug). `openai.gpt-5.4`/`openai.gpt-5.5`: clean 401 "model not available for this account" — request reaches AWS correctly.
- **Messages** — `anthropic.claude-opus-4-8`, `anthropic.claude-opus-4-7`, `anthropic.claude-haiku-4-5`, `anthropic.claude-sonnet-5`: all clean 403 "model not available for this account" — same as above, correct transport/auth/routing.

All entitlement-blocked models above were tested using a personal AWS account with very low activity; likely account entitlement, may require an AWS Sales Support request to enable — not a defect in this PR's code.

All testing used a **long-lived Bedrock API key** generated via the Amazon Bedrock (mantle endpoint) console's "API keys" page (the same flow every Bedrock Mantle model card's "Sample Code" section documents), not a short-lived/rotated credential.

Two real bugs were found and fixed via this live testing (see commit history): a double-`/v1` URL bug in the Messages tier that was silently producing 404s instead of the real 403 entitlement response, and an inverted default in the Responses tier's per-model URL selection (originally defaulted to the general path with a hardcoded exception list; live testing showed the exception path is actually the norm for every model launched on Mantle so far except two, so the default was flipped and a runtime fallback-with-cache added for models neither list has seen yet).

</details>

## Documentation

- [x] PRD updated (if user-facing behavior changes)
- [x] README updated (if quickstart or capability list changes)

`docs/PRD.md` §9 DE-035 rewritten in full to describe the three-tier Mantle approach. `README.md` and `docs/HONEST-STATE.md` updated to reflect five shipped provider adapters (was four) with per-tier verification status. `docs/api/gateway-openapi.yaml`'s `ProviderType` enum updated at all four sites where it's duplicated (note: unlike `api/`'s OpenAPI sketch, the gateway's has no automated schema-conformance test / `make openapi`-equivalent generator — this is hand-maintained, and one of the four sites was initially missed and only caught by manual re-review before opening this PR; worth considering as a follow-up DE if this class of drift recurs). `gateway.yaml.example` documents the new provider block.

## Transparency & governance invariants

- [x] **Egress (P1):** all three tiers route through this one adapter, invoked only via the gateway's existing `build_adapter` dispatch — no new egress path outside the gateway.
- [x] **Closed set (P2):** n/a — this is an inference provider, not an autonomous/tool capability; models are the operator's own `gateway.yaml` allowlist, unchanged mechanism.
- [x] **No raw payloads (P3):** error mapping (`_classify_mantle_error`) surfaces status/type/code only; `test_error_mapping_never_leaks_api_key` pins that the Bearer token never appears in a surfaced error.
- [x] **Fail restrictive (P4):** missing `AWS_BEARER_TOKEN_BEDROCK` at startup is a warning, not fatal (matches every other adapter's pattern) — the provider is simply skipped, requests routing to it get a clean 503.
- [x] **Atomic audit (P5):** n/a — no new state-changing endpoint; existing `inference_routing_log` write path is unchanged by this adapter.
- [x] **One governance path (P6):** the Responses tier's tool-translation only ever forwards the gateway's own governed `tools`/`tool_choice` (ADR 0015) — never defaults to or passes through an AWS/OpenAI built-in/server-side tool type; pinned by `test_responses_tier_no_tools_configured_sends_no_tools_field`.
- [x] **Human gate (P7):** n/a — no destructive action.
- [x] **Operator control (P8):** provider is opt-in via `gateway.yaml`'s existing enable/disable + `api_key_env` mechanism, unchanged from every other adapter.
- [x] **User owns data (P9):** n/a — no new data flow beyond the existing inference request/response path all adapters share.
- [x] **Contract is truth (P10):** `docs/PRD.md`, `docs/HONEST-STATE.md`, `docs/api/gateway-openapi.yaml`, `README.md`, `gateway.yaml.example` all updated in this PR.

## DCO sign-off

- [x] All commits in this PR are signed off per the DCO

## Related issues

None filed — DE-035 was already tracked in `docs/PRD.md` §9 prior to this work; this PR closes it out.

## Reviewer notes

**This PR touches `gateway/`, which is CODEOWNERS-routed to `@legalquants/security` per the repo's own rule — flagging that explicitly since it's a hard gate, not optional.**

Commit-by-commit structure (6 commits, each independently reviewable):
1. `docs(DE-035)` — the PRD rewrite, precedes any code.
2. `feat(gateway): add bedrock_mantle provider type...` — config-level plumbing (`ProviderType` enum, `build_adapter` dispatch, `gateway.yaml.example`, `.env*`/`docker-compose*` plumbing for `AWS_BEARER_TOKEN_BEDROCK`, OpenAPI enum sites).
3. `feat(gateway): implement BedrockMantleAdapter` — the adapter itself.
4. `test(gateway): unit + provider-integration tests` — 23 unit + 1 live test.
5. `fix(gateway): route Responses tier per-model...` — the routing/URL-selection fix described above, found via live testing after the initial implementation.
6. `refactor(gateway): reuse anthropic.py for the Mantle Messages tier` — the Messages tier's request/response/SSE translation was initially a duplicate of `app/providers/anthropic.py`'s (~250 LOC), written defensively against an unvalidated wire-compatibility assumption. AWS documents Mantle's Messages tier as compatible with the Messages API, so this commit removes the duplication and calls `anthropic.py`'s functions directly — mirroring how the Chat Completions tier already reuses `OpenAIAdapter` via composition.

Would appreciate scrutiny on: the Responses-tier URL fallback-and-cache mechanism (`_responses_url_with_fallback` in `bedrock_mantle.py`) — it's a genuine, if small, piece of runtime self-correction logic, and worth a second opinion on whether the caching approach (instance-lifetime, no TTL/invalidation) is the right call given the adapter instance persists for the gateway process's lifetime.

**Cost control:** operators wiring up a real Bedrock Mantle key may want an [AWS Budget](https://console.aws.amazon.com/billing/home#/budgets) with an alert threshold on the Bedrock service before testing broadly — nothing in this PR enforces a spend cap on the AWS side, only the gateway's own existing rate-limit/cost-tracking config applies.

**Getting a key to test with:** Amazon Bedrock (mantle endpoint) console → **API keys** (left nav) → **Create key** (long-term). This is the same flow documented on every Bedrock Mantle model card's "Sample Code" section. No IAM role/policy setup is required beyond having console access to generate the key — the adapter's entire auth surface is the single Bearer token; **it never uses `AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID`/`AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY`/SigV4 at all**, confirmed by grep across `bedrock_mantle.py` and by every live test in this PR running with only `AWS_BEARER_TOKEN_BEDROCK` set.

entries.title: #264: fix(web): persist per-chat model selection across page refresh
entries.text:
fix(web): persist per-chat model selection across page refresh ## What this PR does

Persists a chat's selected model across a page refresh, by saving it to `localStorage`.

## Why

If a user picked a model for a chat and then refreshed the page, their choice was silently thrown away — the chat reverted to the default model with no warning. Root cause: the selected model was only ever held in memory (`modelByChat`, a component-local `Record`), never saved anywhere durable. A refresh remounts the component from scratch, so that in-memory value resets, and the picker's fallback logic quietly picks the default instead.

## What this PR does *not* do

Does not change the per-chat scoping of model selection (a deliberate, pre-existing design — different chats can already use different models; this PR only makes each chat's choice survive a refresh). Does not add cross-device/cross-browser persistence — this is a `localStorage` fix, scoped to one browser on one device, matching the severity of the bug.

## Type of change

- [x] Bug fix (non-breaking change that fixes an issue)

## Testing

- [x] Unit tests added/updated

`web/src/lib/lq-ai/__tests__/ChatPanel-model-persist.test.ts` — 7 tests, mirroring `ReceiptsDrawer.test.ts`'s `MockStorage` pattern exactly (same file already does per-chat `localStorage` persistence for a different feature — the receipts-drawer open state). Covers key-namespacing, read/write, cross-chat isolation, overwrite, and round-trip.

<details>
<summary>Manual testing notes</summary>

Verified against a locally running stack: picked a non-default model for a chat, sent a message, hard-refreshed the page — the picker still showed the previously-selected model instead of resetting to the default (`anthropic-prod`/`claude-opus-4-7`).

Also verified the race-condition concern doesn't apply: `loadShell()` (which populates `availableModels`, the picker's source list) is `await`ed in `onMount` before `selectChat` ever runs, so by the time a persisted model id is hydrated from `localStorage`, the picker's model list is already loaded.

</details>

## Documentation

*(no boxes checked — internal bugfix, no user-facing capability or doc change)*

## Transparency & governance invariants

- [x] **Egress (P1):** n/a — no outbound call involved; `localStorage` is a browser-local read/write.
- [x] **Closed set (P2):** n/a.
- [x] **No raw payloads (P3):** n/a — only a model-id string is persisted, no message content.
- [x] **Fail restrictive (P4):** `readPersistedModel`/`writePersistedModel` both no-op safely if `localStorage` is unavailable (SSR/private-browsing edge cases) — falls through to the existing default-selection behavior.
- [x] **Atomic audit (P5):** n/a.
- [x] **One governance path (P6):** n/a.
- [x] **Human gate (P7):** n/a.
- [x] **Operator control (P8):** n/a.
- [x] **User owns data (P9):** n/a — client-local preference only, not sent to the backend.
- [x] **Contract is truth (P10):** n/a — no API/schema change.

## DCO sign-off

- [x] All commits in this PR are signed off per the DCO

## Related issues

None filed — found during manual testing of an unrelated feature.

## Reviewer notes

`selectModel()` write-throughs to `localStorage` on every pick; `selectChat()` hydrates `modelByChat[chat.id]` from `localStorage` only when no in-session choice already exists, so a same-session pick is never clobbered by a stale persisted value.

entries.title: #263: fix(web): attach chat files to the model and keep their status live
entries.text:
fix(web): attach chat files to the model and keep their status live ## What this PR does

Fixes two bugs in the per-message attach-file flow: attached files never reached the model (the request body never included `file_ids`), and the attached-file status badge stayed frozen at "pending" forever after upload.

## Why

Uploading a file to a chat and asking the model about it silently failed — the model would answer as if no file was attached, with no error surfaced anywhere. Separately, the status badge never reflected real ingestion progress, so a user had no way to tell when a file was actually ready to be referenced.

## What this PR does *not* do

Does not touch the project-level attached-files feature (`attached_file_ids` on `Project`, surfaced read-only via `projectFiles`) — that's a separate, already-working code path. Does not add a UI affordance for manually retrying a failed ingestion; that's out of scope.

## Type of change

- [x] Bug fix (non-breaking change that fixes an issue)

## Testing

- [x] Unit tests added/updated

`web/src/lib/lq-ai/__tests__/ChatPanel-attach-file.test.ts` — 10 tests covering `hasPendingFileStatus()` and `fileIdsForSend()`, the two pure functions extracted into `ChatPanel.svelte`'s module block (mirroring the existing `detectSlashAt` convention, since `@testing-library/svelte` is unavailable for this file — see that test's header for the rationale).

<details>
<summary>Manual testing notes</summary>

Verified end-to-end against a locally running stack:
1. Uploaded a PDF to a chat, watched the attached-files panel badge transition from "pending" to "ready" within a few seconds (previously stayed on "pending" indefinitely).
2. Asked the model a question about the document's content — it answered from the real document text (previously it had no access to the file at all).
3. Confirmed via the gateway's audit log that `inference.message_files_attached` events show `injected_count` matching `attached_count` for the turn.
4. Confirmed via direct DB inspection that `documents.normalized_content` was populated and the ~33K-character contract text was genuinely being injected as a system message before the user turn.

</details>

## Documentation

*(no boxes checked — internal bugfix, no user-facing capability or doc change)*

## Transparency & governance invariants

- [x] **Egress (P1):** n/a — no new outbound call; uses the existing `GET /files/{id}` endpoint already called elsewhere in this file.
- [x] **Closed set (P2):** n/a — no new model/autonomous capability.
- [x] **No raw payloads (P3):** n/a — no new logging.
- [x] **Fail restrictive (P4):** the status-poll `catch` block logs and leaves the badge at its last-known state rather than throwing; a failed poll doesn't break the chat.
- [x] **Atomic audit (P5):** n/a — no new state-changing endpoint.
- [x] **One governance path (P6):** n/a.
- [x] **Human gate (P7):** n/a — no destructive action.
- [x] **Operator control (P8):** n/a.
- [x] **User owns data (P9):** n/a — no new data flow; `file_ids` uses the backend's pre-existing, already-anonymization-aware injection path.
- [x] **Contract is truth (P10):** n/a — `file_ids` was already documented in the backend's `MessageCreateRequest` schema; this PR only wires the frontend to use it.

## DCO sign-off

- [x] All commits in this PR are signed off per the DCO

## Related issues

None filed — found and fixed during manual testing of an unrelated feature.

## Reviewer notes

Two independent root causes in the same file, landed together since they were found and fixed in one pass:

1. `sendMessage()` never included `file_ids: chatFiles.map(f => f.id)` in the `messagesApi.sendMessageStream()` call — the backend's `file_ids` channel (`api/app/schemas/chats.py::MessageCreateRequest.file_ids`) was fully implemented and unused.
2. `uploadAttached()` pushed the upload-response file object into `chatFiles` once and nothing ever re-fetched it, so `ingestion_status` stayed frozen at its upload-time value. Added lightweight polling (3s interval, stops once every attached file reaches a terminal status) rather than reusing the existing chat-streaming SSE infrastructure — smaller diff, matches this repo's other non-realtime status patterns.

Would appreciate a second look at the polling interval/approach if there's a preference for SSE instead.

entries.title: #262: feat(DE-365 sub-2): fiduciary-grade Learn playgrounds (authority/ledger/gate/treatment/matter-session)
entries.text:
feat(DE-365 sub-2): fiduciary-grade Learn playgrounds (authority/ledger/gate/treatment/matter-session) ## DE-365 sub-project 2 — Fiduciary-grade Learn playgrounds

Closes the known Learn-tab visualization gap for the **fiduciary-grade agentic legal work** milestone (flagged in `HONEST-STATE.md §11` and `donna-sync §2`). Adds **5 new self-contained interactive playgrounds** and wires them into the Learn "How it works" page as a labeled cluster (sections 18–22).

### What's here
| # | Slug | Covers | Honest caveat surfaced in-viz |
|---|---|---|---|
| 18 | `authority-sources` | content-source registry; toggle configured providers → `enabled` vs unavailable-with-reason | EUR-Lex get-by-CELEX only (DE-374/DE-375); operator-config-gated |
| 19 | `citation-ledger` | read-trail → click-to-trace to source + char offset; provenance = non-assertion | id + offset only, no raw payloads (P3) |
| 20 | `fiduciary-gate` | derive-don't-assert bucketing into **PASS / SUPPORTED / FAIL** (provenance excluded) | chat vs autonomous parity gaps (DE-370/DE-371) |
| 21 | `treatment-layer` | derived treatment signals (followed/distinguished/criticized) + trace to citing cases | "derived, not editorial"; snippets never stored |
| 22 | `matter-session-flow` | capstone: plan→act→observe→replan under brakes **R5→R6→R4** + step cap | backend shipped; no dedicated matter-intake UI yet |

Plus: how-page wiring (cluster divider + sections 18–22, stale surface count fixed 16→22), and a `donna-sync` update moving the 5 from *planned* to *available* with final slugs/URLs/section numbers + integration guidance for Donna CC.

### Design principles held
- **Honest / no-overclaim** — every viz surfaces its real caveat with terms matching the actual backend code (a final-review pass corrected `citation-ledger` to the real `verification_status` enum and de-styled a paraphrase that read as a verbatim ADR quote).
- **Vendor-neutral** — no named competitor anywhere.
- **Self-contained** — each playground is one static HTML file with **no runtime network calls** (only the GitHub verify-in-source links, opened in a new tab). Verified mechanically.
- **Verify-in-source** — every claim links to the real module + ADR on `main`.

### Verification
- **Real headless-Chromium render** on all 5: `rc=0`, zero JS errors, zero network attempts, JS-driven content populated.
- Prettier + ESLint clean on the Svelte edit; how-page section/iframe counts = 22/22.
- Built via subagent-driven development: one implementer per playground, each through a spec+quality gate, plus a final whole-branch review (verdict: ready to merge) and a consolidated fix wave.

### Scope
`web/` static HTML + one SvelteKit `.svelte` edit + docs. **No** backend/API/DB/gateway/CI/auth changes → not security-review-gated.

Spec: `docs/superpowers/specs/2026-07-02-fiduciary-playgrounds-design.md` · Plan: `docs/superpowers/plans/2026-07-02-fiduciary-playgrounds.md`

Refs DE-365

entries.title: #261: Feat/caddy tailscale recipe cleanup
entries.text:
Feat/caddy tailscale recipe cleanup ## What this PR does

<!-- One or two sentences describing the change. Keep this scoped to what
actually changed; the why goes in the next section. --> 1) Removed duplicate .env.example file, 2) Changed README to contain the .env.example changes for this recipe as well as a paragraph on why to use this deployment, and 3) fixed stale comment in docker-compose.proxy.yml.

n/a. If you can't, stop and either fix the change or surface the decision to a

- [ ] **Egress (P1):** any outbound third-party call goes through the gateway only — no direct egress from `api/`.

- [ ] **No raw payloads (P3):** no row or log line I add carries raw content (message bodies, tool args/results, fetched text, keys) — counts, types, ids, digests, outcomes only.

- [ ] **Atomic audit (P5):** state changes get an audit row committed in the same transaction (helpers flush, the handler commits).
- [ ] **One governance path (P6):** I reused the shared governance/audit helpers rather than re-deriving tier/cost/audit logic.

entries.title: #260: docs(DE-365 sub-1): README/HONEST-STATE/ROADMAP claims-vs-reality honesty audit
entries.text:
docs(DE-365 sub-1): README/HONEST-STATE/ROADMAP claims-vs-reality honesty audit ## DE-365 sub-project 1 — docs/README claims-vs-reality honesty audit

First of three DE-365 sub-projects (**1) this docs honesty audit → 2) Learn-tab fiduciary-grade playgrounds → 3) vendor-neutral competitor comparison**). Makes `README.md`, `docs/HONEST-STATE.md`, and `docs/ROADMAP.md` faithfully reflect what the code does as of the fiduciary-grade milestone — the entire Phase-2 milestone (Citation Ledger, fiduciary gate, governed matter sessions, free authority sources, treatment layer) was previously **absent from all three docs**.

**Docs-only. No code, test, or product changes** (`git diff --name-only main..HEAD` is docs-only).

### Approach — bidirectional audit, durable worksheet

A durable audit worksheet (`docs/audits/2026-07-01-claims-vs-reality.md`) drives everything:
- **Direction A (docs→code):** every existing capability/status claim gets a verdict — 40 rows, 25 Accurate / 8 Stale / 4 Understated / 3 Overstated — each with the proving-or-refuting artifact and a concrete resolution.
- **Direction B (code→docs):** enumerate shipped-but-undocumented capabilities from ADRs 0018–0021, `SOURCE_REGISTRY`, and the DE ledger — the fiduciary-grade milestone — each with its honest caveat.

Then the three docs are reconciled in one consistent pass, and a 6-point definition-of-done verification is recorded. A stdlib-only link-check helper (`docs/audits/check_doc_links.py`) enforces that every added proving link resolves.

### What changed
- **README.md** — added the fiduciary-grade milestone (5 capability paragraphs + a roadmap-table row), each with its honest caveat *in-paragraph* and a proving link; updated the "current shipped" framing (it previously stopped at the MCP milestone); fixed 2 pre-existing dead doc links.
- **docs/HONEST-STATE.md** — new §5.6 (fiduciary-grade shipped state) + the genuine remaining gaps (no matter-intake UI; chat/autonomous gate parity DE-370/371; EUR-Lex get-only DE-374/375); migration head 0055→0064; refreshed test-file counts.
- **docs/ROADMAP.md** — fiduciary items shipped; open punch-list = DE-370/371/374/375/376 + matter-intake UI; fixed the DE-200/MCP staleness (subsystem shipped, no longer framed as future M5 work).
- **docs/PRD.md** — filed **DE-377** (genericize the one pre-existing named third-party reference in README — a scope disclaimer, not an overclaim; deferred to a later vendor-neutrality sweep). PRD §8 otherwise untouched (finding-gated).

### Honesty guarantees (the point of this PR)
- Every "shipped ✓" links to a real proving artifact (ADR / code path); every fiduciary capability carries its honest caveat (CLAUDE.md principle 4).
- Cross-document consistency: nothing marked shipped in README is listed as unbuilt in HONEST-STATE; ROADMAP agrees.
- **Vendor-neutral** — the reconciled docs name no competitor product (0 hits); the deferred comparison (sub-project 3) is itself vendor-neutral.

### Verification (all reproduce on the branch)
- ✅ `git diff --name-only main..HEAD` — docs-only, no code/test/web.
- ✅ `python3 docs/audits/check_doc_links.py README.md docs/HONEST-STATE.md docs/ROADMAP.md docs/audits/2026-07-01-claims-vs-reality.md` → **0 dangling links**.
- ✅ Vendor-neutral grep over README/HONEST-STATE/ROADMAP → **0 hits**.
- ✅ DE state: DE-370/371/374/375/376 open, DE-373 shipped.
- ✅ Worksheet verification counts independently re-verified (MCP-in-ROADMAP = 11, README shipped-grep = 26, test files 233/67/80/17).

### Process
Built via subagent-driven development: 6 tasks, each two-stage-reviewed (spec + quality); an Opus whole-branch review returned **no Critical, no Important** (the two count-precision fixes it recommended are applied). Not security-gated (docs-only, no `gateway/**` / citation-code changes) — normal review + merge; mirror `origin`→`tucuxi` on main after merge.

entries.title: #259: build(windows): enforce LF line endings on entrypoint scripts
entries.text:
build(windows): enforce LF line endings on entrypoint scripts ## What this PR does

This PR adds a root-level `.gitattributes` file to enforce LF (Linux-style) line endings for all shell scripts (`*.sh`).

## Why

On Windows hosts, Git often defaults to converting line endings to CRLF (`core.autocrlf = true`) upon checkout. When copied into Linux-based Docker containers, these carriage returns (`\r`) corrupt the shebang execution, causing immediate startup crashes such as:
`exec /usr/local/bin/lq-ai-gateway-entrypoint.sh: no such file or directory`

Enforcing LF endings at the repository level ensures the stack boots out-of-the-box on Windows development environments without manual line-ending conversion.

## What this PR does *not* do

This PR does not modify any application logic, database schemas, or API endpoints. It is strictly limited to file formatting and repository-level Git configurations for Windows compatibility.

## Type of change

- [x] Bug fix (non-breaking change that fixes an issue)
- [ ] New feature (non-breaking change that adds capability)
- [ ] Breaking change (fix or feature that would change existing behavior)
- [ ] Documentation update
- [ ] Skill contribution (see attestation below)
- [ ] Refactor (no behavioral change)
- [x] Test / CI / tooling

## Testing

- [ ] Unit tests added/updated
- [ ] Integration tests added/updated (if applicable)
- [x] Manually tested against a real deployment (describe scenario below)
- [ ] Acceptance test plan updated (if changing skill behavior)

<details>
<summary>Manual testing notes (if applicable)</summary>

* **Environment:** Windows 11 host running Docker Desktop (without WSL backend) and PowerShell.
* **Initial State:** Running `docker compose up -d` on a fresh checkout resulted in `lq-ai-gateway-1` failing its healthcheck and immediately exiting with the `no such file or directory` entrypoint execution error.
* **Action:** Created `.gitattributes` with `*.sh text eol=lf`, converted `gateway/entrypoint.sh` and `api/entrypoint.sh` line endings from CRLF to LF, and ran `docker compose build --no-cache gateway api` to rebuild.
* **Result:** Ran `docker compose up -d` again. All 9 containers successfully started, passed their respective healthchecks, and entered a `Healthy` state.

</details>

## Documentation

- [ ] PRD updated (if user-facing behavior changes)
- [ ] README updated (if quickstart or capability list changes)
- [ ] Skill-authoring guide updated (if skill conventions change)
- [ ] Deployment cookbook updated (if deployment changes)
- [ ] Compliance documentation updated (if compliance posture changes)
- [ ] OpenAPI schema regenerated (if API endpoints change)

## Transparency & governance invariants

- [ ] **Egress (P1):** n/a
- [ ] **Closed set (P2):** n/a
- [ ] **No raw payloads (P3):** n/a
- [ ] **Fail restrictive (P4):** n/a
- [ ] **Atomic audit (P5):** n/a
- [ ] **One governance path (P6):** n/a
- [ ] **Human gate (P7):** n/a
- [ ] **Operator control (P8):** n/a
- [ ] **User owns data (P9):** n/a
- [x] **Contract is truth (P10):** Included `.gitattributes` inside this PR to maintain repository environment standards.

## DCO sign-off

- [x] All commits in this PR are signed off per the [DCO](../CONTRIBUTING.md#sign-off-developer-certificate-of-origin)

## Related issues

Resolves local Windows development startup failures.

---

## Reviewer notes

None. The change is lightweight and standard for cross-platform repositories containing executable scripts.

entries.title: #257: feat(WS-E PR2b): EUR-Lex authority source (get-by-CELEX)
entries.text:
feat(WS-E PR2b): EUR-Lex authority source (get-by-CELEX) ## WS-E PR2b — EUR-Lex authority source (get-by-CELEX)

Adds **EUR-Lex** (EU legislation + CJEU case law, via the EU Publications Office *Cellar* service) as the **third free authority source** after GovInfo and SEC EDGAR — completing WS-E PR2's "≥2 new free sources" goal (GovInfo + EDGAR + EUR-Lex = three). Retrieve-and-verify **by CELEX id only** (no full-text search — deferred to DE-374), on the generic ADR-0021 content-source registry + `retrieve_authority` + citation-verify path that PR2a generalized.

**No new ADR** (ADR 0021 D6 scopes EUR-Lex). **No migration** — reuses `message_authority_citations` + `authority_text_cache` (mig 0064). `gate.py`, `ledger.py`, `alembic/**` **untouched** (provable via `git diff --name-only main..HEAD`).

### 🔒 Security-review notes (please read first)

This PR touches the gateway egress boundary + the citation-integrity surface + a shared chat schema, so it is security-gated. Key points to pre-empt questions:

- **Egress is https-only; the Cellar 303 redirect target is http.** The gateway adapter follows redirects **manually** (`follow_redirects=False`) so no unvalidated hop is ever dispatched. Each hop is `_force_https`-upgraded **then** `validate_egress_target`-checked (host allowlist `[publications.europa.eu]` + public-IP SSRF gate) **before** it is fetched. Verified against: http-downgrade, open-redirect / redirect-to-internal-host / redirect-to-non-allowlisted-host, and redirect-loop DoS (bounded `MAX_REDIRECTS=5`).
- **Auth = descriptive `User-Agent`, no API key.** Same posture as EDGAR. `skip_anonymization=True`, `read_only=True`. Free source → no `cost_per_call` → R4 cost brake stays a no-op.
- **`external_ref` = the plain CELEX**, validated `^[A-Za-z0-9._-]+$` (the `authority_text_cache` key charset). Unsafe treaty/corrigendum CELEX (ids with `/` or `()`, e.g. `12016E/TXT`) are rejected with a **400 before any egress** (deferred to DE-375).
- **`health_check` is intentionally a policy-only probe** (validates base-url egress policy; does not live-ping Cellar). Cellar has no cheap unauthenticated ping and every real fetch is a redirect-heavy content negotiation, so a live probe would be misleading. It reports `reachable=True` when the config is valid — an honest statement of what it checked.
- Every EUR-Lex call still goes through `guarded_tool_call` → R5/R6/R4 → `governed_tool_invocation` → gateway. Nothing is exposed unless an operator explicitly configures an `eurlex` provider.

### What's in the branch

1. **Gateway `EurLexToolAdapter`** (`gateway/app/providers/tool/eurlex.py`, `config.py`, `main.py`, `__init__.py`) — Cellar content-negotiation by CELEX; manual redirect follow + http→https upgrade + per-hop SSRF re-validation; unsafe-CELEX pre-egress reject; `content_kind` derived from the CELEX descriptor; `get_authority` only. Faithful mirror of the reviewed `EdgarToolAdapter` base-class contract.
2. **Backend `EurLexAdapter` + `SOURCE_REGISTRY["eurlex"]`** (`api/app/research/adapters.py`, `registry.py`) — thin carry-through, `ops=("get_authority",)`, five `eu_*` content kinds.
3. **Per-op chat tool schemas** (`api/app/chat/tool_schemas.py`) — a source appears under `search_authority`/`get_authority` only if its registry `ops` include that op, so a get-only source (EUR-Lex) is exposed honestly (only `get_authority`). **Behavior-preserving for GovInfo/EDGAR** (both ops) — confirmed by unchanged pre-existing tests.
4. **Verify set** (`api/app/citation/authority.py`) — the five `eu_*` kinds added to `_VERIFIABLE_CONTENT_KINDS`; identical across the registry, the CELEX→kind derivation, and the verify set (a mismatch would silently drop quotes).
5. **Docs** — `gateway.yaml.example` `eurlex-prod` block; PRD WS-E status; DE-374 (SPARQL search), DE-375 (treaty/corrigendum CELEX), DE-376 (human subtitle + partial-payload test).

### Verification

- ✅ `git diff --name-only main..HEAD` — no `gate.py` / `ledger.py` / `alembic/**`.
- ✅ **api full DB-backed solo suite: 2583 passed, 1 skipped** (real gate, `DATABASE_URL` set).
- ✅ **gateway suite: 744 passed, 3 skipped**; `mypy --strict` clean.
- ✅ api `ruff check` + `ruff format --check` + `mypy app` clean (189 files); gateway `ruff`/`format` clean.
- ✅ DE-373 OpenAPI drift-guard (`test_openapi_export`) + `test_openapi`/`test_endpoints` path counts **unchanged** — EUR-Lex adds no backend route.

### Process

Built via subagent-driven development: 5 tasks, each implemented by a fresh subagent with a two-stage (spec + quality) review; final Opus whole-branch review returned **no Critical, no Important** findings (verdict: ready to merge, subject to this security review). The two one-liner cleanups it recommended on the security-gated files (drop a dead `except EgressRefused: raise` no-op; harden the schema registry lookup with `.get()`) are applied in the last commit; the remaining Minors are filed as DE-376.

**Live-verified (2026-07-01, https):** `GET publications.europa.eu/resource/celex/{CELEX}` + `Accept: application/xhtml+xml` + `Accept-Language: eng` → 303 → http manifestation → upgrade https → 200 xhtml (GDPR `32016R0679`); missing CELEX → 404.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

entries.title: #255: feat(api): generated OpenAPI export + CI drift-guard (DE-373)
entries.text:
feat(api): generated OpenAPI export + CI drift-guard (DE-373) Implements **DE-373** — the durable fix for the OpenAPI drift that PR #253 patched by hand.

## Problem
`docs/api/backend-openapi.yaml` is a hand-maintained *sketch* with **no test against the live app**, so it silently drifts (4 routes were missing at `3659360`, invisible to the downstream Donna typegen).

## Fix (generate-alongside)
- **`app.openapi_export.build_openapi_yaml()`** — dumps `app.openapi()` to deterministic, canonically-sorted YAML. Shared by the generator and the guard, so they can't disagree.
- **`scripts/gen_openapi.py` + `make openapi`** — write `docs/api/backend-openapi.generated.yaml` (the faithful, machine-generated companion; recommended for client typegen).
- **`api/tests/test_openapi_export.py`** — regenerates in-memory and fails CI if the committed export drifts from the app. Fix is always `make openapi`. **Semantic compare** (parses both sides) so it catches every real route/schema drift but is robust to YAML-formatting differences across environments — no CI byte-brittleness.
- The hand-authored sketch is retained as the human-facing reference (with a header note pointing to the generated file). Replacing the sketch entirely remains a future ADR-lite decision.

This guard would have caught all 4 previously-missing routes at the PR that introduced them.

## Verification
- Generator output verified **deterministic** (two runs byte-identical).
- New drift-guard passes; `test_openapi` still green (no route change).
- `ruff` + `ruff format` + `mypy app` green from repo root.

Not security-gated (no gateway/auth/audit/crypto/workflow changes) → self-merge after CI.

Refs DE-373

entries.title: #254: feat(WS-E PR2a): SEC EDGAR authority source + chat authority wiring generalization
entries.text:
feat(WS-E PR2a): SEC EDGAR authority source + chat authority wiring generalization ## WS-E PR2a — SEC EDGAR authority source (+ chat authority wiring generalization)

Adds **SEC EDGAR** as the second free authoritative research source (GovInfo was the first), on the existing ADR-0021 registry + `retrieve_authority` + mirror-the-caselaw verify path. EDGAR company filings (10-K, 8-K, S-1, …) become **searchable and quote-verifiable in both autonomous matter sessions and chat**. Reuses the entire PR1a/PR1b/PR1c substrate unchanged. **No new ADR** (ADR 0021 D6 scopes EDGAR+EUR-Lex), **no migration**, `gate.py`/`ledger.py`/`alembic` untouched.

This is **PR2a** of a two-source split; **PR2b** adds EUR-Lex on the now-generic base (near-zero chat wiring).

### What ships
- **Gateway `EdgarToolAdapter`** — `search_authority` (EDGAR full-text search, `efts.sec.gov`) + `get_authority` (filing retrieval, `www.sec.gov/Archives`). **Auth = a descriptive `User-Agent`, no API key** (SEC fair-access); SSRF-guarded to `[efts.sec.gov, www.sec.gov]`; `skip_anonymization=True` on public filing text; `read_only=True`. Live API shapes verified during implementation.
- **Backend `EdgarAdapter` + `SOURCE_REGISTRY["edgar"]`** — normalizes payloads into `FetchedAuthority`; single content kind `sec_filing`.
- **Chat authority wiring generalized to registry-driven** — `search_authority`/`get_authority` now take a `source` argument (enum built from enabled authority sources), replacing the GovInfo-hardcoded schemas + `_dispatch_authority` adapter. **Behavior-preserving for GovInfo** (`source:"govinfo"`); pre-existing GovInfo chat tests are append-only.
- **`sec_filing` added to `_VERIFIABLE_CONTENT_KINDS`** (DE-371) so EDGAR quotes verify + ledger; autonomous `content_kind` carried through (absent-kind fallback is now an honest `"unknown"`, not a false `"statute"`).
- `gateway.yaml.example` EDGAR provider block; PRD WS-E status + DE-371 update.

### Security-reviewer notes (this is why it's security-gated)
- **New egress adapter** on the gateway boundary: every EDGAR request runs `validate_egress_target(url, allowlist)`; `external_ref` is `{cik}_{accession}_{document}` — `[A-Za-z0-9._-]`-only (no `/`, no path traversal into the `authority/{source}/{ref}` cache key).
- **Touches shipped chat authority code** (`tool_schemas.py`, `tool_loop.py`) — the GovInfo→registry generalization. Empty-search now degrades to a "no results" observation instead of raising and breaking the turn.
- **Honesty chain intact:** a quote verifies only on a real verbatim/paraphrase match against the cached body; unmatched → dropped, never asserted; no false PASS/SUPPORTED.
- `gate.py`/`ledger.py`/`alembic` provably untouched (`git diff --name-only main..HEAD`).

### Verification
- **DB-backed full SOLO api suite:** `2575 passed, 1 skipped` (14:18, `DATABASE_URL` set). **Gateway suite:** 728 passed/3 skip. Static: `ruff` + `ruff format` + `mypy app` (api) and `mypy --strict` (gateway) all green from root.
- **Built via subagent-driven development** (5 tasks, per-task spec+quality review) + **Opus whole-branch review = Ready to merge** (0 Critical, 0 Important). One Minor fixed in-branch (guard `int(cik)` in EDGAR search); five Minors triaged defer-safe (two folded into PR2b: an explicit no-results sentinel + reordering the unknown-source check ahead of the audit write).
- No new route → `test_openapi`/`test_endpoints` path counts unchanged.

### Also included (from `main`, via merge)
DE-373 (generated OpenAPI export + drift-guard) and the 4-route OpenAPI sketch sync — merged in from `main`, shown here only because the branch was synced.

### Merge handling
Security-gated (gateway egress + citation surface + shipped chat code) → **Kevin/security merges, no self-merge**; I mirror `origin`→`tucuxi` after.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

entries.title: #253: docs(api): sync backend OpenAPI sketch — add 4 drifted routes (+ file DE-373)
entries.text:
docs(api): sync backend OpenAPI sketch — add 4 drifted routes (+ file DE-373) The committed `docs/api/backend-openapi.yaml` is a **hand-authored sketch** with no test guarding it against the live app, so it silently drifted. At pin `3659360` it was missing **4 shipped routes** (found via a full path diff vs the app's pinned `EXPECTED_PATHS`):

| Route | Shipped in |
|---|---|
| `GET /api/v1/research/sources` | WS-E PR1a |
| `GET /api/v1/autonomous/sessions/{session_id}/ledger` | WS-D PR2 |
| `POST /api/v1/projects/{project_id}/knowledge-bases` | Wave D |
| `DELETE /api/v1/projects/{project_id}/knowledge-bases/{kb_id}` | Wave D |

(The last two were surfaced by the "check for others" sweep the downstream Donna BFF asked for.)

## What this PR does
- Adds all 4 paths to the sketch, deriving exact shapes from the real handlers/Pydantic models:
  - `/research/sources` → new `SourcesResponse`/`AvailableSourceResponse` components (P3-safe fields; no keys/cost).
  - `/autonomous/sessions/{id}/ledger` → reuses the chat-ledger `LedgerEntry` + gates shape (identical response).
  - KB attach → `200 + Project` body (idempotent, verified against code — **not** 204/409); KB detach → `204`.
  - Also adds the missing `attached_knowledge_base_ids` field to the `Project` schema (in code since Wave D, absent from the sketch).
- Sketch path count now **137/137** vs the live app (0 missing, 0 extra). `test_openapi.py` still green.
- **Files DE-373** (PRD §9): the durable fix — a code-generated OpenAPI export + a CI drift-guard test so this can never silently drift again. Recommended posture: generate alongside the curated sketch.

Docs-only; not security-gated. No code or test changes.

Refs DE-373

entries.title: #252: docs(integration): Donna fiduciary-grade auditability API reference
entries.text:
docs(integration): Donna fiduciary-grade auditability API reference Integration reference for **Donna CC** (downstream SvelteKit BFF consuming lq-ai via the published API + pinned submodule), produced in response to their auditability/features integration request.

## What it covers
- Capability inventory of the fiduciary-grade auditability surface (ledger, gate, provenance, caselaw + authority citations, treatment layer, research sources, autonomous session ledger/receipt).
- The API contract: exact endpoint shapes, **real example payloads** (reconstructed from integration-test assertions), pagination/ordering, auth/RBAC, and a loosely-typed-field callout list.
- Semantics & guarantees: the integrity model, provenance-chain shape, ordering/time, export, retention/redaction.
- Delivery mechanics (SSE + the poll-for-treatment model) and ops (migrations 0057–0064, config keys, pin).

## Honest posture (per CLAUDE.md conservative-posture rule)
The doc does **not** overclaim. It states plainly where guarantees do **not** exist:
- **No hash-chaining / signatures / HMAC** on the audit trail today (only an unchained per-message `content_hash`; the Merkle layer is "future M2+"). → build an *honest provenance* UI, not a *tamper-proof* one.
- Client-facing field is **`gate_status`** (not `verdict`); `total_assertions == 0` also yields `fiduciary_grade` and must **not** render as "verified."
- The ledger/sources endpoints have **no `response_model`** (gen:api won't type them).
- **No dedicated ledger export** and **no auditor/compliance role** (owner-scoped only).
- **Treatment** is the one eventually-consistent field (poll `/ledger`).

Docs-only; not security-gated. Every claim is verified against source at `file:line`.

Refs DE-369

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

entries.title: #251: feat(WS-E PR1c): chat authority consumer — verified quotes at finalize (DE-369 chat path)
entries.text:
feat(WS-E PR1c): chat authority consumer — verified quotes at finalize (DE-369 chat path) ## WS-E PR1c — chat authority consumer (DE-369 chat path)

Completes the DE-369 chat path: chat sessions can now call granular authority tools, their sources become first-class provenance, and quoted authority text is **verified against the cached body at finalize** before any ledger row is written. Reuses the entire PR1b substrate (`message_authority_citations`, the object-storage `authority_text_cache` with 30-day TTL, the 4th ledger FK). No new migration, no new route, `gate.py` and `ledger.py` untouched.

### What ships
- **Authority tools in the chat tool-loop** — `search_authority` / `get_authority` exposed as chat tools, gated by the same registry allowlist as the autonomous path (`tool_schemas.py`, `tool_loop.py`).
- **Governed dispatch + cache write** — `_dispatch_authority` routes through the governed tool-loop and writes the fetched body into `authority_text_cache` (`tool_loop.py`, `chats.py`).
- **Provenance** — `collect_tool_sources` emits `MessageToolSource` records for authority calls so the UI/ledger see them as sources.
- **Finalize verification (both sites)** — `verify_and_persist_authority_citations` runs at chat finalize:
  - **Pass 1:** verbatim quote match against the cached body → `VERIFIED`.
  - **Pass 2 (SUPPORTED tier):** whole-body paraphrase judge (`authority_content_judge.py`) → `SUPPORTED` on judge yes/partial.
  - No verified/supported row is ever written without a real verbatim match or a judge yes/partial. Conservative by construction — an unmatched quote is dropped, never asserted.

### Honesty / conservative posture
- No false `VERIFIED`, no false `SUPPORTED` — traced end-to-end in the whole-branch review.
- Content kinds outside `_VERIFIABLE_CONTENT_KINDS = {statute, regulation}` are silently **not** verified (no ledger row) rather than asserted — a conservative drop. The filter carries a comment that this set must grow for WS-E PR2 sources (SEC filings, EUR-Lex); tracked as DE-371.

### Scope guards (unchanged surfaces)
- `gate.py`, `ledger.py`, and `alembic/` are provably untouched (`git diff --name-only 96a7784..HEAD` shows none).
- No new route, no new migration. Reuses PR1b's mig 0064 substrate.

### Deferred (filed this PR)
- **DE-370** — attributed-authority FAIL surfacing (chat, B1c-analog).
- **DE-371** — autonomous-path SUPPORTED tier + drop the dead `gateway=` Stage-3 arg in PR1b `build_authority_citations`.
- **DE-372** — HTTP-level finalize integration fixture covering caselaw + authority wiring.

### Verification
- **DB-backed full SOLO api suite:** `2560 passed, 1 skipped in 14:12` (`DATABASE_URL` set → integration/authority/caselaw tests actually run; not a skipped-hollow-green).
- **Static gates:** `ruff check` + `ruff format` + `mypy` clean on touched files (CI scope from root).
- **Opus whole-branch review:** Ready to merge — 0 Critical, 0 Important, 5 Minor (all deferred/captured). The two zero-risk nits it flagged are applied in `a8e9f56`.

### Review routing
Touches citation/authority verification and the governed chat tool-loop → security-gated. Requesting Kevin/security review; no self-merge.
