entries.title: #246: WS-E PR1b — fetched-authority verification + ledger-backing (DE-369)
entries.text:
WS-E PR1b — fetched-authority verification + ledger-backing (DE-369) ## WS-E PR1b — Fetched-authority verification + ledger-backing

Makes a fetched-authority quote (a GovInfo statute/reg passage the autonomous matter loop fetched via PR1a's `retrieve_authority`) a **char-fidelity-verified, ledger-backed, gate-counted** citation — closing **DE-369**. Realizes **ADR 0021 D3** (mirror-the-caselaw-path). **No gateway/egress change; `gate.py` unchanged.**

Spec/plan: `docs/superpowers/{specs,plans}/2026-06-30-wse-pr1b-authority-verification*`.

### What landed (mirrors the caselaw path throughout)
- **Migration 0064:** `message_authority_citations` (mirrors `message_caselaw_citations`; `source_type`/`external_ref`/`content_kind` replace the int ids) + `authority_text_cache` (object-storage body + metadata, 30-day TTL) + a 4th `citation_ledger_entry` FK slot (exactly-one CHECK extended to 4 terms via drop+recreate).
- **`citation/authority.py`:** in-memory `authority_target` (uuid5, duck-types the verifier protocols — no `Document` row) + a durable text cache (`store_/load_authority_text`) reusing `app.storage`; **imports** `verify()` + `locate_passage` (no re-implementation). Path-traversal-hardened storage key (`[A-Za-z0-9._-]+`) + `begin_nested` concurrent-safe upsert.
- **`citation/ledger.py`:** a 4th (authority) branch in `assemble_ledger_entries` + `resolve_ledger_entries` — `source_kind = content_kind` (statute/regulation), `provider = source_type` (govinfo). No N+1.
- **Autonomous fetch (`guard.py`):** PR1a's `_handle_retrieve_authority` now writes the fetched body to the cache (savepoint-isolated, non-fatal) + threads `source`/`content_kind` onto the `EvidenceItem`.
- **Autonomous delivery (`ledger_bridge.py`):** `build_authority_citations` verifies each finding's authority quote against the cache-loaded body (carried-content fallback) → `MessageAuthorityCitation` rows → ledger → fiduciary gate. The previously-dropped `"authority"` split branch is wired.

### Load-bearing invariants (verified by the Opus whole-branch review)
- **Fiduciary-gate honesty chain — airtight:** a fabricated/no-locate authority quote → FAIL row → `verification_status="unverified"` → gate `flagged`; a verbatim quote → verified → PASS. No path lets a fabricated statute quote silently PASS or get dropped. (No-locate → FAIL, not drop, is the spec's deliberate conservative posture — statutes are quoted verbatim.)
- **Never-poison-the-session:** the cache write is wrapped in `begin_nested()` at the guard call site (so `store_authority_text`'s bare SELECT/flush can't leave the session pending-rollback); `build_authority_citations` has a per-item guard and runs inside `delivery_node`'s SAVEPOINT. A Task-4 proving test fires a real `SELECT 1/0` and asserts the session stays usable.
- **P3:** the body lives in object storage (read at trace time); `authority_text_cache` is a content store **outside** `_AUDIT_MODELS`; citation rows carry only the cited passage + offsets.
- **Reuse-not-fork:** `verify()`, `locate_passage`, `assemble_ledger_entries`, `compute_and_record_gate` reused; **`gate.py` unchanged**.

### Scope
PR1b delivers the durable substrate + the **autonomous** path (the only path that fetches authority today), closing DE-369 for that path. The **chat** consumer — exposing `get_authority`/`search_authority` in the chat tool-loop + a chat-finalize verify hook, reusing this substrate — is **WS-E PR1c** (separate slice). DE-369's PRD entry is marked SHIPPED (autonomous) with PR1c noted for chat.

### Tests & gates
- Full api suite **2533 passed / 1 skip** (SOLO, DE-368); api ruff/format/mypy clean. One migration (0064).
- Built via `superpowers:subagent-driven-development` (5 tasks, per-task implement→review→fix) + an Opus whole-branch review that caught the cross-task blocker green per-task suites missed (a stale `_resolve_source` test call after the signature grew a 4th FK arg — outside the per-task glob + not caught by `mypy app`), plus two Task-2 hardenings (path-traversal, concurrent upsert) and the Task-4 session-poison fix.

### ⚠️ Security-gated — held for security/maintainer merge (no self-merge)
Touches `api/app/autonomous/**`, `api/app/citation/**`, and a migration. Per CODEOWNERS this routes to security review. **Do not self-merge.** After merge: mirror `origin/main → tucuxi`.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

entries.title: #245: WS-E PR1a — content-source registry + GovInfo + DE-344 cost model
entries.text:
WS-E PR1a — content-source registry + GovInfo + DE-344 cost model ## WS-E PR1a — Content-source registry + GovInfo + DE-344 cost model

Lets the **autonomous legal-matter loop reach GovInfo** (US federal statutes/regs: USCODE + CFR) through the governed gateway egress via a new closed-set `retrieve_authority` tool-intent, validated against a backend **content-source registry**, with a real **DE-344 per-provider cost model** so R4 throttles external tools. Fetched authority is captured at **provenance level** (`tool_call_log` audit + `ToolResult.data`); durable content storage + char-fidelity verification + ledger-backing is **PR1b** (tracked as **DE-369**, mig 0064). **No migration in this PR.**

Spec/plan/ADR: `docs/superpowers/{specs,plans}/2026-06-29-wse-pr1*`, `docs/adr/0021-content-source-registry-and-free-source-expansion.md` (D3 = mirror-the-caselaw-path).

### What landed
- **Gateway** (`gateway/app/providers/tool/govinfo.py`): new `govinfo` tool-provider adapter — `X-Api-Key` (DATA.GOV key style), SSRF via `validate_egress_target`, `skip_anonymization=True` (public statutory text reaches the verifier verbatim, ADR 0014 D5); `search_authority` + `get_authority` ops (live GovInfo `POST /search` + `GET /packages/{id}/summary` → `download.txtLink`). `gateway.yaml.example` documents the entry + the optional `cost_per_call`.
- **Backend registry** (`api/app/research/{registry,adapters}.py`): `SOURCE_REGISTRY` (courtlistener + govinfo), `resolve_available_sources` = operator-enabled ∩ adapter-shipped, `GovInfoAdapter.from_response` → `FetchedAuthority`.
- **Endpoint**: `GET /api/v1/research/sources` — P3-safe (name/type/jurisdiction/coverage/content_kinds/enabled/egress_tier only; never auth/cost).
- **Autonomous** (`enums/guard/planner/nodes/cost.py`): `ToolIntent.retrieve_authority` (granted in `analysis` only), governed dispatch through the existing chokepoint, planner allowlist + structural arg-validation + `kind="authority"` evidence + minimal source-awareness in planner messages.
- **DE-344** (`tools/governance.py` + `autonomous/cost.py`): per-provider `cost_per_call`/`cost_per_unit` cached (rides `extra="allow"` model_extra — no schema field/migration); `estimate_tool_cost` projects the configured rate at R4 pre-flight; all three external intents (`retrieve_caselaw`, `retrieve_authority`, `call_mcp_tool`) record realized cost on `tool_call_log` + accumulate onto `session.cost_total_usd`.

### Load-bearing invariants (verified clean by the Opus whole-branch review)
- **One egress (ADR 0014)** — backend reaches GovInfo only via `gateway.call_tool`; the `get_authority` txtLink follow discards the upstream host and re-validates the path against the allowlist (no SSRF redirect).
- **Never poison the session (WS-D PR1-C1 lesson)** — bad/disabled/unknown source/op/args → clean `ValueError` → non-fatal failed observation; bad source = 0 audit rows, bad op = 1 `outcome="error"` row, session stays `running`. The new `resolve_available_sources` call in `_run_analysis_loop` is non-fatal (gateway-down → empty list, loop continues).
- **Byte-identical legacy path** — all new behavior is gated to the `if query:` agentic matter path; query-less sessions are unchanged.
- **P3 (ADR 0016)** — no secrets/raw-payload in `/sources`, planner context, or any audit/log surface.

### Provenance decision (maintainer-approved 2026-06-29)
The plan called for a `MessageToolSource` provenance row, but that model's `message_id` is `NOT NULL FK → messages.id` and the autonomous executor has no message (the sibling `retrieve_caselaw`/`call_mcp_tool` handlers write none either — it's a chat-path/DE-350 mechanism). Provenance is captured via `tool_call_log` + `ToolResult.data`; **durable content provenance is sequenced to PR1b as a tracked mandate (DE-369, four acceptance criteria) — not dropped.**

### Tests & gates
- api full suite **2512 passed / 1 skip** (SOLO, DE-368); gateway **715 passed / 3 skip**; api+gateway ruff/format/mypy(`--strict` gateway) all clean. No migration.
- Built via `superpowers:subagent-driven-development` (per-task implement→review→fix) + an Opus whole-branch review that caught 2 production-blocking defects the green suites masked (gateway-envelope unwrap; matter-session abort on gateway hiccup) + the `egress_tier`-always-null cross-task bug — all fixed.

### ⚠️ Security-gated — held for security/maintainer merge (no self-merge)
Touches `gateway/**`, `api/app/autonomous/**`, `api/app/tools/governance.py`. Per CODEOWNERS this routes to security review. **Do not self-merge.** After merge: mirror `origin/main → tucuxi`.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

entries.title: #242: docs(WS-E): PR1 spec + PR1a plan + ADR 0021 D3 refinement
entries.text:
docs(WS-E): PR1 spec + PR1a plan + ADR 0021 D3 refinement ## WS-E PR1 — planning docs (registry + GovInfo + DE-344)

**Docs-only.** Lands the WS-E PR1 spec + the PR1a implementation plan ahead of the code build, plus a small ADR 0021 D3 mechanism refinement and the session handoff. Building on accepted ADR 0021 (#241). The PR1a **code** ships as its own security-gated PR (built next session, fresh branch off this once merged).

### Contents
- **`docs/adr/0021-...` — D3 refinement note (2026-06-29, maintainer).** On mapping the code, D3's literal "ephemeral `Document` → `MessageCitation`" collides with `MessageCitation.source_file_id` being NOT NULL (would force ephemeral-`File`/storage machinery). Decision: realize D3 by **mirroring the existing caselaw path** — an in-memory verification target (like `caselaw.opinion_target`) + one `message_authority_citations` table + a ledger FK slot + a `(source_type, external_ref)→text` TTL cache. Preserves D3's intent (reuse the shared `verify()` core; one ledger; one gate; reuse-not-fork) without the `File`/storage surface.
- **`docs/superpowers/specs/2026-06-29-wse-pr1-registry-govinfo-design.md`** — the WS-E PR1 design (C1–C6): gateway GovInfo adapter, backend content-source registry + `GET /research/sources`, `retrieve_authority` closed-set intent (registry-validated args), DE-344 configured cost model, and the mech-B authority verify+ledger. Flags a **PR1a/PR1b cut point**.
- **`docs/superpowers/plans/2026-06-29-wse-pr1a-registry-govinfo.md`** — the PR1a plan (7 TDD tasks, C1–C5: registry + GovInfo + `retrieve_authority` + DE-344 cost, provenance-level, **no migration**). PR1b (C6 verify+ledger, mech-B, migration 0064) gets its own plan after PR1a lands.
- **`docs/LQVern/HANDOFF-2026-06-29-wse-pr1a.md`** — session handoff (leads with a session-start note); build PR1a code on a fresh branch off this once merged.

### Decisions locked with the maintainer (2026-06-29)
Subagent-driven build; **USCODE + CFR** for GovInfo PR1a; **PR1a/PR1b split**; D3 = mirror-the-caselaw-path.

No code, no migration, no API change in this PR.

entries.title: #241: ADR 0021 — content-source registry & free-source expansion (WS-E)
entries.text:
ADR 0021 — content-source registry & free-source expansion (WS-E) ## ADR 0021 — Content-source registry & free-source expansion (WS-E)

**Docs-only — for maintainer review + acceptance** (the milestone's ADR-acceptance gate; the four design forks were settled with the maintainer 2026-06-29). Status flips `Proposed → Accepted` on merge. **Not self-merging** — acceptance is the maintainer's call.

WS-E is the milestone's "authoritative content" workstream: a registry of authoritative sources the agentic planner can reason over, plus free-source expansion (GovInfo / SEC EDGAR / EUR-Lex on the ADR-0014 tool-provider class), plus the real DE-344 external-tool cost model. LQ.AI ships the *registry + adapters + cost model + free connectors* — never a curated corpus or anyone's licensed content (that stays a BYO-MCP path).

### The seven decisions
- **D1 — Hybrid registry.** `gateway.yaml` holds egress (type/auth/base_url/tier/allowlist/rate-limit + new cost fields, ADR 0014); a backend `api/app/research/registry.py` (keyed by provider type) holds content semantics (jurisdiction, coverage, content-type, op set, adapter, `enabled`). The live registry is the **intersection** of operator-configured-in-gateway ∩ backend-ships-an-adapter. `GET /api/v1/research/sources` exposes the join (never auth/cost secrets).
- **D2 — `retrieve_authority(source, op, args)`.** One new closed-set `ToolIntent`, args model-generated/handler-validated against the registry (the ADR-0015 boundary; WS-D's `validate_action_args` pattern), added to the planner allowlist. Bad/disabled source/op → non-fatal failed observation (the PR1-C1 lesson). `retrieve_caselaw` stays for the WS-G treatment/citation-graph path.
- **D3 — Ephemeral-document pipeline (reuse the KB verifier).** Per PRD §3.6: fetched authority → ingested `Document` → the **existing** KB character-fidelity cascade → `MessageCitation` → ledger + fiduciary gate. Session/matter-scoped (excluded from user KBs, like WS-D PR2's hidden chat), TTL-cached by `(source_type, external_ref)`, always provenance-tracked (`MessageToolSource`). One verifier; reuse-not-fork. CourtListener stays on its dedicated caselaw cache (it feeds the WS-G graph).
- **D4 — DE-344 cost model = configured rate.** `cost_per_call`/`cost_per_unit` on the `tool_providers` entry; `estimate_tool_cost` returns it (free sources omit → $0 → R4 a no-op for them); realized cost on `tool_call_log.cost_usd` (plumbing already exists). R4 becomes real for external tools by construction.
- **D5 — Honest unavailability + conservative coverage** (anti-overclaiming; a not-configured source is surfaced unavailable-with-reason, never silently dropped or implied as comprehensive coverage).
- **D6 — Phasing.** PR1 = registry + `retrieve_authority` + ephemeral-verify + cost model + **GovInfo** (US federal statutes/regs). PR2 = **SEC EDGAR** + **EUR-Lex**, flagged (≥2 new free sources behind flags).
- **D7 — Reuse, never bypass** the governance + output rails (no second egress, ledger, verifier, or governance path).

### Open questions (deliberately left as WS-E PR-spec inputs)
Ephemeral-document model/TTL; the adapter contract; GovInfo's op surface (USC/CFR/Fed-Register/bills); the `retrieve_authority` arg-validation extension; realized-cost parsing; how much registry metadata to inject into the planner prompt (P3 budget).

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

entries.title: #240: WS-D PR2 — fiduciary ledger + gate for matter sessions (Phase 2)
entries.text:
WS-D PR2 — fiduciary ledger + gate for matter sessions (Phase 2) ## WS-D PR2 — Fiduciary ledger + gate for matter sessions

Makes an autonomous **matter session** produce the *same* citation-ledger (`citation_ledger_entry`) + fiduciary-gate (`work_product_fiduciary_gate`) rows a chat turn produces — so a reviewer traces a session's citations exactly as they trace a chat turn's, and the work product is honestly labeled `fiduciary_grade` / `supported_only` / `flagged`. ADR [0020](docs/adr/0020-governed-agentic-legal-matter-sessions.md) D6. **Reuse, do not fork** (ADR 0016 P6): the session writes the same rows and calls `verify()` / `assemble_ledger_entries` / `compute_and_record_gate` unchanged.

Builds on WS-D PR1 (#239, the governed agentic loop). PR1 produced findings + a plan trace; PR2 makes them ledger-backed and gate-labeled.

### How it works
1. **Hidden session-owned chat (migration 0063):** `chats.autonomous_session_id` FK → `autonomous_sessions.id ON DELETE SET NULL`. At delivery, a matter session manufactures a `Chat`+`Message` carrying the work product, marked session-owned so it's **excluded from `list_chats`/`search_chats`** (reachable only by the read endpoint). This gives the cascade the `message_id → chat_id` identity it requires, with zero schema fork of the ledger/gate.
2. **Loop evidence registry (P3-safe):** the analysis loop accumulates a numbered registry of the authority it gathered (KB chunks + caselaw). The **planner** still sees only compact observations; the **synthesis** additionally receives the numbered evidence so it can quote-and-cite. Raw chunk/opinion content never enters the trace, audit, or receipt.
3. **Structured per-finding citations:** the synthesis emits `citations: [{quote, source}]` per finding, tagged to the evidence numbers.
4. **Delivery cascade:** for each citation, the existing **character-fidelity verifier** (`verify()`) checks the quoted span against the authoritative source (KB chunk / cached opinion) and persists a `MessageCitation`/`MessageCaselawCitation` **only when verified** — unverifiable quotes are dropped, never fabricated. Then the unchanged `assemble_ledger_entries` + `compute_and_record_gate` run, and the gate verdict is embedded in `session.result["fiduciary_gate"]` alongside PR1's `plan_trace`.
5. **Read endpoint:** `GET /api/v1/autonomous/sessions/{id}/ledger` → owner-scoped (404 for missing/non-owned), returns the **identical** `{chat_id, entries, gates}` shape as the chat ledger endpoint (so the PR2-UI follow-up reuses the chat ledger component directly).

### Load-bearing invariants (all test-pinned)
- **Reuse-not-fork:** same rows, verifier/assemble/gate called unchanged.
- **Strictly additive:** query-less / non-matter sessions are byte-identical — no manufactured chat, no ledger/gate, `session.result` unchanged.
- **Robustness (the PR1-C1 lesson):** the delivery-time bridge is **SAVEPOINT-isolated** (`db.begin_nested()`); a flush failure inside it rolls back only the bridge's writes and the terminal commit still succeeds — **no zombie running session**. A dedicated regression test injects a partial-write-then-raise bridge stub and asserts the session still reaches `completed` with no orphaned chat.
- **Honest labeling (ADR 0018 D3):** a citation row is persisted only when `verify()` confirms it; the gate reflects only verified assertions.
- **P3:** evidence `content` is synthesis-context only; ledger rows store offsets/labels/ids.
- **Security/authz:** read endpoint 404s (no existence leak) for non-owned sessions.

### Review trail (subagent-driven development)
8 TDD tasks, each implemented → spec-review → quality-review clean (Task 8 had one fix wave: response-shape harmonization + a cross-user 404 test). The **Opus whole-branch review** then found no Critical/Important *code* defect — savepoint isolation, evidence numbering (no fidelity false-positive), P3, honest-labeling, hidden-chat filter, and reuse-not-fork all verified — and one Important **test gap** (no regression test on the PR1-C1 savepoint surface), now fixed, plus two trivial hardenings (read-endpoint `.first()`; `raise` over `return None` on an unreachable gate-None path). A focused re-review confirmed the fixes and adjudicated a `SAWarning` on the new test as a **test-harness artifact** (the conftest's double-SAVEPOINT nesting; production has no outer harness savepoint) — proven non-actionable by the passing orphan-chat assertion.

### Test evidence
- Solo full api suite: **2460 passed, 1 skipped, 0 failed**. (Per DE-368, the suite is serial-only against the shared test DB; concurrent pytest runs produce spurious research/MCP/gateway failures — those are not real.)
- `ruff check`/`ruff format --check` (`api scripts`) + whole-app `mypy` clean. Gateway untouched.
- New tests: hidden-chat exclusion; `locate_in_chunk`; evidence registry; structured-citation parse; KB + caselaw structured-citation builders (verified/unverifiable/empty-quote); `build_session_ledger` (happy path + no-citations skip); the savepoint-isolation regression; the read endpoint (happy / no-ledger 404 / cross-user 404).

### Deferred / follow-ups (not blockers)
- **PR2-UI** (separate, self-merge after CI): the Svelte session-ledger view, reusing the chat ledger component.
- **DE-369 (product call):** `user_export` includes session-owned chats in the GDPR export ZIP — arguably correct (it's the user's own data; "hidden" was about the chat-list UI), surfaced for a decision.
- **Shared-gate semantics (note, not a PR2 fork):** a work product where every citation is unverifiable yields zero assertions → `fiduciary_grade` ("nothing checkable → top grade"). This is inherited from the shared gate and affects the chat path identically; flagging for a possible shared-gate DE.
- Cosmetic minors logged (db-schema partial-index doc line; backend-openapi.yaml sketch one path behind — `test_openapi.py` is the authoritative conformance check and is green).

### Scope deferred to later WS-D / WS-E
MCP source provenance in the ledger (WS-F, Phase 3); `treatment_id` linkage (WS-G; column stays null); metered-source cost (DE-344, WS-E).

🔒 **Security-gated** (migration + `api/app/autonomous/**` + `api/app/citation/**` + `chats.py` + a new authed endpoint) — routed to security review. **Do not self-merge.**

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

entries.title: #239: WS-D PR1 — governed agentic loop + matter intake (Phase 2)
entries.text:
WS-D PR1 — governed agentic loop + matter intake (Phase 2) ## WS-D PR1 — Governed agentic loop + matter intake

Turns the autonomous `analysis` phase from a single `run_skill` inference into a governed **plan → act → observe → replan** loop **for matter-scoped sessions only**, while leaving query-less sessions (cron/watch/schedule) byte-identical. The planner picks the next observe-intent each step (all through `guarded_tool_call`), observations fold back compactly, and a final `run_skill` synthesis produces the structured findings the rest of the graph already consumes. ADR [0020](docs/adr/0020-governed-agentic-legal-matter-sessions.md). **No migration.**

### What changed
- **`ToolIntent.plan`** — closed-set planner-decision intent (a gateway inference), granted **only** in `PHASE_GRANTS[analysis]`, costed by R4 as inference. No DB CHECK → no migration.
- **`planner.py`** — `PLANNER_ALLOWLIST` (observe intents only), `PlannerDecision`, `build_planner_messages`, `parse_planner_decision` (parse-or-`None`, never raises), `summarize_observation` (P3-clean), and `validate_action_args` (closed-set arg boundary, see C1).
- **`prompts.assemble_synthesis_messages`** — reuses `assemble_analysis_messages` so the fenced-JSON `findings` contract the drafting node parses is byte-identical.
- **`nodes.py`** — the loop + backward-compat gate in `make_analysis_node` (`_run_analysis_loop`); the receipt plan-trace merge in the delivery node.
- **`state.py` / `config.py`** — `analysis_plan_trace` state field; `DEFAULT_MAX_ANALYSIS_STEPS = 6`.

### Load-bearing invariants (all test-pinned)
1. **Backward-compat:** the loop engages only when `state["query"]` is non-empty; query-less sessions take the unchanged single-call path — no `plan` intent, no `analysis_plan_trace` key.
2. **Synthesis contract:** however the loop ends (`planner_done`/`step_cap`/`planner_unparseable`), a final synthesis always produces the structured findings as `analysis_content` — partial-but-honest on a cap-halt, never fabricated-complete, never a crash.
3. **Every step via `guarded_tool_call`** (R5→R6→R4); no bypass; no new brake machinery; bounded by `max_analysis_steps` + R4's cost cap.
4. **Planner allowlist = observe intents only** (`retrieve_chunks`/`retrieve_caselaw`/`call_mcp_tool`); `run_skill`/`run_playbook` reserved for synthesis.
5. **Args model-generated, boundary-validated** (ADR 0015); P3: trace + observations hold counts/ids/case-names/short snippets/rationale only — never full payloads.

### Review trail (subagent-driven development)
6 TDD tasks, each implemented → spec-review → quality-review clean. The Opus whole-branch review then found a **Critical (C1)** + I1 + M1; all fixed and re-reviewed clean:

- **C1 (Critical, fixed):** a model-generated planner arg (e.g. `retrieve_chunks {top_k: -1}`) faulted at the SQL layer (`hybrid_search` doesn't clamp `top_k` → negative `LIMIT` → `DBAPIError`); the loop's catch swallowed it without rollback, poisoning the `AsyncSession` so the next call/synthesis raised `PendingRollbackError`, and the executor's failure-handler `commit()` (no prior `rollback()`) left a zombie `running` session. **Fix (maintainer-approved approach):** (a) `validate_action_args` rejects SQL-reaching bad args before dispatch so they degrade to a clean failed observation (no poisoning); (b) the executor's generic `except` now `rollback()`s before persisting `failed` (defense-in-depth). No savepoints.
- **I1 (fixed):** playbook matter-sessions now synthesize/audit under `run_playbook`, not `run_skill`.
- **M1 (fixed):** `assert next_intent` replaced with an `-O`-safe guard that keeps synthesis reachable.

### Test evidence
- Solo full api suite: **2436 passed, 1 skipped, 0 failed**. Autonomous suite: **522 passed**. `ruff check`/`ruff format --check` (`api scripts`) + whole-app `mypy` clean. Gateway untouched (0 files).
- New tests: agentic loop (planner→done→synthesis, step-cap, unparseable, bad-arg-nonfatal), `validate_action_args` unit matrix, observation summarizer, synthesis messages, plan-intent grant/cost, receipt plan-trace, executor rollback regression.

### Notes
- **DE-368** filed: the test suite is serial-only against one shared Postgres (CI runs it serially → green); flagged for any future `pytest-xdist` parallelization (needs per-worker DBs).
- Residual minor (logged, backstopped by C1b): `validate_action_args` doesn't dimension-check `query_embedding` — a wrong-dim vector would fault at pgvector but the executor rollback degrades it cleanly (no zombie). Low-probability (a text planner rarely emits raw vectors).

### Scope deferred to WS-D PR2
WS-A ledger + WS-B gate integration (reusing the chat-path `assemble_ledger_entries` + `compute_and_record_gate`). PR1 adds no ledger/gate.

🔒 **Security-gated** (`api/app/autonomous/**`, `guard.py` dispatch, `executor.py`) — routed to security review. **Do not self-merge.**

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

entries.title: #238: docs(adr): ADR 0020 — governed agentic legal-matter sessions (WS-D, Phase 2)
entries.text:
docs(adr): ADR 0020 — governed agentic legal-matter sessions (WS-D, Phase 2) ## ADR 0020 — Governed agentic legal-matter sessions (WS-D)

**Accepted** (maintainer-accepted 2026-06-28). Docs-only; not security-gated. Opens Phase 2's WS-D workstream.

Pins the methodology for "describe a matter in plain language → an agentic session that plans, retrieves, adapts, and produces a fiduciary-grade work product," reconciling genuine agency with ADR 0015's closed-set governance:

- **D1** — a governed `plan→act→observe→replan` loop **inside** the existing `guarded_tool_call` chokepoint; the planner picks the next `ToolIntent` from the phase's `PHASE_GRANTS` allowlist (never open function-calling).
- **D2** — scripted phase backbone; agency confined to the **analysis** phase so `ethics_review` / `delivery` stay deterministic (a model never decides it's "done" or self-delivers).
- **D3** — plain-language matter intake (the reserved `query` seam made real); **"matter" = project**, no new model.
- **D4** — bounded by a new per-phase **step cap** + the existing **R4 $5 budget**; **no new brake machinery**.
- **D5** — the plan is transparent, auditable work product (every planner choice + rationale → audit log + receipt).
- **D6** — phased build: **PR1** = governed loop + intake (findings/artifacts, no ledger/gate); **PR2** = WS-A ledger + WS-B gate integration **reusing** the chat-path cascade (ADR 0016 P6, not a fork).
- **D7** — reuse, never bypass, the governance + output rails.

§Open questions are the PR1/PR2 spec inputs. Grounded in a 2026-06-28 verification pass of `api/app/autonomous/` (the governance + brakes + audit are reusable; the agency, the matter intake, and the ledger/gate hookup are net-new).

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

entries.title: #237: fix: WS-G PR3 — treatment hardening (DE-364 + DE-363), Phase 2
entries.text:
fix: WS-G PR3 — treatment hardening (DE-364 + DE-363), Phase 2 ## WS-G PR3 — treatment hardening

Closes the two committed WS-G follow-ups before Phase 2 closes. Builds on PR1 (#233) + PR2 (#236). **No migration.**

**🔒 Security-gated** (`api/app/citation/**` + `api/app/api/chats.py`) — security/maintainer merges; **not self-merged**. Mirror `origin/main → tucuxi` after.

### DE-364 — per-cluster SAVEPOINT isolation (concurrency)
`derive_treatment_for_message`'s per-cluster non-fatal guarantee now genuinely holds under concurrent same-case citation. The parent-INSERT path is wrapped in `begin_nested()`; on a `uq_citation_treatment_cluster_id` conflict it re-reads + **reuses** the concurrent winner's row (links the entry, skips this turn's judge pass) instead of letting an `IntegrityError` poison the session and lose the rest of a multi-cluster turn.

### DE-364b — refresh-path judge-write race (found by the Opus whole-branch review, fixed in-PR)
The Opus review surfaced a *sibling* race the insert-path fix didn't cover: two concurrent re-derivations of the same stale cluster collide on the signal unique constraint at the judge-pass flush. `_run_judge_pass` is now split into a **gateway-judge phase** (no DB writes) + a **tight SAVEPOINT persist phase** (atomic DELETE+INSERT → last-writer-wins) with an `IntegrityError` backstop that **restores the parent rollup to match the winner's signals** — so the branch can never commit a contradictory `derived_method='citation_graph'` parent beside judge signal rows (the invariant PR2 FIX 1 protects). The review also caught a production-critical async bug: reading an ORM attribute after `ROLLBACK TO SAVEPOINT` expires it → `MissingGreenlet` that silently skipped the restore; fixed by capturing the id before the savepoint.

### DE-363 — lazy-on-trace-open fallback
The async-only derivation's gaps no longer become permanent. `GET /chats/{id}/ledger` now best-effort re-enqueues `treatment_derivation_job` for any caselaw turn whose treatment is null or stale, via a **pure** `message_ids_needing_treatment` helper; the enqueue is coalesced by arq `_job_id=f"treatment:{message_id}"` so repeated trace-opens (and the finalize-path enqueue) don't storm the queue. **Re-enqueue only** — no synchronous egress on the read; the resolver stays pure; the response shape is unchanged (the derived signal appears on the next read).

### 👀 For the security reviewer
- **No new egress, no schema change, no migration.** Treatment still never gates the turn; P3 / derive-don't-assert / the persisted shape untouched.
- The two SAVEPOINTs wrap only the conflict-prone DB writes; gateway calls stay outside them.
- The lazy-enqueue block is `try/except`-guarded and can never fail the read.

### Built via SDD (5 tasks, per-task spec+quality reviews + Opus whole-branch review)
The Opus whole-branch review (run after Tasks 1–4) found the DE-364b refresh-path race → added Task 5, which then took two more Opus task-scoped reviews to reach APPROVED (skip-path contradiction, a test-tail-wags-dog deviation, and the `MissingGreenlet` bug all caught and fixed).

### Tests / gates
- api full suite **2392 passed / 1 skipped**; gateway **704 passed / 3 skipped**.
- api (CI scope `api scripts`) + gateway: `ruff check`, `ruff format --check`, whole-app `mypy` (`--strict` gateway) — all clean.
- New: DE-364 insert-conflict isolation + reuse (deterministic single-session staged-winner); DE-364b last-writer-wins consistency + conflict→skip→restore backstop (round-tripped parent assertions); `message_ids_needing_treatment` (null/fresh/stale/non-caselaw/scoped); `_job_id` dedup; GET /ledger enqueue (null → enqueue, fresh → skip, shape unchanged).

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

entries.title: #236: feat: WS-G PR2 — treatment-classifying judge (Phase 2)
entries.text:
feat: WS-G PR2 — treatment-classifying judge (Phase 2) ## WS-G PR2 — the treatment-classifying judge

Phase 2, WS-G (transparent validity/treatment layer). Builds on PR1's citation-graph signal (#233). Resolves ADR 0019 §Open-questions for PR2.

**🔒 Security-gated** (`gateway/**` + `api/app/citation/**`) — security/maintainer merges; **not self-merged**. Mirror `origin/main → tucuxi` after merge.

### What it does
For each case a turn cites, an LLM judge classifies how each citing opinion treats it — `followed / distinguished / criticized / questioned / overruled / superseded / neutral` — over a recency-capped (N=10), per-case-budget-bounded (\$0.25) subset, rolling up to a **strongest-negative** case-level signal on `citation_treatment`. Runs **off the turn's critical path** (PR1's arq job). **Treatment never gates the fiduciary verdict** (ADR 0018 D3 / 0019 D2).

### Design decisions (maintainer-approved; spec + plan in `docs/superpowers/`)
- **Snippet localization:** judges CourtListener's per-result `snippet` (no per-citing-opinion materialization egress) — ADR 0019 D4/D10 "phase the cost in."
- **P3 (load-bearing):** persists derived classification + confidence + the judge's **describe-don't-quote justification** + refs — **never the raw snippet or opinion text**. `_strip_snippet` keeps the persisted JSONB refs-only; the new child table has no snippet column and joins the P3 tripwire. Trace links out to CourtListener for the full passage.
- **Derive-don't-assert (D1):** no good/bad-law verdict; strongest-negative + per-class counts + per-signal confidence/reasoning. Judge prompt prefers `neutral` on uncertainty.
- **Schema (mig 0062):** new `citation_treatment_signal` child + parent rollup cols (`strongest_negative_class`/`judged_count`/`judge_as_of`); `derived_method` CHECK relaxed to `citation_graph+judge`.
- **Safe degrade:** no gateway / no judge model / no snippet / no case_name → byte-identical to PR1 graph-only.
- **Cost (DE-344 first bite):** the per-case judge budget is PR2's cost bound; the autonomous-R4 `estimate_tool_cost` wiring is pinned to **WS-E** (its first-metered-source trigger) — see the updated DE-344 PRD entry.
- **Escalation (DE-360):** deferred; PR2 tags `purpose='judge_treatment'` so calibration data accrues.

### 👀 For the security reviewer
- **One new gateway egress field:** `get_citing_opinions` now returns the CourtListener `snippet` (public search highlight). Defensive extraction; never raises on missing fields. BYO-key-gated exactly as today (no new provider/boundary).
- **New P3 surface:** `citation_treatment_signal` is metadata/derived-reasoning only and is added to the no-raw-payload tripwire in this PR. Confirm no opinion payload is persisted.
- **Intended behavior:** the persisted judge `justification` is our derived reasoning (prompt instructs describe-not-quote), surfaced on the ledger read alongside a link out to the opinion.

### Built via SDD (7 tasks, per-task spec+quality reviews + Opus whole-branch review)
The Opus whole-branch review caught a real gate-passing defect: a stale-refresh without a judge pass (gateway=None / missing case_name) left **orphaned child signals** that the read path resurfaced on a row reset to graph-only — a contradictory derived-validity claim. **Fixed** (unconditional child-signal delete on every refresh + 2 regression tests covering both uncovered paths), plus a `citing_opinion_id` dedup guard and a migration index-name alignment.

### Tests / gates
- api full suite **2381 passed / 1 skipped**; gateway **704 passed / 3 skipped**.
- api + gateway: `ruff check`, `ruff format --check`, whole-app `mypy` (`mypy --strict` gateway) — all clean.
- New: gateway snippet; child-model + migration + CHECK + tripwire; judge prompt/parser (all classes + every parse-failure → None); rollup (severity + corroboration + cap); judge-pass integration (P3 strip, budget, idempotent refresh, non-fatal, graph-only degrade, **refresh-clears-signals**, dedup); worker gateway-resolution + degrade; ledger read (no N+1, P3, graph-only safe).

### Follow-ups filed
- **DE-366** — worker short-circuits the judge pass when no gateway is reachable (latency/log-noise only; data correct).
- **DE-367** — materialize the rollup to avoid future stored-vs-recomputed `judged_count` divergence.
- Still committed before Phase 2 closes: **DE-363** (lazy-on-trace-open fallback), **DE-364** (per-cluster SAVEPOINT).

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

entries.title: #234: feat(web): WS-G PR1-UI — treatment signal in the ledger trace (Phase 2)
entries.text:
feat(web): WS-G PR1-UI — treatment signal in the ledger trace (Phase 2) ## WS-G PR1-UI — Treatment signal in the Citation Ledger trace

Makes the citation-graph treatment signal (shipped backend-only in WS-G PR1 #233) **visible** in the C1 one-click trace. On a caselaw ledger entry, a compact muted line — **⚖ Cited by N later opinions · derived <date>** — with a disclosure that reveals the most-recent citing cases. **Graph-only provenance, deliberately NOT a "good law" verdict** (ADR 0019 D1). Web-only; **not security-gated**.

### What it renders
- Collapsed: `⚖ Cited by 412 later opinions · derived 2026-06-26  ▸`
- Expanded: a 5-case preview + `+ 25 more · 30 most recent of 412` (the capped microcopy keeps it honest that the shown list is a subset).
- Null treatment (derivation pending) or non-caselaw entries → render nothing.

### Anti-overclaiming (ADR 0019 D1) — the load-bearing UI decision
- **Neutral/muted only — no green/red validity coloring.** (The green `exact_match` chip is the existing *verification* status, untouched. Treatment color/severity arrives with PR2's real classifications.)
- "derived" in the visible line + a `title`/`aria-label` stating it is *not* an editorial good-law judgment.

### Structure
- `LedgerTreatment` types + a pure, Vitest-tested `treatment-display.ts` helper (`treatmentSummary`/`formatCitingRef`); the `.svelte` is render-only with **local, token-driven CSS** (the P1-C1 scoping lesson). No backend/panel change.

### Review & evidence
SDD: per-task spec+quality reviews + a whole-branch review. The whole-branch review caught a real a11y defect (an empty citing list rendered an interactive no-op button — broken `aria-expanded`); fixed to a non-interactive line, with the `aria-label` now carrying the count/date. **Visual evidence** (headless-Chrome, light+dark, collapsed+expanded) confirmed the line is muted (no coloring), the disclosure toggles, and the capped microcopy shows.

### Gates
`npm run check:lq-ai` 0 errors (the 5 warnings/3 files are pre-existing in unrelated components — this branch adds none); `npm run test:frontend` 8/8.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Refs ADR 0019 D1. Renders the data from WS-G PR1 (#233). Extends P1-C1 (#228).

entries.title: #233: feat: WS-G PR1 — citation-graph treatment signal (Phase 2)
entries.text:
feat: WS-G PR1 — citation-graph treatment signal (Phase 2) ## WS-G PR1 — Citation-graph treatment signal

First slice of the **validity/treatment layer** (ADR 0019) — the KeyCite/Shepard's analog, done LQ.AI's way: **derive the signal transparently, never assert an editorial verdict.** For each case an assistant turn cites, this derives a citation-graph signal ("cited by N later opinions; here are the most recent ≤30") **off the turn's critical path** and populates the Citation Ledger's reserved `treatment_id` slot. **Graph-only — no LLM-judge** (that's PR2, per ADR 0019 D4).

### Pipeline
`get_citing_opinions` CourtListener gateway op (Search `cites:(opinion_id)` filter) → `research.service` wrapper → `derive_treatment_for_message` (per-cited-case cache-reuse-or-fetch, upsert `citation_treatment`, link ledger entries) → arq `treatment_derivation_job` enqueued **best-effort after `_audit_message_sent`** at all **3** turn-finalize sites → `resolve_ledger_entries` exposes a `treatment` object on the `/chats/{id}/ledger` read.

### Load-bearing properties (verified by the Opus whole-branch review)
- **Gate independence** — derivation is async; the enqueue fires *after* the message+citations+ledger commit, is best-effort (never blocks/raises into the turn), and the fiduciary-grade gate verdict/latency never depends on treatment (ADR 0018 D3 / 0019 D2).
- **No LLM** anywhere in PR1 — graph-only.
- **P3** — `citation_treatment.citing_opinions` holds **refs only** (cluster/opinion ids + case_name/court/date, never opinion text); the model joins the `_AUDIT_MODELS` no-raw-payload tripwire; the ledger read batch-loads treatments (no N+1).
- **Per-case non-fatal** + **cache/TTL** (30-day reuse, in-place upsert), **`flush` not `commit`** (worker commits once), **`cited_by_count` = upstream total** (not the capped list length).
- **Security** — new egress is `read_only`, BYO-key-gated like the other CL ops; `cites:(<int>)` built from an int-validated value (no injection); no new agent-exposed surface.

### Migration
`0061` creates `citation_treatment` (chains 0060). No change to the ledger/gate tables.

### ⚠️ For the security reviewer — two tracked follow-ups (both committed to land before Phase 2 closes)
- **DE-363** — the ADR-0019-D2 **lazy-on-trace-open fallback** is deferred; PR1 ships async-only (an undurived entry renders `treatment: null` — honest, not a silent gap).
- **DE-364** — the Opus review found a concurrency edge: a `db.flush()` IntegrityError (two concurrent turns citing the **same uncached case** → `uq_citation_treatment_cluster_id` collision) poisons the session, so a **multi-cluster** turn loses its remaining derivations. **Non-crashing** (worker returns `ok:False`), single-cluster turns unaffected, DE-363-recoverable. Fix = per-cluster `begin_nested()` SAVEPOINT; filed (not fixed) to keep this slice scoped.

### Spec deviation (recorded)
Ordering is **most-recent-first** (`dateFiled desc`) — CourtListener's Search API has no server-side court-rank sort; court-rank re-ranking of the materialized subset is PR2. The capped list still pre-stages PR2's judge subset.

### Tests
8 new tests (gateway provider op; model+migration+P3 tripwire; derivation service cache/stale/non-fatal/link; async job; ledger exposure). **Full api suite: 2339 passed, 1 skipped, 0 failures.** Gateway: 703 passed, **mypy --strict** clean. `ruff format`+`check` clean.

### Review
Subagent-driven: per-task spec+quality reviews + an **Opus whole-branch review** (Ready-to-merge: Yes, no Critical). The Task-3 review's tz-awareness finding was folded forward into Task 4 as a guard; the implementer correctly discovered the **third** finalize site the plan missed.

**Attestation:** N/A — citation-engine infrastructure, no skill legal-substance change.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Refs ADR 0019 (D2/D3/D4/D7/D8), ADR 0018 D6, ADR 0014. Builds on Phase 1 (Citation Ledger + fiduciary gate).

entries.title: #232: docs(adr): ADR 0019 — transparent validity/treatment layer (WS-G, Phase 2)
entries.text:
docs(adr): ADR 0019 — transparent validity/treatment layer (WS-G, Phase 2) ## ADR 0019 — Transparent validity/treatment layer (WS-G)

Opens **Phase 2** of the fiduciary-grade milestone. The KeyCite/Shepard's analog, done LQ.AI's way: **derive** a case's treatment from the citation graph + an LLM-judge over citing passages and **show the work** — never an opaque editorial "good law / bad law" verdict.

**Maintainer-accepted 2026-06-26** (Status: Accepted). Docs-only (ADR + mini-PRD pointers).

### Decisions (D1–D10)
- **D1 — Derive, don't assert.** Signals + reasoning + confidence + one-click links to the exact citing case/passage; every signal labeled "derived, not editorial." Absence of a negative signal ≠ "good law" — it's "no negative treatment found as of <date>."
- **D2 — Per-cited-case, cached, off the turn's critical path.** New `citation_treatment` table; async after finalize (lazy-on-trace-open fallback); populates the reserved `citation_ledger_entry.treatment_id`. Treatment enriches the ledger entry; it does **not** gate the turn (the fiduciary-grade gate stays citation-only, ADR 0018 D3).
- **D3 — One new gateway egress op** (`get_citing_opinions` on the ADR-0014 boundary, BYO-key-gated). Only new external surface; security-gated.
- **D4 — Phased build: graph-first (PR1), judge-second (PR2).** PR1 ships the citation graph as a derived provenance signal (no judge fan-out). PR2 adds the treatment judge over a prioritized, capped, budget-bounded subset (first DE-360 proving ground).
- **D5 — Taxonomy + rollup:** followed / distinguished / criticized / questioned / overruled / superseded / neutral, rolled up to the strongest-negative case-level signal.
- **D6–D10:** reuse the existing confidence scale; **P3-preserving reference storage** (joins the no-raw-payload tripwire); staleness/as-of dating (30-day default TTL); derived/connector-editorial coexistence (each attributed); and **the R4 economic brake becomes real** (DE-344).

### Why now
Phase 1 (Citation Ledger + fiduciary gate, WS-A/B/C) is complete and merged. ADR 0018 D6 reserved the `treatment_id` slot for exactly this; the judge rails + opinion store are reusable. The only net-new external surface is the one citing-graph operation.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Refs ADR 0014/0015/0016/0018, DE-344, DE-360.

entries.title: #231: feat(citation): caselaw FAIL tier via H3 attribution (P1-B1c)
entries.text:
feat(citation): caselaw FAIL tier via H3 attribution (P1-B1c) ## P1-B1c — Caselaw FAIL tier via H3 attribution

Closes the last Phase-1 honesty gap in the fiduciary-grade chain: a **fabricated or materially misquoted** caselaw quote is no longer silently dropped — it is now flagged.

Before this PR, a caselaw quote that matched no consulted opinion (verbatim *or* via P1-B1b's SUPPORTED judge) was dropped → invisible → the fiduciary gate could not flag it. P1-B1c attributes each blockquote to its `### [Case Name], [Court], [Year] ([Citation])` H3 heading, matches the case name to **exactly one** consulted opinion, judges the passage against **that opinion only**, and writes a FAIL row on reject — which flows through the **unchanged** ledger + gate to a `flagged` turn.

### Design (maintainer-approved decisions)
- **Matcher: normalized-exact, single-match only.** A passage is attributed only when exactly one consulted cluster's `case_name` normalizes-equal to the parsed H3 case name. Zero/multiple matches, or no H3, → the passage stays on B1b's all-opinions SUPPORTED-or-drop path. **This match-gate is the false-positive guard** — a statute/KB/emphasis blockquote never matches a consulted case, so it can never produce a FAIL row.
- **Can't-judge an attributed passage:** over-budget → unverified FAIL row ("claims case X, not verified"); transient gateway error → drop (don't flag a good draft on a flaky call).
- **Strictly additive on P1-B1b.** Only confidently-attributed passages can FAIL; everything else behaves byte-for-byte as on `main` today. `gateway=None` stays verbatim-only.

### ⚠️ One intended behavior change (for the security reviewer)
A quote confidently attributed to case X, not verbatim, that **case X's own opinion rejects** now FAILs — *even if* some other consulted opinion would have SUPPORTED it (B1b's all-opinions path would have tagged it SUPPORTED). This is **intentional**: it is precisely the honesty gap this slice closes (a quote attributed to a case whose text does not support it is unverified, regardless of other cases on the docket).

### No schema / gate / ledger / UI change
FAIL rows are `verified=False, verification_method=NULL` — already permitted by `chk_message_caselaw_citations_verified_has_method`. `ledger.py` already maps `verified=False → "unverified"`; the gate already maps that to `flagged`; C1 already renders the red badge. **No migration.** Only `ResearchClusterMetadata` is newly loaded (for case names). FAIL-reason severity split (judge-rejected vs over-budget) deferred as **DE-362**.

### Two pure, unit-tested helpers
- `attribute_passages` — blockquote → nearest `### ` H3 → case name (text before first comma)
- `normalize_case_name` / `match_case_name` — normalized-exact single-match guard

### Tests
- 7 new B1c tests (2 unit files + 1 integration file): reject→FAIL→`flagged`; accept→SUPPORTED (B1b preserved); **unattributed→drop, no FAIL** (false-positive guard); over-budget→unverified FAIL; transient-error→drop; missing-confidence/non-JSON judge output→drop (no false FAIL).
- 167-test B1b + verbatim + unit regression: green (additive guarantee).
- **Full api suite: 2331 passed, 1 skipped, 0 failures.** `ruff format` + `ruff check` + `mypy` clean.

### Review
Subagent-driven development: per-task spec + quality reviews, plus an **Opus whole-branch review** (Ready-to-merge: Yes — no Critical/Important; verified the additive guarantee, airtight reject-vs-drop, no new egress/injection). The Opus task-review caught a real fiduciary-critical defect mid-build — `_parse_judge_response` collapses an explicit `"no"`, truncated JSON, an unknown verdict, and a missing-confidence `"yes"` all to the same miss — which would have written a **false FAIL** on judge-output noise; fixed so only a parseable `{"verdict":"no"}` flags a FAIL.

**Attestation:** N/A — no skill legal-substance change (citation-engine infrastructure only).

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Refs ADR 0018 D2/D3. Builds on P1-A1 (#218), P1-B1 (#225), P1-B1b (#229).

entries.title: #229: feat(citation): caselaw paraphrase/content judge — SUPPORTED tier (P1-B1b)
entries.text:
feat(citation): caselaw paraphrase/content judge — SUPPORTED tier (P1-B1b) ## P1-B1b — Caselaw paraphrase/content judge (SUPPORTED-only)

Gives a non-verbatim caselaw blockquote that a **whole-opinion** LLM judge finds faithfully supported a `paraphrase_judge` row — so the already-shipped gate renders the turn `supported_only` (amber in the P1-C1 ledger UI) instead of silently dropping the quote. Realizes the [DE-280](../PRD.md#de-280) content-accuracy judge as the SUPPORTED tier. Milestone: fiduciary-grade agentic legal work, Phase 1 (WS-B).

### What's here
- **`api/app/citation/case_content_judge.py`** — `judge_case_content` runs an LLM fidelity judge over the **full** stored opinion text (a dropped passage has no located span, so the cascade's ±200-char window doesn't apply), reusing the cascade's `_JudgeGatewayProtocol` + `_parse_judge_response`. Accept → `paraphrase_judge`; reject / malformed / gateway-error → MISS (never raises). Plus `estimate_case_content_cost_usd` (per-call cost × opinion-length, floored). Inference tagged `purpose='judge_case_content'`; `estimate_judge_call_cost_usd` generalized with a `purpose` param (cache key `<model>:<purpose>` — backward-compatible).
- **Migration `0060`** — relaxes the `message_caselaw_citations` method CHECK to allow `paraphrase_judge`.
- **`caselaw.py` orchestration** — after the verbatim loop, a **cost-gated** judge pass over dropped passages writes SUPPORTED rows (whole-opinion span, `partial=True`, first accepting opinion wins). Gateway + the operator-configured judge model threaded at the two caselaw finalize sites.

### Load-bearing invariants
- **Additive-only.** Writes ONLY `verified=True, method='paraphrase_judge'` rows — never a FAIL/unverified row, never flips a turn to `flagged`. A passage no judge accepts stays dropped. (Caselaw fabrication-flagging is the separate **P1-B1c** slice, which needs passage→opinion attribution.)
- **`gateway=None` is byte-identical** to today (verbatim-only; no egress; no cost) — every existing caller/test uses the default and is unaffected.
- **Per-turn cost budget** stops the *whole* judge pass when exceeded (pre-flight, so an over-budget call is never made); the estimate is floored so a zero estimate can't defeat the bound (DoS-safe fan-out cap).
- **No gate / ledger change** — the gate already buckets `paraphrase_judge`→SUPPORTED; B1b only produces richer rows.

### Testing
- Unit (mocked gateway): accept→`paraphrase_judge`, reject/malformed/gateway-error→MISS, prompt includes whole opinion + passage, purpose tag, cost scaling.
- Integration (real Postgres, mocked gateway): paraphrased quote → `paraphrase_judge` row → gate `supported_only`; reject-all → drop (additive-only); budget=0 → judge never called + no row; `gateway=None` → unchanged.
- Migration 0060 chains from 0059. Full api gate: ruff + mypy clean; full suite green.

### Review
Built via brainstorm → spec → plan → subagent-driven-development (per-task spec+quality reviews + an Opus whole-branch review). The Opus review caught that the caselaw judge ignored the operator-configured judge model (used hardcoded `fast`, diverging from the document-citation path) and a theoretical zero-cost-estimate budget hole — both fixed.

> **Security review (CODEOWNERS):** new gateway egress + cost accounting + citation surface (`api/app/citation/**`, `chats.py`) — please review/merge; I have not self-merged.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

entries.title: #228: feat(web): Citation Ledger UI — fiduciary badge + one-click trace (P1-C1)
entries.text:
feat(web): Citation Ledger UI — fiduciary badge + one-click trace (P1-C1) ## P1-C1 — Citation Ledger UI

The transparency payoff made visible: a per-assistant-turn **fiduciary badge** that opens a **one-click-trace panel** listing every source the turn brought into context — resolved to the passage read and its verification state. Consumes the merged `GET /api/v1/chats/{chat_id}/ledger` (`{entries, gates}`). Frontend-only (`web/`), **not security-gated**. Milestone: fiduciary-grade agentic legal work, Phase 1 (WS-C).

### What's here
- **`api/ledger.ts`** — `getChatLedger(chatId, messageId?)` via the canonical `apiRequest` wrapper.
- **`citations/ledger-state.ts`** — pure, unit-tested helpers: `ledgerEntryState(status)` reuses the existing M2-C2 5-state palette (verbatim→green, supported→amber, unverified→gray); `gateBadge(gate)` maps the verdict to a `TrustPill` tone (`fiduciary_grade`→sage, `supported_only`→amber, `flagged`→red).
- **`CitationLedgerPanel.svelte` + `LedgerEntryRow.svelte`** — a `TierDetailsPanel`-style trace modal: gate badge + per-tier counts header, one row per entry (source identity + the passage(s) read with char offsets + a verification-state chip), empty-state line.
- **`MessageBubble.svelte`** — lazy-fetch per assistant turn (mirrors `loadSources`), render the badge in the pill row, open the panel on click. **Additive** — `M2Citations`/`ToolSourcesPanel` untouched; degrades silently on 404.

### Posture
- **No new dependencies / no component-test harness.** All branching logic lives in pure, unit-tested helpers; the `.svelte` files are thin and verified by `svelte-check` — matching the codebase's testing convention.
- **No XSS surface** — all ledger text renders via Svelte's auto-escaped `{...}`; no `{@html}`; the source URL link carries `rel="noopener noreferrer"`.
- The richer **caselaw SUPPORTED** (amber) state arrives with **P1-B1b**; C1 renders whatever statuses the ledger contains, so it works today (KB verbatim + provenance) and gets richer automatically.

### Review
Built via brainstorm → spec → plan → subagent-driven-development (per-task spec+quality reviews + an Opus whole-branch review). The Opus review caught a Svelte CSS-scoping bug — the entry chip reused M2Citations' component-scoped `.state-*` classes, which silently no-op'd the verification colors (the feature's core distinction); fixed by defining the chip + 4 state colors (incl. dark mode) locally. `svelte-check` 0 errors; ledger unit tests + the existing 728-test suite green.

> **Manual visual QA recommended at merge:** CSS class resolution isn't covered by any automated gate — worth opening the panel once to confirm the chips render colored (the fix makes this correct by construction, but a glance confirms it). The `web` container serves a prebuilt bundle — rebuild before checking.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

entries.title: #227: docs: split P1-B1c (caselaw FAIL + attribution) out of P1-B1b
entries.text:
docs: split P1-B1c (caselaw FAIL + attribution) out of P1-B1b ## docs: split P1-B1c (caselaw FAIL + attribution) out of P1-B1b

Docs-only. Corrects the milestone proposal's Phase-1 PR decomposition after the B1b brainstorm (2026-06-25):

- **P1-B1b** is now **SUPPORTED-only** — a whole-opinion judge gives paraphrased caselaw quotes a `paraphrase_judge` row (gate → `supported_only`). It is **additive-only**: it writes no FAIL/unverified rows and can never flip a turn to `flagged`. (The prior entry wrongly said B1b "persists caselaw FAIL attempts.")
- **P1-B1c** (new) — safe caselaw fabrication-flagging needs reliable **passage→opinion attribution** (parse each blockquote's `### Case Name` H3 header, match it to a consulted opinion, judge only against the attributed opinion). Without it, judging a dropped passage against *all* consulted opinions false-positives on legitimate non-caselaw blockquotes (a statute, a KB quote) — wrongly flagging a good draft, the worst failure for a fiduciary tool. Split out as its own security-gated slice; pairs with the deferred DE-279 case-cite resolution.

No code.

entries.title: #226: docs: file DE-361 (route llm_judge into the fiduciary-grade gate)
entries.text:
docs: file DE-361 (route llm_judge into the fiduciary-grade gate) ## docs: file DE-361 — route `llm_judge` into the fiduciary-grade gate

Docs-only follow-up surfaced by the **P1-B1** (#225) final whole-branch review.

The fiduciary-grade gate buckets each citation's `verification_status` into PASS/SUPPORTED/FAIL; an unrecognized status is logged and **excluded** (never silently PASS). The `message_citations.verification_method` CHECK admits **`llm_judge`** as a legal value, but it's in none of the gate's sets — so a citation carrying it would be excluded from the counts.

**Latent and harmless today** — the citation cascade does not emit `llm_judge` (only the model docstring + CHECK reference it). DE-361 tracks the forward-consistency obligation: when LLM-judge verification ships, route `llm_judge` (→ almost certainly SUPPORTED) into the gate, and more broadly ensure every legal `verification_method` maps to exactly one gate bucket (a structural test would guard this).

No code; PRD §9 only.

entries.title: #225: feat(citation): deterministic fiduciary-grade gate (P1-B1)
entries.text:
feat(citation): deterministic fiduciary-grade gate (P1-B1) ## P1-B1 — Deterministic fiduciary-grade gate

Computes the **fiduciary-grade verdict** (ADR 0018 D3) for each assistant turn, deterministically over the turn's citation-ledger entries, and records it. The transparency posture becomes a hard, computed gate — not a label. Milestone: fiduciary-grade agentic legal work, Phase 1 (WS-B). Builds on P1-A2 (ledger) and the merged P1-A3 (read API).

### What's here
- **`work_product_fiduciary_gate`** table (migration `0059`) — 1:1-per-assistant-message, **metadata-only**: `gate_status` (`fiduciary_grade`/`supported_only`/`flagged`) + per-tier counts + aggregate `confidence`. Joins the P3 no-raw-payload tripwire.
- **`compute_and_record_gate`** (`api/app/citation/gate.py`) — buckets each ledger entry by `verification_status`: PASS `{exact_match, tolerant_match}` / SUPPORTED `{paraphrase_judge, ensemble_strict, ensemble_majority}` / FAIL `{unverified, failed}`; `provenance` entries are not assertions (excluded). Verdict: `flagged` on any FAIL; else `supported_only` if any SUPPORTED; else `fiduciary_grade` (zero-assertion turn → `fiduciary_grade`, vacuously). Confidence = mean of counted non-null confidences. **Upserts** the verdict (the ledger is the history-preserving record). Flushes, never commits (P5).
- **Assembler honesty fix** — `assemble_ledger_entries` mislabeled unverified KB `message_citations` (`verified=False`) as `"verified"`; now `"unverified"`. This is what makes **FAIL detection honest** for KB quotes — without it the gate would count a failed quote as PASS. Regression-locked.
- **Finalize hooks** — `compute_and_record_gate` runs at all three chat-finalize sites (each guarded; a gate failure never breaks the turn/stream).
- **Read** — `GET /chats/{chat_id}/ledger` now returns a `gates[]` array of per-turn verdicts alongside `entries` (additive — the A3 response object reserved this). OpenAPI extended; no path-count change.

### Scope boundary (honest)
**Gate-only / deterministic — no new egress.** The DE-280 opinion-scale **paraphrase judge** (the SUPPORTED tier for caselaw), **caselaw-FAIL persistence**, and the long-opinion **cost pre-flight** are deferred to **P1-B1b** (new egress + R4 cost). So today: KB-document FAIL is detected and flagged; caselaw failures are *dropped at persistence* (not miscounted — the gate cannot read a dropped failure as PASS), and surface honestly once B1b lands.

### Invariants
- **P3:** gate row holds only labels/counts/confidence/ids — no content; in the tripwire scan.
- **P5:** the helper flushes, never commits — rides the finalize transaction (verdict atomic with the audit row).
- **Conservative posture:** unrecognized status is excluded + logged, never silently counted as PASS; any FAIL → `flagged`, never silently emitted.
- Migration `0059` chains from `0058`.

### Testing
- Gate classification (all-PASS, mixed SUPPORTED, any-FAIL, provenance-excluded, zero-assertion, unknown-status-excluded), upsert, `resolve_gates`, the assembler-mislabel regression (genuine `verified=False` → `unverified` → `flagged` path), the endpoint `gates[]` integration.
- Full api gate: `ruff format` + `ruff check` + `mypy app` clean; full suite green.

### Review
Built via brainstorm → spec → plan → subagent-driven-development (per-task spec+quality reviews + an Opus whole-branch review: *Ready to merge*; P3 + P5 + conservative posture independently verified). Final review surfaced one **forward-consistency follow-up** (filed as a DE): `llm_judge` is a legal `verification_method` value not yet routed into a gate bucket — latent (the cascade doesn't emit it today), excluded-not-miscounted if it ever appears.

> **Security review (CODEOWNERS):** citation/audit surface — please review/merge; I have not self-merged.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Spec: `docs/superpowers/specs/2026-06-25-p1b1-fiduciary-grade-gate-design.md` · Plan: `docs/superpowers/plans/2026-06-25-p1b1-fiduciary-grade-gate.md` · Refs ADR 0018 D3/D5.

entries.title: #224: docs: file DE-360 (gateway-native quality-escalation routing) + P1-B1/B1b split
entries.text:
docs: file DE-360 (gateway-native quality-escalation routing) + P1-B1/B1b split ## docs: file DE-360 (gateway-native quality-escalation routing) + P1-B1/B1b split

Docs-only. Two changes:

### 1. DE-360 — gateway-native quality-escalation routing (renumbered from a colliding DE-345)
A standalone `docs/DE345…md` draft reused **DE-345**, which is already assigned (the `_stream_loop_outcome` renderer extraction). Renumbered to **DE-360** (next free after DE-359) and folded into **PRD §9**.

The enhancement: a **gateway-native** cheap→capable escalation router (run a small model, judge confidence + rationale, escalate only on low confidence) whose **judge reasoning is exposed and logged to the citation ledger** — not an opaque score. The importable thing from Tucuxi-Inc's MIT FLO project is the *pattern* (and possibly MIT code), **not** the service: a second provider-key holder would violate the gateway boundary (ADR 0014) and an opaque score violates the transparency mandate. First proving ground: **WS-G**'s citing-passage LLM-judge fan-out, the milestone's largest cost lever.

### 2. P1-B1 / P1-B1b split recorded in the proposal
Reflects the maintainer-approved 2026-06-25 decision: **P1-B1 is the deterministic gate-only slice** (no egress, no cost; records the verdict on a new `work_product_fiduciary_gate` table; fixes the P1-A2 unverified-KB-citation mislabel). **P1-B1b** carries DE-280's opinion-scale paraphrase judge + caselaw-FAIL persistence + the DE-344 cost pre-flight (new egress → heavier security review), and is the first consumer of DE-360.

No code, no schema, no security-path changes.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

entries.title: #223: feat(citation): Citation Ledger read API + one-click trace (P1-A3)
entries.text:
feat(citation): Citation Ledger read API + one-click trace (P1-A3) ## P1-A3 — Citation Ledger read API + one-click trace

Exposes the already-populated Citation Ledger as a read surface — the transparency payoff of **ADR 0018 D4** ("one click from any cited assertion to the exact source and passage read"). Part of the **fiduciary-grade agentic legal work** milestone, Phase 1 (WS-A). Builds on P1-A2 (#219, ledger table + assembly) and P1-A1 (#218).

### What's here
- **`resolve_ledger_entries`** (`api/app/citation/ledger.py`) — a pure-DB read-side resolver. Batch-loads the three referenced artifact tables (`message_citations` / `message_caselaw_citations` / `message_tool_sources`) — one `IN (...)` query per table, no N+1 — and shapes each `citation_ledger_entry` into source identity + the passage(s) read (offsets + text for quote kinds; provenance-only for tool sources) + verification status + confidence + provenance.
- **`GET /api/v1/chats/{chat_id}/ledger`** (`api/app/api/chats.py`) — one rich list endpoint, chat-scoped, with an optional `?message_id=` turn filter. Ownership parity with `get_citations` (cross-user/unknown chat → 404; unknown `message_id` → 404; non-UUID → 400). Response is an **object** `{chat_id, entries}` so P1-B1 can add a `gates` key additively.
- **P10 collision guards:** route in `IMPLEMENTED_ROUTES`; path in `EXPECTED_PATHS`; pinned count `134 → 135`; OpenAPI sketch path block + `LedgerEntry` schema.

### Invariants
- **P3 (no raw payload):** the `citation_ledger_entry` row holds no content — passages are resolved from the content layer (`MessageCitation.source_text`, `MessageCaselawCitation.source_text`) at **read time**, never duplicated into the ledger row.
- **P1 (no new egress):** pure DB reads; no gateway/LLM call.
- **Conservative posture:** a dangling referenced row is skipped with a logged warning, never a 500.
- No schema change / no migration.

### Decisions (maintainer-approved 2026-06-25)
One rich endpoint (embedded trace) over list+per-entry-trace; chat-scoped now, matter/project-scoped aggregation deferred. See the spec for the full fork list.

### Testing
- Resolver: shaping per source_kind, `message_id` filter, empty, dangling-skip, no-N+1.
- Endpoint: 200 + resolved entries, `?message_id` filter, cross-user 404, non-UUID 400.
- OpenAPI conformance green at 135; `IMPLEMENTED_ROUTES` updated.
- Full api gate: `ruff format` + `ruff check` + `mypy app` clean; **2281 passed, 1 skipped**.

### Review
Built via brainstorm → spec → plan → subagent-driven-development (per-task spec+quality reviews + an Opus whole-branch review: *Ready to merge* — P3 preserved, no cross-user leak, batch-load confirmed N+1-free, guards consistent).

> **Security review (CODEOWNERS):** citation surface (`api/app/citation/**`, `chats.py`) — please review/merge; I have not self-merged.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Spec: `docs/superpowers/specs/2026-06-25-p1a3-ledger-read-api-design.md` · Plan: `docs/superpowers/plans/2026-06-25-p1a3-ledger-read-api.md` · Refs ADR 0018 D4/D5.

**Merged this session** (main @ `e9c399d`): #185/#186 closed as duplicative + **DE-358** filed (#215); the fiduciary milestone scoped with **ADR 0018 accepted** (#216); and **all three Phase 1 WS-A slices** — P1-A1 caselaw quote-verification (#218), P1-A2 Citation Ledger table + assembly (#219), DE-350 generic-MCP provenance (#220).

The doc lays out the **next slices** (P1-A3 ledger read API + one-click trace, and P1-B1 the fiduciary-grade gate — parallelizable — then P1-C1 the matter-scoped UI), the Phase 2–3 roadmap, the load-bearing **workflow reminders** (origin+tucuxi mirroring, security review, never-self-merge gated branches), and the **test-harness recipe** (host venv + throwaway pgvector; next migration = `0059`).
