entries.title: #220: feat(chat): generic-MCP retrieval-provenance (DE-350)
entries.text:
feat(chat): generic-MCP retrieval-provenance (DE-350) Closes the [DE-350](../blob/main/docs/PRD.md#de-350) gap and completes the Citation Ledger's tool-source coverage. Sequenced immediately after P1-A2 (#219) per maintainer directive. Spec + plan in `docs/superpowers/`.

## What it does

PR6c recorded retrieval-provenance (`message_tool_sources`, the "Sources consulted" panel) for **case-law** tool results only. A chat turn that called an operator-wired **MCP** tool recorded nothing. DE-350 records **one `message_tool_sources` row per successful MCP call** (`source_kind='mcp'`, `provider=`server, `tool=`tool name) with a best-effort label/url.

Because the P1-A2 ledger assembler is source-kind-agnostic, these `mcp` rows flow into the Citation Ledger automatically — **no ledger or schema change**.

## Changes

- `api/app/chat/tool_loop.py` — `extract_mcp_tool_source(spec, data)` + a defensive `_mcp_label_url(data)` (best-effort title/url from dict-shaped payloads; **never raises** on a malformed shape) + a `collect_tool_sources(spec, data)` router keyed on `spec.kind`. The capture site swaps `extract_tool_sources(spec.tool, …)` → `collect_tool_sources(spec, …)` (one line; the `external_ref` dedup is unchanged).
- `api/app/models/message_tool_source.py` — docstring (MCP now supported).
- `docs/PRD.md` — DE-350 marked **SHIPPED**.

## Decisions (maintainer-approved 2026-06-24)

- **One row per MCP call** (not per result item) — honest "consulted tool X on server Y," robust against heterogeneous MCP result shapes.
- **Best-effort, defensive label/url** — surfaces a url/title when the payload obviously carries one, behind a try/except that never crashes the turn.
- Per-result-item provenance and per-server label/url conventions are **deferred**.

## Posture

No schema change (`source_kind` is a free string), no new egress, no governance/tier change, caselaw path untouched. The label/url helper is fully wrapped — a malformed MCP payload can never break the assistant turn.

## Verification

Built via TDD with per-task spec+quality review and an Opus whole-branch review (**Ready to merge**, no Critical/Important; the reviewer traced 13 adversarial payloads confirming the helper never raises). Gates: `ruff` + `mypy` clean; full api suite **2274 passed, 1 pre-existing skip** (no `-m provider`).

Practicing-attorney attestation: **N/A** (pure code; no legal-substance skill).

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

entries.title: #219: feat(citation): Citation Ledger entry table + assembly (P1-A2)
entries.text:
feat(citation): Citation Ledger entry table + assembly (P1-A2) Phase 1 WS-A, second slice of the **fiduciary-grade** Citation Ledger milestone ([ADR 0018](../blob/main/docs/adr/0018-citation-ledger-and-fiduciary-grade-output.md) D1/D5/D6/D7). Builds on P1-A1 (#218). Spec + plan in `docs/superpowers/`.

## What it does

Adds `citation_ledger_entry` — a **thin referencing** table that unifies a chat turn's three previously-disconnected per-turn artifacts into one matter-and-turn-scoped record:
- `message_citations` (KB-document quote verification)
- `message_caselaw_citations` (caselaw quote verification — P1-A1)
- `message_tool_sources` (retrieval provenance — "sources consulted")

Each entry references **exactly one** underlying row via three nullable FKs (a CHECK enforces exactly-one-non-null) + a `source_kind` discriminator, and mirrors that source's verification status as a queryable label. A reserved `treatment_id` slot awaits the WS-G validity layer. A source-kind-agnostic assembler writes one entry per row at chat finalize.

## Changes

- `api/app/models/citation_ledger_entry.py` + migration **`0058`** — the table (3 nullable source FKs + exactly-one CHECK, confidence-range CHECK, 3 indexes, reserved `treatment_id` with no FK; no `tier` — `message_tool_sources` can't populate it).
- `api/app/citation/ledger.py` — `assemble_ledger_entries(db, *, message_id)`, self-deriving chat/project scope; one entry per citation/source row.
- `api/app/api/chats.py` — guarded assembler call at all three finalize branches (incl. the single-shot non-tool path, so KB-only chats get a ledger).
- `api/tests/test_transparency_invariants.py` — `CitationLedgerEntry` added to the P3 no-raw-payload scan.
- `docs/db-schema.md` + ADR 0018 D1 reconciliation note (`citable_source_id` → `message_caselaw_citation_id`).

## Security posture

- **Metadata-only:** the entry holds NO content (`source_text` lives on the referenced row); it references by id. Verified by its inclusion in the **P3 tripwire** (`test_audit_models_have_no_raw_payload_columns` green; denylist/allowlist unchanged).
- **No new egress** — pure DB reads + writes.
- **Conservative posture** — every finalize hook is guarded; a ledger-assembly failure logs and degrades to "no ledger this turn," never aborting the assistant turn/stream.

This touches the citation/audit-adjacent surface → **security review per CODEOWNERS**.

## Sequencing note

The assembler is **source-kind-agnostic** over `message_tool_sources`, so generic-MCP entries flow into the ledger automatically once **DE-350** lands — which is sequenced as the immediate next slice.

## Verification

Built via TDD with per-task spec+quality review and an Opus whole-branch review (**Ready to merge**, no Critical/Important). The final review independently probed the exactly-one CHECK (Postgres evaluates CHECK before FK lookups → the 2-FK rejection test genuinely exercises it). One mid-stream fix: aligned `retrieved_at` to `TIMESTAMPTZ` to match the migration and removed a tzinfo-stripping shim. Gates: `ruff` + `mypy` clean; full api suite **2266 passed, 1 pre-existing skip** (no `-m provider`).

Practicing-attorney attestation: **N/A** (pure code; no legal-substance skill).

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

entries.title: #218: feat(citation): external caselaw quote-verification core (P1-A1)
entries.text:
feat(citation): external caselaw quote-verification core (P1-A1) First buildable slice of the **fiduciary-grade** Citation Ledger milestone (Phase 1, WS-A), implementing [ADR 0018](../blob/main/docs/adr/0018-citation-ledger-and-fiduciary-grade-output.md) D2/D3. Spec + plan: `docs/superpowers/specs/2026-06-24-…` / `docs/superpowers/plans/2026-06-24-…`.

## What it does

Character-verifies the model's **verbatim caselaw quotes** against the opinion text **already stored** by the research service — no new content store, no second fetch, no ghost `files`/`documents` rows.

At chat finalize: derive the consulted CourtListener clusters from `LoopFinal.tool_sources` → load each consulted cluster's stored opinion plaintext → extract markdown blockquote passages from the answer (the `case-law-research` skill's `> …` "Relevant passage:" convention) → locate each verbatim in an opinion → run the **existing** citation cascade `verify(…, gateway=None)` (deterministic stages 1–2, **no LLM, no cost**) → persist verified rows in a new `message_caselaw_citations` table.

## Scope (deliberately tight)

- **In:** exact/tolerant char-fidelity verification of verbatim caselaw quotes + persistence. Paraphrased passages correctly stay **unverified** (honest, conservative posture).
- **Deferred:** the opinion-scale paraphrase judge (P1-B1 / DE-280); the `citation_ledger_entry` table (P1-A2); the read API + one-click trace (P1-A3); UI (P1-C1).

## Changes

- `api/app/models/message_caselaw_citation.py` + migration **`0057`** — new table (mirrors `message_citations` verification fields; keyed to `opinion_id`/`cluster_id` + offsets into opinion plaintext; no `file_id`).
- `api/app/citation/caselaw.py` — blockquote extraction, opinion verify-target adapter, exact-substring locator, and the verify-and-persist orchestrator.
- `api/app/api/chats.py` — guarded hook at the two tool-loop finalize sites (not the single-shot `records=[]` site); a failure here can never abort the turn.
- `docs/db-schema.md` — documents the table.

## Security posture (no new attack surface)

- **No new egress** — the orchestrator only reads already-stored opinion text + the DB; no gateway/MCP/external call (`gateway=None` is structurally guaranteed → the LLM stages are unreachable here, so no cost/tier change).
- **No raw payloads** — the table carries only the quoted `source_text` (the existing citation convention); it is a **content** table, correctly absent from the ADR 0016 P3 audit-model tripwire (`test_transparency_invariants.py` stays green).
- **Conservative posture** — `verified=true` is written only on a passing cascade result; storage miss / paraphrase → no verified row, never a false claim; both finalize hooks are guarded so verification failure degrades to "no verified caselaw citations."

This touches `api/app/citation/**` + `chats.py` → **security review per CODEOWNERS**.

## Verification

Built via TDD with per-task spec+quality review and a final whole-branch review (**Ready to merge**, no Critical/Important findings). Gates: `ruff` + `mypy` clean; full api suite **2262 passed, 1 pre-existing skip** (deterministic — no `-m provider`). New tests cover verbatim → verified, invented quote → no row, and storage-miss → no crash.

Follow-up filed: **DE-359** (savepoint-isolate the chat-finalize persistence flushes — pre-existing risk class, defense-in-depth).

Practicing-attorney attestation: **N/A** (pure code; no legal-substance skill).

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

entries.title: #217: docs: capture Donna's skill_inputs upstream request (non-templated skills)
entries.text:
docs: capture Donna's skill_inputs upstream request (non-templated skills) Commits a stray-but-useful doc that was sitting untracked in the worktree: **Donna's upstream request** that `MessageCreate.skill_inputs` reach the model for **non-templated** skills.

Verified-live finding (pin `438198c`): `skill_inputs` is accepted, anonymized, and forwarded as `lq_ai_skill_inputs`, but the gateway assembler only substitutes `{{placeholder}}` tokens — and none of the 14 built-in skills use placeholders, so collected inputs (jurisdiction, audience, …) silently vanish. The doc records the evidence, a recommended fix (append unreferenced bound inputs as a labelled context block — smallest blast radius, backward-compatible), and a suggested test.

Docs-only; recorded so the request has a tracking home. **Likely warrants a DE or issue** for the actual fix — flagging for a maintainer call (didn't file one unilaterally).

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

entries.title: #216: docs: ADR 0018 (Citation Ledger) + fiduciary-grade Phase 1 spec/plan
entries.text:
docs: ADR 0018 (Citation Ledger) + fiduciary-grade Phase 1 spec/plan Evaluates the **fiduciary-grade agentic legal work** mini-PRD (LQ.AI's transparent answer to the next-gen CoCounsel), then specs + plans **Phase 1**. Planning docs only — no code.

## Evaluation (verified 2026-06-24 against `main`)

A four-front codebase pass confirms the mini-PRD's "the foundations are built" thesis. Load-bearing pieces verified present: the autonomous layer (`Phase`/`PHASE_GRANTS`/`guarded_tool_call`/R5→R6→R4), the chat tool-loop sharing one `governed_tool_invocation` path, the citation cascade with a **reusable** LLM-judge + char-precise offsets, the `MessageToolSource` source-kind model, counts-not-payloads audit logs (ADR 0016 P3 tripwire), and the web provenance primitives. The pre-req (PR5b + PR6/WS5 C4) is merged, so Phase 1 is unblocked.

Four corrections the inventory understated (now folded into the doc): brakes are autonomous-only; R4 is a no-op for external tools (DE-344); `MessageToolSource` is case-law-only (DE-350 pulled in-scope); **external/caselaw citations are not character-verified today** — the largest Phase 1 scope lever.

## Decisions taken (maintainer, 2026-06-24)

1. **External quotes verified in v1** (not provenance-only) — via materializing fetched source text as a citable source so the existing cascade verifies external quotes; shared substrate with DE-279/DE-280.
2. **Thin referencing ledger table** (`citation_ledger_entry`) — references existing rows by id/offset + a reserved treatment slot; P3-preserving.
3. **Phase 1 deep + ADR now; Phases 2–3 at roadmap resolution.**

## What's in this PR

- **`docs/adr/0018-citation-ledger-and-fiduciary-grade-output.md`** (proposal) — the Phase 1 centerpiece decision: data model (D1), external quote-verification (D2), the fiduciary-grade gate (D3), one-click trace (D4), no-raw-payload guarantee (D5), reserved WS-G slot (D6), retention (D7).
- **`docs/proposals/fiduciary-grade-agentic-legal-work.md`** — the mini-PRD, **relocated from repo root** (its `../`-relative links were written for `docs/proposals/`), inventory reconciled, decisions 5–7 recorded, and a **Phase 1 PR decomposition** (P1-0 → P1-A1 → P1-A2 → {P1-A3, P1-B1} → P1-C1) with sequencing, acceptance criteria, and security-review flags.

Also removed a stale `adr0016transparencyinvariants.patch` from the worktree (ADR 0016 + its CI test are already committed) — untracked, so not in the diff.

ADR 0018 is a **proposal for maintainer + security acceptance** (it introduces a content store derived from egress and touches the citation/audit surface); acceptance gates Phase 1 code.

entries.title: #215: docs(prd): DE-358 — tool-use hardening backlog from parallel PRs #185/#186
entries.text:
docs(prd): DE-358 — tool-use hardening backlog from parallel PRs #185/#186 Files **DE-358** in PRD §9 — six genuinely-additive items harvested from the parallel contributor PRs #185 (PR5b-i) and #186 (PR5b-ii) before they are closed as duplicative of merged `main`.

## Why

Both PRs rebuilt the PR5b governed chat tool-loop already on `main` (ADR 0015: the gateway Anthropic `tool_use` bridge, `api/app/chat/tool_loop.py`, the `chat_pending_tool_call` store + migration `0054`, the confirmation endpoint). Checked out + merged `main` into each locally:

- **#185** conflicts in 4/6 files (gateway adapters + tests) — convergent parallel rebuild.
- **#186** conflicts in 8 files incl. `tool_loop.py` (add/add). Hard blocker: its `0054_pending_tool_call.py` and main's `0054_chat_pending_tool_call.py` both declare `revision="0054"` off `0053` → Alembic multiple-heads. Its test guards also revert the PR6c `/sources` route (`134→133`).

Nothing is cherry-pickable cleanly, but the review surfaced six small, real gaps. DE-358 captures them to be **re-built against current `main`**, and gives the contributor a scoped follow-up to pick up.

## What's in DE-358

1. Gateway Anthropic **streaming** `tool_use` accumulation (latent — chat loop is non-streaming; verify reachability first).
2. OpenAPI doc gap for `tools`/`tool_choice` on the gateway `ChatCompletionRequest`.
3. `tools` count cap (defense-in-depth) on gateway + api boundary.
4. Granular `tool_choice` unit tests (`required`/`none`/forced/no-params).
5. Encryption-at-rest for the `chat_pending_tool_call` resume payload (optional hardening; main stores plaintext JSONB by design).
6. Api-side per-chat tier ceiling (already flagged in-code; tracked here).

Docs-only. Refs #185, #186.

entries.title: #213: docs: DE-357 (tool-call cap default) + sticky-skills frontend-integration note
entries.text:
docs: DE-357 (tool-call cap default) + sticky-skills frontend-integration note Docs-only follow-ups from the #207 work.

## DE-357 (PRD §9)
Files the last open #207 item (finding 1): the default chat tool-call cap of 8 is low for multi-call-per-case research skills (`case-law-research` runs several calls per case). Tracking issue **#212**. Notes it's a tuning/UX gap, not correctness (empty-answer-at-cap fixed in #208; env override works after #210), with options (raise default / per-skill cap / document tuning).

## New: `docs/contribute/sticky-skills-frontend-integration.md`
Generalizes the sticky-skills feature (#211) into an integration guide for **anyone building a frontend** against the LQ.AI backend — the OpenWebUI fork, the Donna shell, the Word add-in, or a custom client. Documents:
- the **backend contract** (`MessageCreate.set_sticky`, `Chat.sticky_skills`) and per-turn server behavior;
- the **design invariants** (off by default, explicit control, per-chat scope, audit-honest — per ADR 0016);
- the **reference `web/` implementation** + a porting checklist;
- canonical diff reference `5ad9f9e`.

Refs #207 #212

entries.title: #211: feat(chat): opt-in sticky-skills toggle (#207 finding 4)
entries.text:
feat(chat): opt-in sticky-skills toggle (#207 finding 4) Implements the opt-in **sticky skills** mechanism from #207 finding 4: a skill applied in a chat can persist to follow-up turns instead of dropping when the client stops re-sending it — but only when the user explicitly turns it on.

Designed to fit the transparency posture: **off by default (P4 fail-restrictive)**, **explicit user control (P8)**, per-chat scope that **resets for a new chat**, and every turn still records its own `applied_skills` so the audit trail stays honest.

## Behavior (per maintainer decisions)
- **Off by default**; a new chat never inherits stickiness.
- Toggle **on** snapshots *all skills currently applied* on that turn as the chat's sticky set.
- While on, the set is **unioned into every turn** (carried forward without re-sending).
- An explicit per-turn skill **unions for that turn only**; the sticky set is left unchanged (no silent re-snapshot).
- Toggle **off** clears the set.

## Backend (`api/`) — commit 1
- Migration **0056**: `chats.sticky_skills text[] NOT NULL DEFAULT '{}'` (empty = off).
- `Chat`/`ChatResponse` expose `sticky_skills`; `MessageCreateRequest.set_sticky` (true=snapshot, false=clear, null=unchanged).
- `send_message` unions the sticky set into the turn's effective skills (forwarded as `lq_ai_skills` + recorded on the user message).
- Docs: `backend-openapi.yaml` (both fields) + `db-schema.md` (the column) — P10 contract-is-truth.
- Tests: snapshot-on-toggle, carry-to-follow-up, union-with-set-unchanged, toggle-off-clears, GET exposes the set.

## Frontend (`web/`) — commit 2
- A **"Keep skills on"** toggle in the composer toolbar; mirrors the chat's persisted set, re-syncs on chat switch (id-tracked so it never clobbers an in-progress toggle), sends `set_sticky` only on a real change.
- `Chat.sticky_skills` + `MessageCreate.set_sticky` types.

## Testing
Local gates (Python 3.12.13 / ruff 0.15.17; throwaway pgvector): ruff + mypy clean, **74 api tests pass** (incl. 5 new), migration 0056 round-trips; **svelte-check 0 errors**, prettier/eslint clean on changed files. Migration does not touch the running launcher stack (released images); never ran host alembic on a live DB.

Refs #207

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

entries.title: #210: fix(chat): correct cap env-var alias + thread chat_id into tool-call audit (#207)
entries.text:
fix(chat): correct cap env-var alias + thread chat_id into tool-call audit (#207) Addresses two of the lower-priority follow-up findings from #207 (the empty-answer fix landed separately in #208). Both are correctness fixes with no runtime behaviour change beyond the audit row and the now-honored env var.

## (2) Cap env-var alias
`Settings.chat_tool_call_cap` is documented as overridable via `LQ_AI_CHAT_TOOL_CALL_CAP`, but `Settings` has no `env_prefix`, so only the bare `CHAT_TOOL_CALL_CAP` actually bound — the documented var silently did nothing. Adds a `validation_alias=AliasChoices("LQ_AI_CHAT_TOOL_CALL_CAP", "CHAT_TOOL_CALL_CAP")` (matching the autonomous-settings convention) so the documented name works, keeping the bare name for back-compat.

## (3) chat_id in the tool-call audit
`run_chat_tool_loop` never passed `chat_id` to `execute_tool`, so `tool_call_log.chat_id` was always NULL and chat-scoped audit queries missed the rows. `execute_tool` already accepts `chat_id`; this just threads it through the loop signature and all three call sites in `chats.py` (resume / non-streaming / streaming).

## Testing
- `test_config.py`: the documented `LQ_AI_` var is honored, and the bare name still binds.
- `test_tool_loop.py` (scenario k): the chat tool-call audit row is chat-scoped end to end.
- Local gates (Python 3.12.13, ruff 0.15.17): ruff format/check clean, mypy clean, 4 config + 12 tool-loop tests pass.

## Not in this PR
- Finding (1) (default cap of 8 is low for multi-call skills) — config/docs tuning, follow-up.
- Finding (4) (skills drop on follow-up turns) — being designed as an explicit, off-by-default per-chat "sticky skill" toggle (transparency/operator-control posture); separate PR.

Refs #207

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

entries.title: #209: Add transparency & governance invariants (ADR 0016) with CI gates
entries.text:
Add transparency & governance invariants (ADR 0016) with CI gates ## What this PR does

Names the ten invariants that constitute LQ.AI's transparency/auditability
posture (P1–P10) as a binding decision record (ADR 0016), and makes the
cheap-to-check ones enforceable in CI instead of relying on review culture
alone. Adds four structural gate tests and a transparency pre-flight
checklist to the PR template.

## Why

The posture that *is* the product — one audited egress boundary, closed-set
tool calling, counts/types-not-payloads audit, fail-restrictive defaults,
atomic audit — is currently enforced almost entirely by review culture
(CLAUDE.md, the ADRs, CODEOWNERS). Nothing in CI fails when a change quietly
violates it: a contributor (or a coding agent) could add a raw-payload column
to an audit table, make the backend call a third-party endpoint directly, or
commit an audit row outside its state-change transaction, and every existing
gate would stay green. This PR closes that gap for the invariants that are
cheap and unambiguous to check, and records the rest as binding-but-reviewed
with a follow-up list. Each invariant restates an existing decision (ADRs
0013/0014/0015 + the audit-logging contract) — this is a consolidation and a
hardening, not a new direction.

## What this PR does *not* do

- Does **not** change any runtime behaviour — no endpoints, models, or
  migrations.
- Does **not** add the heavier/security-gated gates (CODEOWNERS routing of the
  backend governance surface, audit-coverage gate, secret scanning, pip-audit,
  coverage no-decrease, mypy --strict for `api/app/tools/`). Those touch
  `.github/workflows/**` or need maintainer buy-in and are recorded as
  follow-ups in ADR 0016 §Follow-ups.

## Type of change

- [x] Documentation update
- [x] Test / CI / tooling

## Testing

- [x] Unit tests added/updated
- [ ] Integration tests added/updated (if applicable)
- [x] Manually tested against a real deployment (describe scenario below)
- [ ] Acceptance test plan updated (if changing skill behavior)

<details>
<summary>Manual testing notes</summary>

Ran the new gates under the CI-pinned toolchain (Python 3.12, ruff 0.15.17):

- `pytest tests/test_transparency_invariants.py` → 4 passed.
- `ruff format --check` + `ruff check` on the new file → clean.
- Confirmed each detector fires on a real violation (a `request_body` column,
  an in-helper `.commit()`, a non-allowlisted `import httpx`) and does not
  false-positive on the legitimate columns/imports present today
  (`args_digest`, `details`, the gateway client).

</details>

## Documentation

- [ ] PRD updated (if user-facing behavior changes) — n/a, no user-facing change
- [ ] README updated (if quickstart or capability list changes) — n/a
- [ ] Skill-authoring guide updated — n/a
- [ ] Deployment cookbook updated — n/a
- [ ] Compliance documentation updated — n/a (candidate to map P1–P10 into the
      Compliance Pack later)
- [ ] OpenAPI schema regenerated — n/a, no endpoints changed

ADR 0016 is the documentation deliverable of this PR.

## Transparency & governance invariants

<!-- This PR adds the invariants; it does not exercise the runtime surfaces
they govern, so most are n/a here. -->

- [ ] **Egress (P1):** n/a — adds the enforcing test, no new egress.
- [ ] **Closed set (P2):** n/a.
- [ ] **No raw payloads (P3):** n/a — adds the enforcing test, no new rows/logs.
- [ ] **Fail restrictive (P4):** n/a.
- [ ] **Atomic audit (P5):** n/a — adds the enforcing test, no new writes.
- [ ] **One governance path (P6):** n/a.
- [ ] **Human gate (P7):** n/a.
- [ ] **Operator control (P8):** n/a.
- [ ] **User owns data (P9):** n/a.
- [x] **Contract is truth (P10):** the relevant ADR (0016) is added in this PR;
      OpenAPI/DB schema unchanged.

## DCO sign-off

- [x] All commits in this PR are signed off per the DCO

## Related issues

<!-- Link any tracking issue if one exists. -->

## Reviewer notes

- The two structural scanners are deliberately conservative to avoid the
  "gate with false positives gets disabled" failure mode: the egress check
  targets *import statements* (not the word "requests" in prose), and the
  payload-column check matches column-name patterns with an explicit
  `_ALLOWED_CONTENT_COLUMNS` escape hatch. Both are green on current `main`.
- The commit detector is AST-based on purpose — `app/audit.py`'s docstring
  documents the caller's `db.commit()`, which a grep would false-positive on.
- ADR status is **Proposed**; I'd value a maintainer call on whether to move it
  to **Accepted** in this PR or a follow-up, and on the §Follow-ups items
  (especially the CODEOWNERS routing of `api/app/tools/` +
  `api/app/autonomous/guard.py` to `@legalquants/security`).

entries.title: #208: fix(chat): write a final answer when the tool-loop hits the call cap
entries.text:
fix(chat): write a final answer when the tool-loop hits the call cap ## What this PR does

Fixes the chat tool-loop so it writes a real final answer when a turn exhausts `chat_tool_call_cap`, instead of persisting an empty assistant message.

## Why

Closes #207.

The forced final round disabled tools (`tool_choice="none"`) but re-sent the message list verbatim, with no instruction to stop calling tools and synthesize. A research turn that hit the cap mid-research therefore returned ~empty content and persisted a zero-length assistant message (observed live: 25 tool calls, 121 sources collected, final round `tokens_out=9`, `length(content)=0`).

This adds `_final_synthesis_round()`, which appends an explicit synthesis directive to a **copy** of the message list before the no-tools round, so the model composes its answer from what it already gathered. Appending to a copy keeps the directive out of the persisted turn history. It is wired into all three forced-synthesis exit points (cap-before-loop, all-hallucination guard, cap-after-increment).

## What this PR does *not* do

Deliberately scoped to the empty-answer bug. Out of scope (noted as follow-ups in #207):
- Raising the default cap (`8`) for multi-call-per-case skills.
- The `config.py` docstring naming the override `LQ_AI_CHAT_TOOL_CALL_CAP` (real var: `CHAT_TOOL_CALL_CAP`).
- `tool_call_log.chat_id` landing NULL (`execute_tool` called without `chat_id=`).
- `applied_skills` emptying on follow-up turns.

## Type of change

- [x] Bug fix (non-breaking change that fixes an issue)

## Testing

- [x] Unit test added (`test_loop_cap_final_round_injects_synthesis_directive`): asserts the cap-triggered final round disables tools, appends a `role="user"` synthesis directive as the last message, and that the directive does **not** leak into the persisted turn.
- [x] Manually tested against a real deployment: a cap-hit (25-call) `case-law-research` turn went from a **0-character** answer to a full **8,305-character** report.

> ⚠️ **Reviewer note (transparency):** I could not run the test suite or linters locally — the api image is runtime-only (no pytest/ruff, no `tests/` baked in) and I didn't have a dev env handy. CI will be the first real run of the new test. Please give it a look there.

## DCO sign-off

- [x] All commits are signed off per the DCO.

## Related issues

Closes #207

## Reviewer notes

Two design choices I'd specifically welcome your call on:
- The wording of the synthesis directive (`_SYNTHESIS_DIRECTIVE`).
- Whether the directive should be a `role="user"` turn (current approach) vs. a system message — `user` was the most reliable steer in testing, but you know the gateway/Anthropic bridge better than I do.

entries.title: #206: feat(ingest): accept text/markdown uploads (DE-332)
entries.text:
feat(ingest): accept text/markdown uploads (DE-332) <!-- Paste into the PR body (their PULL_REQUEST_TEMPLATE.md). Fill #NN with the claim-issue number. -->

## What this PR does

Adds a plain-text / Markdown ingest path. `.md` and `.txt` uploads now ingest into a knowledge base through the same chunk → embed → cite pipeline that PDFs use, via a new `parse_text` branch alongside `parse_pdf`. The decoded bytes are stored **verbatim** as the canonical character stream.

## Why

**Refs DE-332.** The pipeline was PDF-only — every non-PDF was rejected as `unsupported_type` before a parser ran, so a user couldn't add plain text or Markdown (including the project's own skill files and autonomous-layer memos) to a KB.

The design choice that matters: storing the text verbatim means `documents.normalized_content` equals the uploaded bytes exactly, so the Citation Engine's **exact-match tier resolves byte-for-byte** — the direct defense against quote-misattribution (a quote that *looks* sourced but was silently altered). For text this is *higher* fidelity than the PDF path, which can introduce extraction artifacts. `parse_text` is pure stdlib: no model, no OCR, no network — deterministic, and runs identically air-gapped.

This is the **trivial, dependency-free sibling of the DOCX work in #198**; the `is_X_mime`/`parse_X` dispatch composes with a future `parse_docx`.

## What this PR does *not* do

- **No DOCX** (separate, heavier effort — #198 / its ADR).
- **No in-app text preview** — `.md`/`.txt` is downloadable but the viewer is PDF-only (a raw-text viewer is a follow-up; if added it must show Markdown *raw*, never render to HTML).
- **No structure-aware Markdown chunking** — a long table can split across chunks, identical to the PDF path (follow-up DE).
- **No charset guessing** — UTF-16 / Latin-1 / Word exports are rejected as `decode_error` with a "save as UTF-8" message (deliberate: guessing risks silent mis-decode → wrong citations).
- **No new dependency, no DB migration, no `gateway`/authz/secrets/CI/infra changes.** The PDF path is byte-identical (the gate is *widened*, not rerouted; zero deletions). Per `docs/security/external-contribution-vetting.md` this should be a "normal read," not the supply-chain playbook.

## Type of change

- [x] New feature (non-breaking change that adds capability)
- [x] Documentation update
- [ ] Bug fix / Breaking change / Skill / Refactor

## Testing

- [x] Unit tests added — `api/tests/test_pipeline_parsers_text.py` (fitz-free; verbatim store, BOM, unicode byte-fidelity, empty→0 chunks, **non-UTF-8 → `decode_error`**, **NUL-byte → `decode_error`**, the offset-fidelity invariant, the `.md`/`.txt` extension fallback, and **adversarial-input regression** locking in that pathological text can't hang the chunker).
- [x] Integration tests added — `api/tests/test_pipeline_ingest.py`: Markdown happy-path (`normalized_content == source` verbatim, chunks slice back, `parser="plain-text"`, `page_count=1`, `was_ocrd=False`); non-UTF-8 → `failed`/`decode_error` with no Document persisted; **`.md` sent as `application/octet-stream` routes by extension** (the common browser case).
- [ ] Manually tested against a real deployment — *not yet; flagging honestly. Happy to run a live smoke if useful.*

<details><summary>Robustness notes (what I checked beyond the happy path)</summary>

- **NUL byte:** valid Unicode but unstorable in a Postgres `text` column — would strand the row mid-ingest. Rejected up front as `decode_error`.
- **`.md` MIME variance:** browsers/OSes frequently send `application/octet-stream` (or empty) for `.md`; routing falls back to the filename extension, with `parse_text`'s UTF-8/NUL validation as the safety net (a binary renamed `.md` fails cleanly).
- **DoS / ReDoS:** a `.txt` is attacker-controlled content flowing into the chunker. Both chunker regexes are backtracking-free and the chunk loop is guaranteed to advance — a 100MB pathological file processes in linear time (~0.01s). Regression-tested.
- **No migration:** writes only existing columns (`normalized_content`/`was_ocrd`, etc.). **Re-ingest** is the existing atomic delete-then-insert — format-agnostic, no duplication.
- **Known pre-existing limitation (not changed here):** embedding runs as a separate post-ingest job, so a doc can be `ready` (keyword-searchable) before semantic embeddings complete — format-agnostic, surfaced in KB-detail.
</details>

## Documentation

- [x] PRD updated (DE-332 marked shipped)
- [x] OpenAPI accepted-types / error descriptions (+`decode_error`)
- [x] HONEST-STATE updated (ingest-formats row)
- [x] `parsers.py` / `ingest.py` module docstrings reconciled (no longer "PDF-only")
- [ ] README / deployment / compliance — n/a

## DCO sign-off

- [x] All commits signed off per the DCO.

## Principles self-check (per the Part-2 contributor prompt)

1. **Egress** — n/a: no outbound call; `parse_text` is pure stdlib decode.
2. **Closed set** — n/a: adds no model-/autonomous-invokable capability or hook (the accepted types are a closed, code-defined set).
3. **Payloads in logs** — yes: log lines/rows carry MIME + outcome only; **never the filename or document content**.
4. **Fail restrictive** — yes, and the failure paths are tested: unknown type → `unsupported_type`; bad encoding / NUL → `decode_error`; a binary renamed `.md` is validated then rejected.
5. **Atomicity** — n/a: reuses the existing persist/commit boundary unchanged.
6. **Reuse the governance path** — n/a: no tier/cost/audit logic added.
7. **Irreversibility** — n/a: ingest is reversible; nothing destructive.
8. **Operator control** — n/a: file-format support is core ingest, not a toggleable capability (neither is PDF).
9. **User data** — yes: verbatim inbound text *because citation grounding requires it* (P9); export/delete are format-agnostic.
10. **Contract & docs** — yes: OpenAPI + HONEST-STATE + PRD updated in this PR. The one behavior choice beyond the DE's literal "by MIME" — routing `.md`/`.txt` by filename extension when the MIME is generic (browsers send `.md` inconsistently) — is surfaced here for you to ratify or ask me to drop.
11. **Tests as part of the change** — yes: the offset-fidelity test is a security-relevant invariant that *fails* if violated; plus adversarial-input regression (a `.txt` is attacker-controlled content into the chunker).
12. **No black box** — yes: a citation resolves against the verbatim source at exact offsets, and the parser is open and inspectable.

## Related issues

Refs **DE-332** (PRD §9) · sibling of #198 (DOCX ingest) — happy to sequence behind/ahead of the DOCX work as you prefer.

## Reviewer notes

Scope is deliberately minimal and additive; the verification-critical guarantee (verbatim canonical text → exact-match citations) is encoded as the offset-fidelity test, not asserted in prose. Files touched: `api/app/pipeline/{parsers,ingest,__init__}.py`, the two test files, `web/.../knowledge/[id]/+page.svelte` (one `accept` attribute), and docs. No `gateway`/authz/secrets. Glad to split the `web/` accept-attribute change out if you'd prefer it separate.

entries.text:
chore(release): bump versions + runbook for the BYOK re-cut (v0.5.1 / desktop-v0.5.2) Prep for re-cutting the release so the merged BYOK launcher fix (#202) reaches users.

## Why
`v0.5.0` images and the `desktop-v0.5.1` `.dmg` predate #202, so a fresh `.dmg` still can't make chat work. Re-cut needs new tags on both pipelines.

## Changes
- `api`/`gateway` `__version__` 0.5.0 → **0.5.1** (next image tag `v0.5.1`).
- `desktop` 0.5.1 → **0.5.2** (`desktop-v0.5.1` already tagged; next `desktop-v0.5.2`); `package-lock` kept in sync.
- New **`docs/LQVern/HANDOFF-2026-06-21-byok-recut.md`** — exact tag commands (images first, then desktop), pre-reqs, and the post-cut fresh-install verification gate.

No code change. The bundled `docker-compose.release.yml` (master-key forwarding) is regenerated at dist via `prepack:compose`, so a `.dmg` built from `main` ships it.

## After merge — the two release tags
```
git tag v0.5.1 && git push origin v0.5.1            # release.yml → images (+ :latest)
git tag desktop-v0.5.2 && git push origin desktop-v0.5.2   # desktop-release.yml → signed .dmg
```

entries.title: #202: feat(launcher+web): self-service BYOK provider keys for a fresh .dmg install
entries.text:
feat(launcher+web): self-service BYOK provider keys for a fresh .dmg install ## Problem

A stranger who installs the macOS `.dmg` can log in but **cannot make chat work**. We hit this during the v0.5.0 release-readiness gate:

- the first-run wizard never collected a provider key and `renderEnv` never wrote one;
- there is no in-app provider-keys page;
- the launcher never minted `LQ_AI_GATEWAY_MASTER_KEY`, so the runtime BYOK store (gateway + backend — already fully built) hard-failed with `400 failed_precondition`.

Net: no key-entry path at all. This revises **launcher decision L-3** and ships **both** key paths end-to-end.

## What changed

**Launcher (`desktop/`)**
- Mint `LQ_AI_GATEWAY_MASTER_KEY` as a **Fernet key** (urlsafe-base64 of 32 bytes — byte-identical to Python's `Fernet.generate_key()`), forward it to the gateway in the release compose.
- First-run wizard gains **one optional key field**, auto-detecting Anthropic (`sk-ant-…`) vs OpenAI by prefix → written to `.env`. Validates whitespace + `sk-` prefix.
- The key is written to `.env` but **stripped from the persisted (encrypted) config blob**.
- **Migration**: `ensureMasterKey()` at launch backfills the master key into installs that predate it — **append-only**, so a hand-added provider key is preserved.

**Web (`web/`)**
- New admin **Provider keys** page: list status (last-4, runtime/.env source) + add/replace + revoke. Calls the existing `/api/v1/admin/provider-keys` proxy.

**Docs**
- Revise L-3 in the launcher plan; make `INSTALL-MAC.md` §3/§5 accurate (both paths real); add the master key to `.env.release.example`; file **DE-355** (ingestion needs an OpenAI key specifically — the `embedding` alias is OpenAI-only).

## Verification

- **Runtime path proven live against the shipping gateway image**: `POST` → `source: runtime` + last4; `DELETE` → 204 + cleared; chat still routes via the env key. Default `smart` chat works with **either** an Anthropic key (primary) or an OpenAI key (fallback — confirmed the router skips keyless providers).
- Desktop **60** tests + web provider-keys **8** tests green; `svelte-check` 0 errors; ESLint + Prettier clean.

## Note for reviewers

Touches **cryptographic key material** (minting/forwarding a Fernet master key in the launcher) — flagging for security attention. No `gateway/**` code changed (only the release compose + docs).

The bundled `.dmg` still needs a **re-cut** (new compose + secrets + web bundle) to deliver these to end users — that's the release-tag step, separate from this PR. The `launcher-wizard.png` screenshot also needs regenerating at re-cut (marked with a TODO in the doc).

Refs DE-355

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

entries.title: #200: fix(launcher): make the macOS first-run work for a stranger (DE-353 + DE-354)
entries.text:
fix(launcher): make the macOS first-run work for a stranger (DE-353 + DE-354) The v0.5.0 launcher (`.dmg`) first-run failed with **"Stack failed to start"** → api `password authentication failed` crash-loop. Root-caused to two compounding bugs (the published **images are fine** — Arm B + isolated-boot passed).

### DE-353 — web blocked boot on an unnecessary HF model fetch (RESOLVED)
`web/backend/open_webui/main.py` builds a **local** SentenceTransformer at import (default empty `RAG_EMBEDDING_ENGINE`) → "Fetching 30 files" from HF **before** binding `:8080`. Under HF rate-limiting this stalls for minutes → `web` goes `unhealthy` after its 20s start_period → the launcher (which fails on any `unhealthy` service) gives up. LQ.AI uses its **own** embeddings, so the fetch is waste.
**Fix:** `RAG_EMBEDDING_ENGINE=openai` (non-empty sentinel) on the `web` service in both compose files → `get_ef()` returns `None`, no local model. **Empirically verified** on `lq-ai-web:v0.5.0`: `/health` → 200 in **~11s** (vs never/183s+). Compose-only; **no image re-cut** (the `.dmg` bundles `docker-compose.release.yml` at build).

### DE-354 — launcher regenerated secrets on retry without `down -v` (RESOLVED)
`wizard:complete` (`desktop/src/main/index.ts`) calls `generateSecrets()` each run, only `saveConfig`s on success, and `down -v` was wired only to Reset. So a failed first attempt orphaned a postgres volume with password A; the retry used password B → Postgres (init'd with A, no re-init on existing volume) → auth crash-loop, unrecoverable without manual Reset.
**Fix:** `resetStack()` (`down -v`) before `startStack()` in the first-run wizard — fresh secrets always meet a fresh postgres init (safe; no real data on first-run).

### Validation
Desktop gates pass locally: `tsc --noEmit` clean, `vitest` 47/47. Main CI here covers api/gateway/web (unaffected). After merge: re-cut the launcher (`desktop-v0.5.1`) to pick up the bundled compose + launcher fix, then re-verify the `.dmg` first-run.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

entries.title: #199: docs(prd): file DE-353 — web/OpenWebUI first-boot HF model fetch blocks the user-facing proxy
entries.text:
docs(prd): file DE-353 — web/OpenWebUI first-boot HF model fetch blocks the user-facing proxy Files **DE-353** from the v0.5.0 release-readiness verification. On a fresh deploy the `web` (OpenWebUI) container downloads ~30 HF files (its default local RAG embedding + Whisper models) before binding `:8080`, so the user-facing `proxy` is gated ~10 min until the fetch completes (self-heals; not a hard failure). LQ.AI uses its own embeddings, so the fetch is unnecessary — fix is to disable/redirect OpenWebUI's local model engines or bake the models. Distinct from DE-351 (ingest-worker docling). Doc-only.

entries.title: #198: docs: DOCX-ingest mini-PRD + ADR 0014 (proposal for maintainer decision)
entries.text:
docs: DOCX-ingest mini-PRD + ADR 0014 (proposal for maintainer decision) ## What this is

A **Proposed** mini-PRD + companion ADR to bring `.docx` files into the ingest pipeline (today PDF-only). **Docs only — no code, no migration, no new routes.** This is a **decision request, not a merge** — per [`coding-agent-onboarding` §8](docs/contribute/coding-agent-onboarding.md), it carries two maintainer "stop and ask" calls, which is the whole reason it's a Draft.

## The two decisions to approve

1. **New runtime dependency — Pandoc** in the `api` + `ingest-worker` images. A single mature binary invoked at arm's length via subprocess (GPL-via-subprocess = mere aggregation; the Apache-2.0 code is unaffected). In-repo precedent: `web/` already ships `pypandoc`.
2. **Redline policy — "retain tracked changes + cite accordingly":** canonical text = the changes-accepted final (offset-fidelity preserved, Citation Engine unchanged); insertions / deletions / comments are retained in `structured_content` and emitted as separately-citable provenance records.

## What's in here

- **Mini-PRD** — `docs/contribute/mini-prds/docx-ingest-support.md`: scope, acceptance checklist, scope cuts, where-to-start, definition of "merged".
- **ADR 0014** — `docs/adr/0014-docx-ingest-via-pandoc.md`: Pandoc primary for the canonical character stream; OOXML/`lxml` fallback only for the documented [pandoc #9833](https://github.com/jgm/pandoc/issues/9833) comment-on-insertion gap; alternatives (mammoth / OOXML-only / Docling / LibreOffice) considered with rationale; license posture mirrored on [ADR 0006](docs/adr/0006-document-pipeline-architecture.md).

## Evidence behind it

Backed by a Phase-0 spike on Pandoc 3.7: the tracked-change span syntax and comment reading were confirmed empirically, the accept-reconstruction was validated byte-identical to canonical, and determinism was confirmed on a pinned version. Prior art reviewed: `AnttiHero/lavern` (mammoth — drops redlines/comments) and `willchen96/mike` (OOXML-direct but AGPL — idea borrowed, code not).

## The ask

Approve the dependency + redline policy, or request changes, **before** any build. If approved, the build is a **separate PR** that adds `parse_docx()`, the OOXML fallback, the tests, and the pinned Pandoc — gated on the acceptance checklist in the mini-PRD.

Refs DE-332.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

entries.title: #196: fix(ingest): mark file failed on parse timeout instead of stuck in processing (DE-351)
entries.text:
fix(ingest): mark file failed on parse timeout instead of stuck in processing (DE-351) ## DE-351 — first-run document ingestion left files stuck in `processing`

Found during the **v0.5.0 release-readiness fresh-clone verification**.

**Symptom:** on a fresh deployment the *first* PDF upload fails — docling downloads ~696 MB of HuggingFace layout/OCR models on first use, which exceeds the arq `job_timeout` (`lq_ai_docling_timeout_seconds`, default 300 s). arq hard-kills the worker mid-parse (`CancelledError`), which bypasses the `except ParserError` handlers in `ingest_file`, so the file is left stuck in `processing` with an empty `ingestion_error`. A UI polling for `ready` then spins forever. (A second upload, models now cached, ingests in <6 s — the pipeline itself is sound.)

**Fix (the stuck-state half of DE-351):**
- Wrap the `parse_pdf` thread call in an in-job `asyncio.wait_for` soft timeout (`lq_ai_docling_timeout_seconds`). On `TimeoutError`, mark the file `failed` with `ingestion_error='ingestion_timeout'` and return a failed `IngestResult` — a terminal, visible state.
- Bump arq's hard `job_timeout` to `soft + 60 s` so the soft path wins the race and can persist the row before arq kills the worker.
- The orphaned worker thread finishes its download in the background, so a retry once models are cached succeeds.

**Regression test:** `test_ingest_parse_timeout_marks_failed` — a parse that blocks past a tiny soft timeout flips the row to `failed`/`ingestion_timeout` (not stuck in `processing`).

**Out of scope (deferred, the larger half of DE-351):** pre-warming / baking docling models into the `ingest-worker` image so the first PDF doesn't time out at all. Tracked in PRD §9 DE-351.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

entries.title: #195: v0.5.0 release-readiness: reconcile honesty docs to the legal-research+MCP milestone + fix CourtListener gateway wiring
entries.text:
v0.5.0 release-readiness: reconcile honesty docs to the legal-research+MCP milestone + fix CourtListener gateway wiring ## Release-readiness verification gate (post-WS5, pre-v0.5.0). Head \`0055\`.

Drives the two-arm "100% honest" gate from \`docs/LQVern/HANDOFF-2026-06-20-release-readiness-verification.md\`.

### Arm A — claims-vs-reality audit (6 parallel auditors, one per capability cluster)
**Verdict: the code is honest and works.** Every headline claim verified TRUE with no overreach — the egress boundary is clean (api has exactly one outbound httpx client, pointed at the gateway; zero direct external calls), the 4-stage citation cascade matches, the confirmation gate genuinely fires (MCP tools default \`requires_confirmation=True\`), tier-floor returns 403 \`tier_below_minimum\`, anonymization round-trips with a no-leak-to-logs test, and OAuth tokens are Fernet-at-rest and never logged.

**The drift was documentation**, pinned at the M4 close (head 0047) and omitting the entire milestone:
- **HONEST-STATE.md** — bump to head 0055; add **§5.5** cataloguing the milestone; **remove the now-false §6 "MCP-client subsystem deferred-M5 / grep empty" row** (the grep returns 27 files); broaden §2 to tool egress; recount test files (api 144→183, gateway 41→64, vitest 71→76).
- **README.md** — resolve the internal contradiction (line 83 said MCP shipped; status/roadmap/PrivacyQuant lines still called it M5+/remaining).
- **PRD.md** — DE-200 (MCP) → Delivered; fix §3.10/§8.5 present-tense "remains deferred".
- **proposals/** — "Delivered"/"Resolved" banners ("only a stub exists" is now false).

### Arm B — honest fresh-clone bring-up
Fresh clone → \`docker compose up -d --build\` → **built from source and self-migrated 0001→0055**. Verified **live** on the fresh stack: real Anthropic chat (stream + persist), **tier-floor 403**, skills incl. \`case-law-research\` \`tool_usage\`, **KB → exact-match-verified citation** (confidence 1.0), privacy-safe audit log.

**Bug found + fixed:** case-law was dark on a fresh clone — \`docker-compose.yml\` never forwarded \`COURTLISTENER_API_TOKEN\` into the gateway container, silently breaking the documented (\`.env\` + \`gateway.yaml\`) enable-path. **Fixed the passthrough and re-verified live**: capabilities \`enabled:true\` → real CourtListener retrieval → \`message_tool_sources\` → \`/sources\` (20 real cases) → \`tool_call_log\` (executed) → \`tool_egress_log\` (audited). Added a README "Enabling legal-research connectors" opt-in section.

**Filed:** DE-351 (first-run docling model-download timeout leaves files stuck in \`processing\`), DE-352 (pre-upload format guard). Added the macOS launcher / GHCR playbook for the mechanical gate.

### Tag posture
**Do not tag v0.5.0 from this PR.** The written readiness verdict is for maintainer review; tag only after the mechanical gate (GHCR + launcher + external-user verify) per the handoff.

entries.title: #193: PR6e/WS5: retire the OpenWebUI MCP stub (DE-341)
entries.text:
PR6e/WS5: retire the OpenWebUI MCP stub (DE-341) ## What this is

The milestone's final code change — retires the legacy OpenWebUI MCP client stub (`web/backend/open_webui/utils/mcp/client.py`) and every importer, closing **DE-341**.

## Why

The stub let the **web backend** open a direct MCP connection to an external server — bypassing the Inference Gateway, which ADR 0014 establishes as the *sole* MCP-protocol egress. LQ.AI's real connector model is operator-configured `mcp.yaml`, gateway-brokered, with per-user OAuth (PR4–5). Removing the stub **reduces external-egress attack surface** and aligns the web backend with the boundary. This is a security-*positive* change.

## What changed

- **Deleted** the stub (`utils/mcp/client.py`, the only file in `utils/mcp/`).
- **`configs.py`** (`POST /configs/tool_servers/verify`): removed the `MCPClient` import + the entire `type=='mcp'` branch; an MCP-typed verify now returns a clear *"MCP tool servers are configured by the operator via the gateway, not added here"* (400). The OpenAPI tool-server verify path is untouched.
- **`middleware.py`**: removed the `MCPClient` import + the `mcp_clients`/`mcp_tools_dict` chat-loop branch (dead code in LQ.AI — chat runs through the `api/` service + gateway, not `web/backend`) + its merge.
- **`main.py`**: removed the request-teardown `metadata['mcp_clients']` disconnect block (the cross-file consumer).
- **`AddToolServerModal.svelte`**: the add-tool-server "Type" row is now a static **OpenAPI** label — no MCP toggle. (Minimal scope: the now-unreachable MCP form markup is left in place; a fuller modal cleanup can follow.)
- **PRD §9:** DE-341 marked resolved.

## Scope / non-goals

- **MCP-only removal** — the OpenAPI tool-server paths in both the verify endpoint and the middleware loop are intact.
- **LQ.AI's real MCP flow is untouched:** `api/app/mcp/**`, `gateway/app/providers/tool/mcp.py`, the per-user OAuth (PR4c), `mcp.yaml`, and `api/app/chat/tool_loop.py`.

## Verification

- `grep -rn "MCPClient|utils.mcp.client|mcp_clients|mcp_tools_dict" web/backend/ web/src/` → **clean**.
- `python3 -m py_compile` clean on all three edited backend files (note: `web/backend/` has no CI Python gate — repo ruff excludes `web/` — so this was verified manually).
- svelte-check 0 errors; web Vitest **712 passed**; full **SvelteKit production build succeeds** (compiles the fork component CI doesn't typecheck).
- Not CODEOWNERS security-gated (`web/backend/open_webui/**` + `web/src/**` + docs) → self-merge after CI.

Closes the milestone's code; next is the release-readiness verification + tag.

entries.title: #192: PR6d/WS5: case-law-research skill + C5 tool-usage (declare & surface) + milestone honesty pass
entries.text:
PR6d/WS5: case-law-research skill + C5 tool-usage (declare & surface) + milestone honesty pass ## What this is

The milestone's final **capability** PR: ships the `case-law-research` skill, makes a skill's declared tool usage **load-bearing but non-gating** (the C5 "declare & surface" decision), and runs the closing **D6 honesty pass**.

### 1. The `case-law-research` skill
A **procedural** skill (`skills/case-law-research/`) that drives the governed CourtListener tool chain (`search_case_law → get_cluster → read_opinion / find_in_case`), grounds every statement in retrieved text (the PR6c "Sources consulted" panel shows the provenance), reports gaps honestly, and states its scope limits explicitly — US case law via CourtListener only, **not** a citator. It asserts *method*, not legal positions, which is what permits self-merge (an independent review + the controller confirmed the body stays procedural).

### 2. C5 — `tool_usage` declare & surface
- A skill declares the connectors it uses in frontmatter (`tool_usage: [courtlistener]`). It's parsed into the schema and echoed on the skill summary/detail.
- The skill-detail endpoint computes `unavailable_tool_usage` by checking the declaration against the operator's actually-configured connectors (gateway CourtListener capability ∪ configured MCP servers).
- **Never gating, never 500:** the skill always loads/lists/runs; if availability can't be determined (gateway unreachable), the field degrades to `null` — we surface "uses" without crying wolf. The skill-detail UI shows a non-blocking "Uses: …" line and, only on a real mismatch, an amber "not configured" note. Nothing is disabled.

### 3. D6 honesty pass
Filed **DE-350** (generic-MCP-result provenance — extend `message_tool_sources` to `source_kind='mcp'`), fixed the dangling **DE-360** reference (repo-wide, incl. a `.py` straggler), **dropped** the now-stale "coming next" forward promise from the three narrative surfaces (explorer Availability, Learn §17, README — the skill is shipped; the internal DE-341 cleanup isn't a user-facing promise), documented `tool_usage` as **surfaced-not-enforced**, and corrected two stale status claims (PRD §3.6, boundary-registers).

## Scope / non-goals

- **DE-341 stub retirement is OUT** → its own follow-up **6e** (a behavioral `web/backend/` migration, isolated so it can't muddy the release-gate bring-up).
- No migration / no DB change (`tool_usage` is frontmatter, `unavailable_tool_usage` is computed). **Not security-gated** (skill content + `api/app/skills/**` + `web/` + docs) → self-merge after CI.

## Verification

- Backend: ruff + mypy clean; 56 PR6d tests pass against a throwaway pg (schema parse, the resolver's three states incl. None-on-error, the endpoint's never-gating/null-degrade, skill registration).
- Frontend: svelte-check 0 errors; full lq-ai Vitest suite 498 green; the 4-state skill-detail note visually rendered.
- Built subagent-driven (6 tasks, per-task spec+quality review); final whole-branch review: **Ready to merge — Yes** (procedural-skill gate confirmed; the two load-bearing safety properties verified on every path).

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

entries.title: #191: PR6c/WS5: external-source citations — case-law retrieval provenance
entries.text:
PR6c/WS5: external-source citations — case-law retrieval provenance ## What this is

When a chat turn consults CourtListener case law through the governed tool-loop, PR6c records **which cases it pulled in** as persisted provenance and surfaces them in the chat — answering *"where did this answer's research come from?"* right where the user reads it.

- **New `message_tool_sources` table** (migration 0055) — one row per retrieved case (`label`, `subtitle` court·date, `url`, `external_ref`, `provider`, `tool`), tied to the assistant message. This is **retrieval-provenance** ("sources consulted"), kept deliberately separate from the verification-centric `message_citations` (which is untouched).
- **Capture in the tool-loop** — a pure `extract_tool_sources` maps each `search_case_law`/`get_cluster` result into source records; `run_chat_tool_loop` accumulates them deduped by `external_ref`, and `_persist_message_tool_sources` writes them at turn-end alongside the existing citation-persist sites. Paused turns (`LoopConfirmation`/`LoopMcpAuth`) carry none.
- **Read endpoint** `GET /api/v1/chats/{chat_id}/messages/{message_id}/sources` — mirrors the `get_citations` ownership/404 pattern; `[]` for turns with none.
- **Chat UI** — `MessageBubble` lazy-fetches sources post-stream (same guarded pattern as citations, no SSE change), rendering a `⚖ N sources consulted` provenance pill + a collapsible "Sources consulted" sidecar (`ToolSourcesPanel`) listing each case with its CourtListener link. External strings are plain interpolation (never `{@html}`).

## D6 narrative honesty

Flipped the three centralized availability claims (governed-tool-flow explorer, Learn §17, README) so case-law provenance is **shipped**; "coming next" now points to **6d** (the case-law research skill + retiring the legacy OpenWebUI MCP stub).

## Scope / non-goals

- **Case-law tools only** (`search_case_law`, `get_cluster`) — generic MCP provenance is deferred as **DE-360**.
- Retrieval-provenance only (no claim-level marker grounding, no verification path); inline sidecar only (no chat-wide drawer); no new SSE frames; no cost model.
- **Not security-gated** — a new product table + a read endpoint in `api/`, plus `web/` + docs; no `gateway/**`, audit-log, auth, or crypto. Self-merge after CI.

## Verification

- Backend: ruff + mypy clean; 10 PR6c tests incl. the OpenAPI collision guards (path count 133→134) pass against a throwaway pg; migration 0055 is a single reversible head.
- Frontend: svelte-check 0 errors; full lq-ai Vitest suite 494 green; `ToolSourcesPanel` visually rendered (pill + expanded/collapsed states).
- Built subagent-driven (8 tasks, per-task spec+quality review); final whole-branch review: **Ready to merge — Yes** (no Critical/Important). One known cosmetic nuance: a case with no `external_ref` (a named cluster lacking a CourtListener id — vanishingly rare) bypasses dedup.

entries.title: #190: Add redfern-schedule skill (Redfern Schedule for arbitration document production)
entries.text:
Add redfern-schedule skill (Redfern Schedule for arbitration document production) ## Summary

Adds **`redfern-schedule`**, a skill that builds and maintains the **Redfern Schedule** (the request-to-produce table used to organise document production in international arbitration) for three roles from one artefact: the **requesting party**, the **producing party**, and the **tribunal**.

What it does:
- Applies the **IBA Rules on the Taking of Evidence (2020)** Article 3.3 admissibility checklist (Gate A identification, Gate B relevance-and-materiality, Gate C possession) and the Article 9.2 objection grounds.
- Surfaces a **content-based Article 9.2(f)** political/institutional-sensitivity prompt for State and state-owned parties (only where a document's content implicates a governmental function, not merely because of ownership).
- Produces an **internal flags memo** naming the user's own weak requests or non-colourable objections, marked privileged and never served.
- Holds **column-ownership and verbatim carry-forward discipline** across rounds (requesting writes its columns, producing writes Objections, tribunal column stays blank until ruling).
- **Regime-aware:** IBA 2020 default, plus Prague 2018, ICC, LCIA, and ICSID variants.

It **enforces form and the admissibility checklist; the legal calls stay with counsel.** It does not predict whether an objection will succeed, give enforceability/privilege opinions, or invent facts or citations.

## Conventions

- `output_format: report`; **prompt-only** (no `scripts/`), so on the LQ.AI platform the output is a Markdown table in chat.
- Opens with a hard **privilege gate** and an intake interview (role / regime / round / state-party / issues).
- A separable `reference/issue-matching.md` relevance matcher is kept out of the monolith for reuse by a future cross-examination skill.

## Worked examples (`examples/`)

One per perspective on a single shared investor-State fact pattern (`gold-fact-pattern.md`): `example_requesting.md`, `example_producing.md`, `example_tribunal.md`. The gold pattern doubles as the evaluation reference.

## Citations

**Every legal citation is byte-verified against the official source** and logged in `reference/CITATIONS.md` (IBA 2020, Prague 2018, ICC 2021/2026, LCIA 2020, ICSID 2022, CIArb 2025 AI Guideline). The skill went through two adversarial citation-verification passes; the verification ledger and method are in `reference/CITATIONS.md`.

## Provenance

Authored with Claude Code assistance, adversarially reviewed, and citation-verified. Per `skills/CONTRIBUTING.md`, the human contributor is the attesting party.

## Attestation

> **Attestation.** I have reviewed the substantive legal content of this skill and certify that, to the best of my knowledge as a practicing attorney (Associate General Counsel, LegalQuants), the patterns, severity calibrations, recommended language, and reference material reflect accurate and reasonable legal practice in international arbitration under the IBA Rules (2020) and the other named regimes. I understand that this skill will be used in real practice and that errors could affect real legal work; I have authored this skill with the same care I would apply to my own client work.
>
> *Signed: Alexios Kirillov, Associate General Counsel, LegalQuants*

## Remaining before merge

- [ ] File/confirm a **claim issue** per `skills/CONTRIBUTING.md` §1 (no existing DE-### entry for a Redfern skill).
- [ ] Maintainers assign a **practicing-attorney reviewer + an engineer reviewer** (§4).

---

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

entries.title: #189: PR6b/WS5: chat tool-loop UI — in-chat confirmation gate + connect-on-demand
entries.text:
PR6b/WS5: chat tool-loop UI — in-chat confirmation gate + connect-on-demand ## What this is

Makes PR5b's governed chat tool-loop usable **inside chat**. The chat UI now consumes the two terminal SSE frames the backend emits when a tool call needs a human decision, rendering an in-message card that resumes the same assistant turn — no modals, no lost context.

- **`tool_confirmation_required`** → an in-message **approve/deny** card (`ToolGatePrompt`, variant `confirm`) showing the tool, provider, routed tier, a destructive marker, and the backend-redacted args summary. Approve/Deny `POST`s to `/api/v1/chats/{id}/tool-calls/{pending_call_id}` and the fresh SSE stream finalizes the **same** assistant bubble.
- **`mcp_authorization_required`** → a **connect** card → same-tab redirect to `authorize_url?return_url=<chat URL>`. PR4d returns with `?mcp_connected` (or `?mcp_error&server=`); the chats route shows a banner with a **Continue** button that re-sends the last user message.

## How it works

- **Parser/types** (`sse/parser.ts`, `types.ts`): two new terminal frames + `onToolConfirmation`/`onMcpAuthorization` callbacks, dispatched then `return` (terminal, exactly like `error`).
- **Shared consumption** (`ChatPanel.consumeIntoMessage`): one helper drives both the initial send and the resume POST, so a **chained gate** (a resumed turn that pauses again) finalizes the same bubble. `pendingGate` (shared `PendingGate` type in `chat/toolGate.ts`) is prop-drilled `ChatPanel → MessageList → MessageBubble`.
- **Resume errors**: a `409/410` surfaces an inline "this confirmation expired — re-send" hint; `gateBusy` is always reset.
- **Pure, tested helpers** (`chat/toolGate.ts`): `parseOAuthReturn`, `buildAuthorizeUrl`.

## D6 narrative honesty (mandatory)

Flipped the centralized availability claims from "coming next" → **shipped** for the in-chat confirm/connect UI in the governed-tool-flow explorer (Availability block + station 5/7 toggle copy), Learn §17, and the README. "Coming next" now points to **6c** rich case-law provenance.

## Verification

- Vitest: 23 new assertions (parser dispatch+terminal, `toolGate` helpers, `ToolGatePrompt` copy helpers); full lq-ai suite **490 passing**.
- `svelte-check`: **0 errors**.
- Visual: both card variants rendered (headless) — confirm card with destructive marker + redacted args + tier/destructive/audited pills; connect card with server + Connect button.
- Independent adversarial code review: no material correctness bugs; applied the one suggested hardening (clear stale gate on new send).

## Scope

Frontend + narrative docs only — **not security-gated** (no `gateway/**`, no `docs/security/**`, no API/schema/migration). Self-merge after CI green. The **case-law results panel is deferred to 6c** (needs 6c's structured citation data).

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

entries.title: #188: PR6a/WS5: transparency narrative — governed-tool-flow explorer + Learn + README + authority docs
entries.text:
PR6a/WS5: transparency narrative — governed-tool-flow explorer + Learn + README + authority docs ## PR6a / WS5 — Transparency narrative (governed-tool-flow explorer + Learn + README + authority docs)

First of the four WS5 transparency sub-PRs (6a → 6b → 6c → 6d). Makes the legal-research + MCP **security posture** legible to an in-house lawyer — "your data goes here, gated like this, audited like this, and **you** control the connectors." Spec: `docs/superpowers/specs/2026-06-19-pr6a-transparency-narrative-design.md`. Plan: `docs/superpowers/plans/2026-06-19-pr6a-transparency-narrative.md`.

**Web (static + Svelte) + docs only — no `api/`/`gateway/` code, no new routes, no migration.**

### What's in it
- **`web/static/learn/playgrounds/governed-tool-flow.html`** — a new self-contained explorer matching the existing 18 Learn playgrounds' house style: a step-through of the **7-station governed tool path** (allowlist → model picks tool → gateway egress/SSRF → tier gate → per-user OAuth → counts-only audit → result/confirmation gate) with **4 guardrail toggles** that re-run the flow and reveal the refusal/connect/confirm paths. Each posture claim links to its verifiable source file.
- **Learn → How it works** (`learn/how/+page.svelte`) — new section 17 embedding the explorer.
- **README** — a shipped "Legal research + connectors (MCP)" posture paragraph; reconciles the forward-looking M5–M7 paragraph to reference the now-shipped MCP connector mechanism.
- **Authority docs** — ADR 0014 + 0015 `Status → Accepted (2026-06-19)`; **`docs/security/boundary-registers.md`** tool-provider egress entry refreshed PARTIAL → SHIPPED (adds MCP sole-egress, per-user OAuth header-only/Fernet, the two-layer `tool_egress_log` + `tool_call_log` counts-only audit, and the destructive-tool confirmation gate; fixes a stale ADR-filename link; verification-path commands all resolve). `gateway.yaml.example`/`mcp.yaml.example` verified adequate (no edit).

### 🔒 Security-gated
Touches `docs/security/boundary-registers.md` (CODEOWNERS) → auto-routed to security review. **Kevin reviews/merges**; do not self-merge.

### Honesty (D6 — forward consistency)
The explorer/Learn/README state the in-chat confirmation + connect-on-demand **UI** is "coming in the next release" (6b) — the backend protocol ships now, the chat UI doesn't yet. All "today vs. next" claims live in **one** `AVAILABILITY` block (+ one parallel sentence each in Learn/README) so 6b/6c/6d update them in one place. Each later sub-PR carries a required narrative-update task; 6d does a final milestone-completion honesty pass.

### Verification
- **Independent accuracy review (opus)** against the merged code: every station/toggle/availability/audit claim verified accurate; **no blocking inaccuracies; ship: Yes**. (The project's transparency thesis makes an inaccurate viz a defect — this was the load-bearing gate.)
- `svelte-check` clean (0 new errors/warnings).
- Explorer verified via headless render: all 7 stations render; the 4 toggles each produce the correct break state (allowlisted/tier → REFUSED, oauth → CONNECT, readonly → CONFIRM); no JS console errors.

### Next
6b — the chat tool-loop UI that consumes PR5b's `tool_confirmation_required` / `mcp_authorization_required` SSE protocol (and updates this narrative's AVAILABILITY block per D6).
