entries.title: #90: feat(m3-close): promote skill author to wire shape (DE-316)
entries.text:
feat(m3-close): promote skill author to wire shape (DE-316) ## What

Closes **DE-316**. The M3-F2 `skill.execute` OTel spans read `skill.author`, but `author` lived only on `LQAIFrontmatter` and was never promoted to the `SkillSummary` / `Skill` wire shape that `SkillRegistry.get_skill()` returns — so `skill.author` was **always None** (silently dropped) for every built-in skill.

The existing span unit test masked this by using a *fake* skill that carried `author` directly. This change makes real registry-resolved skills carry it.

## How
- Add `author: str | None = None` to `SkillSummary` (inherited by `Skill`).
- Map `lq.author` in `derive_summary()` (`api/app/skills/schema.py`).

## Tests (TDD)
- `test_derive_summary_promotes_author` — watched it fail (`'SkillSummary' object has no attribute 'author'`), then pass.
- `test_derive_summary_author_none_when_absent` — None when frontmatter omits it; dropped from the response by `filter_summary_for_response`.
- api skill suite green; `ruff` + `mypy` clean; DB-backed skill-endpoint conformance tests pass.

## Docs
- `backend-openapi.yaml` `SkillSummary`: `author` optional field.
- `_emit_skill_spans` docstring: no longer says "NOT promoted (DE-316)".
- PRD §9 DE-316 marked **RESOLVED**.

Refs #10

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

entries.title: #89: feat(m3-close): implement admin anonymization-config GET (replace 501 stub)
entries.text:
feat(m3-close): implement admin anonymization-config GET (replace 501 stub) ## What

`GET /admin/v1/anonymization-config` returned **501** with a now-false message ("Anonymization configuration is an M2 feature… not yet enforced") — even though the M2 anonymization middleware **shipped and runs** (`app.state.anonymizer` wired in `gateway/app/main.py:269`; PRD §4.7).

That stale 501 was the transparency drift flagged for M3-close, and the false-inference target the `data-residency.html` Learn viz pointed at "to verify" anonymization wasn't running. This implements the real read surface.

## How

Mirrors `GET /admin/v1/tier-config`:
```python
cfg = _config(request)
return {"anonymization": cfg.anonymization.model_dump(mode="json")}
```
`enabled` + `apply_at_tiers` are typed; passthrough keys (`entity_types`, …) echo verbatim under `extra="allow"`.

## Tests (TDD)

Replaced `test_anonymization_config_returns_501` with `test_anonymization_config_returns_loaded_block` — watched it fail (501 ≠ 200), then pass.
- Gateway suite: **485 passed / 1 skipped** (matches baseline)
- `ruff format --check`, `ruff check`, `mypy --strict` all clean

## Docs
- `gateway-openapi.yaml`: GET description + 200 schema updated; PATCH cross-reference corrected (the PATCH **write** surface is still unimplemented — read-only here).
- `gateway.yaml.example`: fixed the stale `# … until M2 ships` comment (comment only — the `enabled: false` default is unchanged; enabling is a per-deployment decision, not flipped here).

## Scope note
Read (GET) surface only. The PATCH write surface + admin UI remain unbuilt; if you want those tracked, I can file a DE.

> ⚠️ Touches `gateway/**` — security-gated per CODEOWNERS.

Refs #10

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

entries.title: #88: docs(m3-close): fix stale Learn-viz status claims (transparency audit)
entries.text:
docs(m3-close): fix stale Learn-viz status claims (transparency audit) ## What

M3-close transparency audit of all 12 `web/static/learn/playgrounds/*.html` "How it Works" visualizations for stale M1/M2-era status claims.

A viz that **underclaims** shipped work (says a feature "isn't running" when it ships and runs) is a transparency violation — exactly as bad as overclaiming, per the project's founding principle.

## Findings

**5 false claims in 2 files. The other 10 viz are clean** and correctly cite real PRD §9 deferrals (DE-287/295/309/312).

Two root causes, both underclaiming shipped work:

| File | Was | Now |
|---|---|---|
| `data-residency.html` (×2) | "Anonymization Layer (M2, not yet running)… the middleware is absent", cited admin 501 as proof | "M2 — running"; points at `gateway/app/main.py` + keeps the genuine still-true caveat (Presidio legal-corpus recall/precision unmeasured, DE-282); dropped the 501 reference |
| `skill-composition.html` (×3) | Organization Profile "not active in the current M1 deployment" | Shipped in M2 + active; shown unconfigured in the demo |

## Evidence
- Anonymization middleware runs: `gateway/app/main.py:269` wires `app.state.anonymizer = Anonymizer()`; confirmed by `docs/HONEST-STATE.md §3.2`.
- Organization Profile shipped: `api/app/models/organization_profile.py`, `api/app/api/organization_profile.py`, `docs/db-schema.md` singleton index.

## Notes
- The admin `get_anonymization_config` 501 stub (the false-inference target) is being fixed separately (implement the real endpoint).
- Surgical diff: 5 string replacements, template literals verified balanced.

Refs #10

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

entries.title: #87: feat(m3-f3): OTel deployment recipes + observability.md + OTel-eval playground
entries.text:
feat(m3-f3): OTel deployment recipes + observability.md + OTel-eval playground ## M3-F3 — Deployment recipes + operator guide + OTel-eval playground (PR-3, final of the OTel phase)

Plan: [`docs/superpowers/plans/2026-05-24-m3-f3-deploy-observability.md`](docs/superpowers/plans/2026-05-24-m3-f3-deploy-observability.md). Spec: [`docs/proposals/opentelemetry-deepening.md`](docs/proposals/opentelemetry-deepening.md) PR-3. Builds on F1 (#85) + F2 (#86).

Gives operators a copy-paste path from zero to "I can see a chat-send trace." **No application code changes** — config, docs, one test, one playground.

### What's here
- **`deploy/observability/grafana-tempo-loki/`** — full self-hosted stack: a compose overlay adding OTel Collector + Tempo + Loki + Prometheus + Grafana, plus collector/tempo/loki/prometheus configs, Grafana datasource + dashboard provisioning (3 panels: gateway tier mix, p99 latency by route, error rate), and `.env.example`. The overlay points api/gateway's OTLP exporter at the in-network collector.
- **`deploy/observability/otel-collector-standalone/`** — collector-only overlay for operators who already run Honeycomb / Datadog / Lightstep (commented per-backend exporter blocks; active `debug` exporter so it runs out-of-box).
- **`deploy/observability/README.md`** + per-recipe READMEs — decision guide + run commands + a 15-minute "verify a trace in Grafana" walkthrough.
- **`docs/observability.md`** — operator guide: env-var matrix, per-signal inventory (the F2 domain spans + the Prometheus metrics), the two recipes, anonymization+telemetry posture, no-telemetry-by-default, and links to every deferred enhancement. Linked from README + HONEST-STATE §7.
- **`tests/test_observability.py`** — pins "no telemetry by default" across api + gateway (10 tests).
- **`web/static/learn/playgrounds/otel-eval.html`** + Learn §11 — an interactive annotated trace tree of a chat-send showing the F2 span hierarchy, the 5 operator questions, sample TraceQL/PromQL, and an "attributes that appear vs. never appear (raw PII)" panel reinforcing the anonymization guarantee.

### Decisions locked
OTLP/HTTP transport (collector :4318); sampler documented (`parentbased_always_on` dev / `parentbased_traceidratio` 0.1 prod) via env, no code default changed; overlays set the OTLP endpoint, base compose stays no-telemetry-by-default; OWUI-fork OTel out of scope (DE-302/DE-D).

### Verified
- `docker compose -f docker-compose.yml -f deploy/observability/<recipe>/docker-compose.observability.yml config` → **valid merged config for both recipes**.
- The overlay env-merge sets `OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_ENDPOINT` on api/gateway while retaining base env.
- `tests/test_observability.py` → **10 passed**; existing cross-cutting contract test unaffected.
- Dashboard JSON valid, queries use the real metric names.
- §11 playground well-formed, self-contained (no external CDN/font/script), wired into the Learn page.
- **No api/gateway app code changed** (config/docs/test/web only).

### Pending validations (deferred to M3-close pre-v0.3.0 fresh-install — they require bouncing containers with OTel on)
- **Live bring-up**: recipe 1 up → send a chat → see the trace in Grafana within 15 min + dashboard shows live data (the proposal's headline acceptance).
- **§11 visual smoke**: rebuild `web`, open `/lq-ai/learn/how`, confirm the section renders (Svelte toolchain builds in Docker, not locally).

### Note
CI does not currently run the repo-root `tests/` directory (only `api/`, `gateway/`, `web/`) — so `tests/test_observability.py` won't execute in CI as-is. Adding a CI job is a CODEOWNERS-gated `.github/workflows/**` change; flagging as a possible follow-up rather than bundling it here.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

entries.title: #86: feat(m3-f2): OpenTelemetry domain spans + rich attributes
entries.text:
feat(m3-f2): OpenTelemetry domain spans + rich attributes ## M3-F2 — Domain spans + rich attributes (PR-2 of the OTel-deepening phase)

Plan: [`docs/superpowers/plans/2026-05-23-m3-f2-domain-spans.md`](docs/superpowers/plans/2026-05-23-m3-f2-domain-spans.md). Spec: [`docs/proposals/opentelemetry-deepening.md`](docs/proposals/opentelemetry-deepening.md) PR-2. Builds on F1 (#85).

Adds manual OpenTelemetry **domain spans** to the high-value LQ.AI operations, layered on top of the M1 FastAPI/httpx auto-instrumentation. So an operator one click into a chat-send trace can answer: why slow, how much cost, did anonymization run, which provider+model+tier, citation-cascade outcome.

### What's instrumented

| Span | Attributes (counts/types/ids only — never raw values) |
|---|---|
| `citation.verify` (+ `citation.stage.{exact_match,tolerant_match,paraphrase_judge,ensemble}` children) | `citation.method/confidence/partial/tier_envelope`, `document.id`; per-stage `verified`/`confidence`; ensemble `n_judges`/`rule`. Events: `exact_match.hit`/`tolerant_match.hit` short-circuits; `ensemble.budget_fallback`. |
| `anonymization.pre` / `anonymization.post` | `enabled`, `tier`, `skip_reason`, `entity_count` (int), `entity_types` (list[str]). Skip events per reason. |
| `skill.execute` (one per applied skill) | `skill.slug/version/author`, `project.id/privileged`, `chat.id`. |
| `inference.dispatch` (gateway handler) | `provider`, `model`, `tier`, `outcome`, `tokens_in`, `tokens_out`, `cost_usd`. |
| `playbook.execute` (+ per-position children) | `playbook.id/contract_type`, `position.count`, `document.id`; child `position.id/order`. |
| `tabular.execute` (+ per-cell children) | `document_count`, `column_count`; child `document.id`, `column.name`. |

### Architecture
- New per-service `observability_helpers.py` (`@traced` decorator + `record_attributes(span, **kwargs)`). Cross-service contract is the **attribute names**, not shared code.
- **No-op by default**: when `OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_ENDPOINT` is unset, `get_tracer()` is a no-op tracer and `record_attributes` short-circuits on `is_recording()`. No measurable p99 cost by construction (no perf harness needed).
- **LangGraph nesting**: verified that the executors' per-position/per-cell child spans nest under the top span automatically (contextvars propagate through async nodes) — asserted via `parent.span_id` in the executor span tests.

### Anonymization-of-attributes guarantee (critical)
Span attributes carry **counts and types only, never raw entity values** — the anonymization promise must not regress via the telemetry side-channel. `PseudonymMapper.entity_counts()` exposes counts (a copy of the per-type counters); `_assignments` (raw originals) is never read into a span. Enforced by `gateway/tests/test_anonymization_observability.py` (injects a known name, asserts it never appears in any attribute).

### Decisions locked
- `skill.execute`: one child span **per applied skill** (a send applying N skills → N spans).
- `inference.dispatch`: **handler-level** span (in `inference.py`) so it carries `cost_usd` (computed post-router).
- `tabular.skill_id`: not emitted — `TabularExecution` has no skill linkage (→ DE-314).
- Sampler / OTLP-HTTP / per-service helpers / OWUI-out-of-scope: unchanged from F1.

### Deferred enhancements filed (PRD §9)
- **DE-314** — tabular execution↔skill linkage for `tabular.skill_id`.
- **DE-315** — streaming-rehydration per-chunk spans.
- **DE-316** — promote skill `author` to the `Skill`/`SkillSummary` wire shape (so `skill.author` populates; currently None).
- **DE-317** — `inference.dispatch` span on the streaming path.
- **DE-318** — `playbook.position` child spans on the redline node (deviating positions).

### Verification
- **gateway**: ruff (format + check) + mypy `--strict` clean; **485 passed**, 1 skipped.
- **api**: ruff + mypy clean; **1290 passed**, 1 skipped.
- New tests per site (in-memory span exporter); executor span tests run against Postgres (throwaway DB, integration-marked).
- Final holistic review: SHIP — zero attribute-name drift, anonymization guarantee holds across both services, no stray edits.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

entries.title: #85: test(m3-f1): pin W3C trace-context propagation across api→gateway→provider
entries.text:
test(m3-f1): pin W3C trace-context propagation across api→gateway→provider ## M3-F1 — Trace-context propagation audit + regression test (PR-1 of the OTel-deepening phase)

Spec: [`docs/proposals/opentelemetry-deepening.md`](docs/proposals/opentelemetry-deepening.md) PR-1 + [`docs/M3-IMPLEMENTATION-PLAN.md`](docs/M3-IMPLEMENTATION-PLAN.md) Phase F.

### Audit outcome — no propagation gap

W3C `traceparent` already propagates **end-to-end** across `api → gateway → provider` via OpenTelemetry auto-instrumentation:
- **Egress** — `HTTPXClientInstrumentor().instrument()` (global) wraps `AsyncHTTPTransport.handle_async_request` and injects via `opentelemetry.propagate.inject`.
- **Ingress** — `FastAPIInstrumentor.instrument_app()` extracts the incoming context.
- Both use the **default global textmap propagator** (W3C TraceContext + baggage).

Confirmed every internal hop uses a standard `httpx.AsyncClient` with the default transport, so the global instrumentation covers it:
- api→gateway: `api/app/clients/gateway.py` `GatewayClient`.
- gateway→provider: `gateway/app/providers/{anthropic,openai,azure_openai}.py` adapters.

**No code change to either `observability.py`.** Deliberately did **not** add `set_global_textmap(TraceContextTextMapPropagator())` (floated in the proposal) — it would drop baggage from the default composite propagator. Per the proposal's own risk table, "PR-1 finds nothing to fix" is an accepted outcome; the regression tests ship as the standing contract.

### Regression tests (one chat-send = one trace, not three)

- `api/tests/test_trace_propagation.py` — api injects a traceparent on the outbound gateway call; gateway joins it. Modeled across an **explicit wire boundary** (capture the header in the api half, replay it from a fresh context in the gateway half) so the test exercises real header propagation rather than the in-process contextvar leakage `ASGITransport` would otherwise mask (which would make the test a false positive).
- `gateway/tests/test_trace_propagation.py` — gateway joins an upstream traceparent on ingress and re-injects on the provider hop.

Both files go **red under `OTEL_PROPAGATORS=baggage`** (W3C disabled) — proving they would fail without working propagation, per the PR-1 acceptance criterion.

### Verification

- `api` unit suite: **530 passed, 12 skipped**.
- `gateway` unit suite: **411 passed**.
- No regression in existing `test_observability.py` (both services).
- `ruff check` + `ruff format --check` clean on both new files.
- `docs/architecture.md` §OBS updated to confirm verified end-to-end correlation.

### PR-open decisions locked (from the proposal, for the rest of Phase F)

- **Sampler:** `parentbased_always_on` (dev) / `parentbased_traceidratio` 0.1 (prod-ref) — operator picks via env; code default unchanged.
- **Transport:** stay OTLP/HTTP.
- **Helper location:** per-service `observability_helpers.py` (F2); cross-service contract is the attribute names.
- **OWUI-fork OTel:** out of scope (DE-D).

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

entries.title: #84: docs(m3-e2c): three M3 Learn-tab playgrounds + system-architecture M3 update
entries.text:
docs(m3-e2c): three M3 Learn-tab playgrounds + system-architecture M3 update ## Summary

Final M3-E2 documentation PR (E2c). Adds the three M3 Learn-tab playgrounds and brings the existing system-architecture playground to M3 parity.

**New playgrounds** (self-contained HTML in `web/static/learn/playgrounds/`, mirroring `citation-engine-cascade.html`; zero external deps; inline JS passes `node --check`):
- `playbook-cascade.html` — the 4-node executor cascade walking the 8 NDA-Mutual positions across synthetic NDAs.
- `tabular-review.html` — interactive N×M grid; clicking a cell opens the citation drawer.
- `word-addin-flow.html` — the 4-stage install/auth flow (manifest → unsigned sideload → OAuth → version handshake).

Each carries the honest-state caveats (FTS `matched_text` not Citation-Engine for playbooks; display-only citations DE-309 + cost/tier-0 DE-310 for tabular; plumbing-only DE-287/DE-295 for Word). Wired into the "How It Works" page as sections 8–10 with framing + source links; intro/count updated 7 → 10.

**Existing visualization updated for M3** (answering "have existing visualizations been updated to the extent necessary?"):
- `system-architecture.html` — added 4 nodes (`arq-worker`, `slack-bridge`, `teams-bridge`, `word-addin`) + 7 edges, matching the existing `NODES`/`CONNECTIONS` schema. This brings the playground in line with the `architecture.md` Mermaid diagram I updated in #83. The other 7 playgrounds cover M1/M2 subsystems (citation engine, tier system, anonymization, request lifecycle, skill composition/format, data residency) that M3 didn't change — audited, no update needed.

## Process

The 3 playgrounds + the system-architecture update were built/edited by parallel subagents (each given the template + capability doc + exact schema), then verified: valid HTML parse, `node --check` on inline JS, zero external deps, content-accuracy + honest-caveat grep.

## Test plan

- [ ] CI: Web (svelte-check + Vitest). Note: the fresh clone has no `web/node_modules`, so I couldn't run svelte-check locally; the how-page edits are static markup (tag-balanced) and the playgrounds are static HTML (not Svelte-compiled).
- [ ] **Visual smoke (browser):** open `/lq-ai/learn/how` and confirm sections 8–10 render and each iframe loads; open each playground full-screen and exercise its controls. (This is the browser step I can't drive.)
- [ ] Reviewer: spot-check playground accuracy against the linked source files.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Refs M3-E2.

entries.title: #83: docs(m3-e2b): onboarding walkthrough + architecture viz + API audit + db-schema + compliance
entries.text:
docs(m3-e2b): onboarding walkthrough + architecture viz + API audit + db-schema + compliance ## Summary

Second of three M3-E2 documentation PRs (E2b). Developer-onboarding + API/schema audit + architecture-diagram enhancement + compliance.

**Onboarding** (`quickstart.md`, `README.md`): new "Verify the M3 surfaces" fresh-install walkthrough (Playbook → Easy Playbook → Tabular → Word install against the synthetic corpus — the M3-E1 path); host-port-collision troubleshooting (DE-306). README's four M3 capability blurbs corrected to shipped reality + capability-doc links.

**Architecture viz** (`architecture.md`): system diagram now shows Playbook **+ Tabular** and the **async worker tier** (arq-worker `arq:m3a6` + ingest-worker); M3 milestone prose fixed.

**API audit** (`backend-openapi.yaml`): F1 added missing required `document_names` on `TabularExecution`; F3/F4 added missing `401`/`422`; F6 `additionalProperties:false` on the execute body. (Full audit found everything else matches.)

**DB schema** (`db-schema.md`): migrations 0036/0037/0038 documented.

**Compliance**: `sig-lite.md` updated for the Word/Slack/Teams trust boundaries; DE-313 filed for the SOC2/ISO27001 alignment docs (counsel-gated stubs that don't exist yet).

## Honesty corrections this PR makes

- README said **four** built-in playbooks with wrong names → **five** (NDA-Mutual, NDA-Unilateral, MSA-SaaS, MSA-Commercial-Purchase, DPA-GDPR).
- README + architecture.md described the Word add-in **feature surface** and the Slack **`/lq` slash-command** as shipped → both are deferred (DE-287, DE-288); only plumbing shipped.
- architecture.md claimed the **signed manifest (M3-B7)** shipped → descoped to community (DE-295); only the unsigned path ships.
- `tabular-review.md` (from E2a) listed `document_names` as a DB column → it's a response field.

## Known pre-existing issue (not addressed here)

Two README links point to never-written docs — `docs/playbook-authoring-guide.md` and `docs/deployment-cookbook.md` (pre-existing, in lines this PR doesn't touch). Flagging rather than fabricating those docs; can be a quick follow-up (repoint or remove).

## Test plan

- [ ] CI: markdown/link checks. All other internal links in touched docs now resolve.
- [ ] Reviewer: confirm the honesty corrections; spot-check the agent-drafted db-schema + sig-lite sections.
- [ ] Stacked conceptually on #82 (merged); branched off updated main.

entries.title: #82: docs(m3-e2a): capability docs + PRD §3 status flips
entries.text:
docs(m3-e2a): capability docs + PRD §3 status flips ## Summary

First of three M3-E2 documentation PRs (E2a). Adds the four M3 capability companion docs — verified against the codebase, mirroring `docs/citation-engine.md`'s house style — and flips the PRD §3 statuses from "Deferred-M3" to shipped reality.

**New docs:**
- `docs/playbooks.md` — built-in playbook executor (retrieve→classify→redline→compile) + Easy Playbook wizard.
- `docs/word-addin.md` — manifest generation, unsigned-manifest sideload, OAuth, M3-B8 version handshake.
- `docs/tabular-review.md` — `output_format:table` mode, LangGraph workflow, export, the display-only citation bridge.
- `docs/intake-bridges.md` — Slack + Teams plumbing, admin surface, security/threat model.

**PRD §3 flips:** §3.7 Playbooks → SHIPPED; §3.9 Word → PLUMBING SHIPPED (feature surface M4/DE-287, signed dist community/DE-295); §3.14 Tabular → SHIPPED; §3.15 Slack/Teams → PLUMBING SHIPPED (slash-command M4/DE-288, OAuth E2E unverified/DE-312).

## Honesty notes (per CLAUDE.md conservative posture)

Writing these against the code surfaced that **both the playbook executor and the tabular executor anchor answers with `matched_text` + `cited_chunk_ids` (lexical FTS), NOT the M2 Citation Engine verification cascade** — Citation Engine integration is explicitly deferred for both (`playbooks/state.py` docstring; tabular tracked as DE-309). The docs document this honestly rather than implying verified citations. This also corrects an imprecise claim from the M3-E1 report ("Citation Engine confirmed" for playbooks) — the anchoring is real, but it's not the full cascade.

## Process note

3 of the 4 capability docs were drafted by parallel subagents reading their respective code areas, then reviewed + harmonized; `tabular-review.md` was written directly. All relative file links verified to resolve.

## Test plan

- [ ] CI: markdown/link checks.
- [ ] Reviewer: spot-check the honest-state framing in each doc's Scope + Known-limitations sections — especially the citation caveats (playbooks/tabular) and the OAuth-E2E-unverified caveat (intake-bridges).
- [ ] `grep -nE "M3-[A-Z][0-9]" docs/PRD.md` returns matches for the shipped tasks.

entries.title: #81: docs(prd): file 8 deferred enhancements from M3-E1 (DE-305..312)
entries.text:
docs(prd): file 8 deferred enhancements from M3-E1 (DE-305..312) ## Summary

Files the deferred items surfaced during **M3-E1 fresh-install verification** into PRD §9. These are the findings we chose *not* to fix in the companion code PR (#80) — they're tracked here with severity + scope + when-to-ship.

| DE | Finding | Pri |
|---|---|---|
| DE-305 | Bridge `${VAR:?}` interpolation breaks all compose commands when unset (even without slack/teams profiles) | P2 |
| DE-306 | Quickstart needs a prominent host-port-collision callout | P3 |
| DE-307 | `File` schema exposes `page_count`/`character_count` that never populate | P3 |
| DE-308 | Easy Playbook clustering over-segments (20 vs ~5-10) + missed the "Standard of Care" designed axis | P2 |
| DE-309 | Real Citation-Engine-backed tabular provenance (F6 follow-on) | P2 |
| DE-310 | Per-cell `tier_used` + cost propagation from gateway | P3 |
| DE-311 | Single source of truth for app version (F3 follow-on) | P3 |
| DE-312 | Slack + Teams bridge OAuth **end-to-end tunnel** verification | P1 |

## Context

The four **fixed** findings (F6 citations serialization, F7 document_name, F3 version, F8 OpenAPI gap) are in **PR #80**. This PR is the deferred-work paperwork. DE-309/310/311 are explicit follow-ons referenced by #80's fix commit; DE-312 captures that the bridges shipped plumbing-only and a real OAuth round-trip was never exercised (no tunnel ever stood up in M3-D1/D3).

## Test plan

entries.title: #80: fix(m3-e1): surface tabular citations + document names; bump version
entries.text:
fix(m3-e1): surface tabular citations + document names; bump version ## Summary

Fixes four read-side/metadata defects surfaced during **M3-E1 fresh-install verification** (the pre-v0.3.0 tag pass). None require migrations or executor changes.

- **F6 — tabular citations never reached the API/UI.** The executor persists grounding chunks as raw `cited_chunk_ids: list[str]`, but the read-side `CellResult` models structured `Citation` objects under `citations`, with no bridge. So `citations` deserialized **empty on every cell** even though the chunks were recorded — the citation drawer (a core M3-C4 feature) showed nothing. Adds a `TabularRow` `model_validator(before)` projecting each `cited_chunk_id` into a `Citation` (row `document_id` + cell `confidence` + deterministic display-only `citation_id = uuid5(NS, chunk_id)`). Cells already carrying real citations pass through untouched. **Both the API response and the XLSX/CSV export pick this up.**
- **F7 — row `document_name` showed the document UUID, not the filename.** The load-documents node read `original_filename`/`name` off the `Document` row (which has neither) and fell back to the id. Now joins the parent `File` for `files.filename`.
- **F3 — `app.__version__` was still `0.1.0`**, never bumped through v0.2.0, so the M3-B8 Word handshake reported a stale `deployment_version`. Bumped to `0.3.0`.
- **F8 — OpenAPI sketch** now documents the M3-C4a `GET /tabular/executions/{id}/export` endpoint (xlsx|csv), which M3-C4a omitted.

## Deferred (PRD §9, filed separately)

- Real Citation-Engine-backed provenance for tabular cells (resolvable `citation_id` rather than synthetic) — the F6 fix is a display-only bridge.
- Single-source-of-truth for `__version__` so it stops drifting (F3 root cause).

## Verification

Verified live on a fresh `~/Code/lq-ai` stack (full cold build, all profiles):
- Existing execution citations **surface post-fix** (read-side transform retro-applies to already-persisted `cited_chunk_ids`).
- Fresh execution shows `document_name: nda-1-acme-beta.pdf` + 2 citations per grounded cell.
- CSV `citation_links` column populated on all 5 rows (was empty).
- `ruff format --check` + `ruff check` clean on all changed files.

## Test plan

- [ ] CI: ruff + mypy + pytest (new `api/tests/tabular/test_schemas.py` — 5 regression cases covering the citation projection, determinism, failed-cell, pass-through, and full results round-trip).
- [ ] Reviewer: confirm the synthetic `citation_id` approach is acceptable for v0.3.0 (display-only; never resolved against the Citation Engine).

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Refs M3-E1.

entries.title: #79: docs(claude): add DELETE-204 FastAPI pitfall + fix recipe
entries.text:
docs(claude): add DELETE-204 FastAPI pitfall + fix recipe ## Summary

- Adds a new **Common pitfalls (catch at write-time, not test-time)** subsection to `CLAUDE.md` under "What good agent behavior looks like", right after "Read before writing".
- First entry documents the `DELETE` endpoint + `status_code=204` + default `JSONResponse` collision that asserts at import time and collapses the whole test suite at collection.
- Includes the fix recipe (`response_class=Response` + explicit `return Response(status_code=204)`) so future contributors avoid the trap at write-time.

## Why

The bug bit M3-C2 (fix in commit `c613c43`) and recurred in M3-D4 because the recipe lived only in code comments inside `app/api/admin_intake_bridges.py`. Surfacing it in `CLAUDE.md` — the orientation doc every agent and human reads first — closes the recurrence loop before M3-E1 fresh-install verify and before M3-F starts adding new endpoints.

## Test plan

- [ ] CI is docs-only (no behavioral change); `markdownlint` / link-check workflows should pass without intervention.
- [ ] Spot-check rendered Markdown on the PR page — code-fence + bold formatting renders cleanly.
- [ ] Confirm no other section header collides with the new "Common pitfalls" header.

entries.title: #78: M3-D4 — admin intake-bridges shell (list + soft-delete for Slack + Teams)
entries.text:
M3-D4 — admin intake-bridges shell (list + soft-delete for Slack + Teams) ## Summary

Closes M3-D4 plumbing — operators see what's currently connected and can disconnect installs from the LQ.AI admin UI without env-var edits or redeploys. The slash-command surface (DE-288) lands later; its UI hooks (quick-ask skill picker + audit-log section) are visible-but-disabled with tooltips pointing at DE-288.

## What's in the diff

* **Backend (`api/`)**: new admin router `app/api/admin_intake_bridges.py` with `GET /admin/intake-bridges` + `DELETE /admin/intake-bridges/{slack,teams}/{id}`. Pydantic schemas in `app/schemas/intake_bridges.py`. Section-split response (slack_workspaces + teams_tenants), soft-delete (revivable per M3-D1/M3-D3 upsert), admin-gated. Both DELETEs use the M3-C2 `response_class=Response` pattern (preserved as code comment).
* **Frontend (`web/`)**: `intakeBridgesApi` client + admin page at `/lq-ai/admin/intake-bridges` with two sections (Slack + Teams), each surfacing live installs in a table with relative-time install date, disconnect button, and visible-but-disabled quick-ask-skill picker (DE-288 tooltip). Empty `/lq` audit-log shell at the bottom.
* **Layout nav**: new "Intake bridges" link between Word add-in and Developer Support.
* **OpenAPI sketch + 3 schemas**: documented for the operator-facing surface.

## Local verification

| Surface | Result |
|---|---|
| api venv: ruff format/check, mypy app/ | clean |
| api venv: test_admin_intake_bridges.py (10/10) | pass |
| api venv: test_openapi paths_match_sketch + test_endpoints route_inventory | pass |
| web: vitest src/routes/lq-ai/admin/intake-bridges/__tests__/ (20/20) | pass |
| web: svelte-check on new files | clean (only pre-existing OWUI errors elsewhere) |

## Test plan

- [ ] CI green on API + Gateway + Web jobs
- [ ] Reviewer eyeballs the SvelteKit page in browser (run the stack, log in as admin, visit /lq-ai/admin/intake-bridges, confirm empty state + "Open install" buttons disabled when URL fields are blank)

## What's NOT in this PR (deferred):

entries.title: #77: M3-D3 — teams-bridge service + Microsoft OAuth + backend persistence
entries.text:
M3-D3 — teams-bridge service + Microsoft OAuth + backend persistence ## Summary

Mirrors the M3-D1 slack-bridge for Microsoft Teams. Operator installs LQ.AI in any M365 tenant via the multi-tenant Azure AD app; the tenant record persists in the api for M3-D4's admin UI to surface.

**4 architectural decisions locked at start** (handoff recommendations all ratified):

1. **Encryption key** — reuse `LQ_AI_BRIDGE_MASTER_KEY` from M3-D1 (same bridge threat model; nothing per-tenant to encrypt today since Teams uses app-level credentials)
2. **Bridge bearer** — reuse `LQ_AI_BRIDGE_TOKEN` (one rotation point for both bridges)
3. **MS Bot Framework auth** — raw httpx, no `botbuilder-core` SDK (saves ~15 transitive deps; mirrors slack-bridge's posture)
4. **Tenancy** — multi-tenant Azure AD app (one registration serves any tenant)

## What's in the diff

* **`teams-bridge/`** standalone FastAPI service on port 8003 — pyproject + Dockerfile + README + app/main + config + OAuth handler (Microsoft identity platform `/common/oauth2/v2.0/{authorize,token}` + Graph `/organization`) + OTel substrate + Teams App manifest.json
* **`api/`** — migration 0038 `teams_tenants` table + ORM + Pydantic + `POST /api/v1/integrations/teams/tenants` endpoint + 6 integration tests
* **Shared `require_bridge_auth`** lifted out of `integrations_slack.py` into `app/api/dependencies.py` so both bridges share the same constant-time `LQ_AI_BRIDGE_TOKEN` matcher
* **docker-compose** — new `slack-bridge` service under `profiles: [teams]`; required env vars marked with `:?` form
* **`docs/api/backend-openapi.yaml`** — new path + schemas (TeamsTenantCreate, TeamsTenantResponse)
* **`.env.example`** — new M3-D3 section

## Local verification

| Surface | Result |
|---|---|
| api venv: ruff format/check, mypy app/ | clean |
| api venv: test_integrations_teams (6) + test_integrations_slack (8) + test_encryption (8) | 22/22 pass |
| api venv: test_openapi paths_match_sketch + test_endpoints route_inventory | pass |
| teams-bridge venv: ruff format/check, mypy --strict | clean |
| teams-bridge venv: pytest tests/ (test_oauth.py) | 9/9 pass |
| slack-bridge venv (sanity re-check after BridgeAuth refactor) | 15/15 pass |

## Test plan

- [ ] CI green on API + Gateway + Web jobs
- [ ] Reviewer skims `app/oauth.py` for Microsoft OAuth correctness (multi-tenant common endpoint, scopes list, code exchange body shape, id_token decode safety, Graph fallback)
- [ ] Reviewer confirms shared `require_bridge_auth` refactor doesn't change Slack endpoint behavior
- [ ] Reviewer eyeballs the Teams App manifest schema against [Microsoft's v1.16 reference](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/microsoftteams/platform/resources/schema/manifest-schema)

## Deferred (DEs in PR description, not new):

- Identity-mapping UI (Teams email ↔ LQ.AI user) — M3-D4 admin UI scope
- On-behalf-of refresh-token storage column — M4 scope (only needed once DE-288's `/lq` surface lands)
- Teams app icons (color.png + outline.png) — operators provide own; reference icons can ship in a follow-up docs PR

entries.title: #76: M3-D1 (DRAFT, ~half) — slack-bridge service scaffold + OAuth handlers
entries.text:
M3-D1 (DRAFT, ~half) — slack-bridge service scaffold + OAuth handlers ## Status: DRAFT — partial M3-D1 (~6 of ~11 hr)

This PR lands the **bridge-side half** of M3-D1: the standalone slack-bridge FastAPI service with OAuth install + callback handlers + Slack signature verification + 8 unit tests. Mergeable in isolation as a self-contained service.

**Held as draft until the backend half lands:** the OAuth callback POSTs to `POST /api/v1/integrations/slack/workspaces` on the lq-ai api, which does not yet exist. Without it, installing a Slack app would 404 on persistence. The next commit(s) on this branch add the backend endpoint + migration 0037 (`slack_workspaces` table) + the Slack App manifest YAML + the docker-compose `--profile slack` entry + integration tests. Once the api endpoint lands the PR flips to ready-for-review.

## What's in this PR

- **`slack-bridge/`** — new standalone FastAPI service:
  - `pyproject.toml` — runtime deps: fastapi, uvicorn, httpx, slack-sdk, OTel substrate
  - `Dockerfile` — mirrors `gateway/` (no DB, no migrations, runs uvicorn directly on port 8002)
  - `README.md` — env-var matrix, security posture, install flow walkthrough
  - `app/main.py` — FastAPI factory with /healthz + /readyz + /slack/events stub
  - `app/config.py` — pydantic-settings Settings (3 credential groups)
  - `app/oauth.py` — install + callback handlers (state-token CSRF, 10-min TTL, narrow `commands` + `chat:write` scopes only)
  - `app/signing.py` — HMAC-SHA256 inbound webhook verification + 5-min replay window
  - `app/observability.py` — OTel substrate (TracerProvider + FastAPI + httpx auto-instrumentation, opt-in)
  - `tests/test_signing.py` — 8 tests: valid accept, tampered reject, wrong secret reject, stale/future ts reject, missing prefix reject, empty inputs reject without raising, real wall-clock sanity

## Security notes (CODEOWNERS-routed)

- Bot tokens never persist in the bridge. They POST to the LQ.AI api on receipt for encrypted persistence there.
- State tokens are in-memory with TTL; bridge restart between install start and callback invalidates the flow (operator restarts the install).
- Slack scopes are narrow on purpose: only `commands` (for the future M3-D2 slash-command surface) and `chat:write` (so the bot can post replies in channels it is invited to). The bot does **not** read silent channels.
- The internal bridge→api channel carries `LQ_AI_BRIDGE_TOKEN` as a bearer. Operator-supplied; must differ from any user-facing token.

## Test plan

- [x] Bridge unit tests: `pytest tests/` — 8/8 pass.
- [x] ruff format + ruff check + mypy --strict — all clean.
- [ ] CI: ruff + mypy + pytest in the new `Slack Bridge` workflow (to be added in a CI follow-up commit if needed; this PR may need a workflow file).
- [ ] Backend persistence endpoint lands (this branch, follow-up commit).
- [ ] OAuth callback integration test against mocked slack_sdk (this branch, follow-up commit).
- [ ] Manual E2E: install the bridge into a test Slack workspace, verify bot appears + persistence row lands in the LQ.AI api.

## Next session pickup

Per the M3-D1 task spec at `docs/M3-IMPLEMENTATION-PLAN.md` lines 682+:

1. Add `POST /api/v1/integrations/slack/workspaces` to the api — bridge-token bearer auth, persists `team_id`, encrypted `bot_token`, `team_name`, `bot_user_id`, `installer_slack_user_id`.
2. Migration 0037 — `slack_workspaces` table (uuid PK, team_id text unique, bot_token_encrypted bytea, etc.).
3. `slack-bridge/manifest.yml` — Slack App manifest declaring scopes + redirect URI.
4. `docker-compose.yml` — `slack-bridge` service under `profiles: [slack]`.
5. OAuth callback integration test with mocked `slack_sdk.AsyncWebClient`.
6. Backend endpoint test in `api/tests/test_integrations_slack.py`.

Estimated remaining effort: ~3–4 hours of focused work.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

entries.title: #75: M3-C4a — Tabular Review XLSX/CSV export
entries.text:
M3-C4a — Tabular Review XLSX/CSV export ## Summary

Adds the export half of M3-C4 (per the M3 plan task spec). Bulk operations (M3-C4b — "Redline column N", "Draft memo summarizing column N") are deliberately scoped out of this PR; they require architecture decisions (does redline produce N new grid columns? N new chats? a single combined output?) that warrant their own design pass.

## Three atomic commits

1. **`feat(m3-c4a)`: backend export endpoint** — `GET /api/v1/tabular/executions/{id}/export?format=xlsx|csv`. XLSX uses `openpyxl` with citation comments per cell; CSV uses stdlib `csv` with a trailing `citation_links` column. Adds `openpyxl>=3.1,<4` to api runtime deps (no pandas — keeps the dep tree tight). Audit row on every export.
2. **`test(m3-c4a)`: 5 unit tests** — XLSX openpyxl round-trip, CSV stdlib `csv.reader` round-trip, empty-results header-only XLSX + CSV, 5-citation cap on cell comments. Tests pass against the running container (live-smoked the endpoint too).
3. **`feat(m3-c4a)`: frontend Export buttons** — Export XLSX / Export CSV buttons in the result-view action bar, gated to `status === 'completed'`. New `exportTabularExecution(id, format)` client helper in `lib/lq-ai/api/tabular.ts` uses focused `fetch` (binary response). Standard Blob-URL + anchor-click download pattern.

## Behavior

- **XLSX:** opens cleanly in Excel desktop, Numbers (macOS), Google Sheets (per spec). Each grid cell with at least one citation carries an openpyxl `Comment` listing citation IDs + confidences (capped at 5 per comment with `... and N more` overflow line; lead line preserves total count).
- **CSV:** RFC 4180 via stdlib `csv`; trailing `citation_links` column flattens every cell's citations into semicolon-separated `<column_name>:<citation_id>` pairs.
- **Failed cells** render as `"(failed)"` rather than empty string so the gap is visible without cross-referencing the source execution.
- **409** on non-completed executions — partial grids would mislead downstream consumers.

## Live smoke

```
$ curl -H "Authorization: Bearer $TOKEN" \
    "http://localhost:8000/api/v1/tabular/executions/41d2d31e-.../export?format=xlsx" \
    -o /tmp/tab.xlsx
HTTP 200  Content-Type: application/vnd.openxmlformats-officedocument.spreadsheetml.sheet
Size: 5615

$ python3 -c "import openpyxl, io; ..."
Sheet: Tabular Review
('Document', 'Term + Renewal', 'Payment Terms', 'Limitation of Liability', 'Indemnification')
('msa-1-...', 'Initial term: 3 years; auto-renews...', 'Milestone invoices Net 30...', '(failed)', '(failed)')
...
```

## What's deferred

- **M3-C4b — Bulk operations** ("Redline column N", "Draft memo summarizing column N"). These introduce a cross-row skill-execution pattern that needs UX/architecture decisions: does redline produce N new grid columns appended to the right, N new chats, or a single combined report? Memo as a new artifact type on the execution row, or as a chat-message? Surfaced as task #17 for design conversation before code.

## Test plan

- [x] Backend pytest: `pytest tests/tabular/test_export.py` — 5/5 pass.
- [x] Full tabular suite: `pytest tests/tabular/` — 37 pass, 9 skipped (existing skip-set).
- [x] Live smoke against running container: XLSX downloads, opens via openpyxl with expected shape.
- [ ] CI: ruff format, ruff check, mypy, pytest, svelte-check, vitest.
- [ ] Browser smoke: click Export XLSX in the result view → file downloads with correct filename.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

entries.title: #74: M3 Phase F (planning) — OpenTelemetry deepening: trace correlation, domain spans, deployment recipes
entries.text:
M3 Phase F (planning) — OpenTelemetry deepening: trace correlation, domain spans, deployment recipes ## Summary

Lands the **planning artifacts** for M3 Phase F (Observability deepening) on top of PR #62 (M3-C3 Tabular Review polish). Does not implement Phase F itself — that's a separate ~32–42-hour body of work captured here for sequencing.

Three docs deliverables:

1. **`docs/proposals/opentelemetry-deepening.md`** — the OTel-deepening mini-PRD. New `docs/proposals/` directory documented in CLAUDE.md's decision-routing table as the home for contributor-pickup mini-PRDs.
2. **`docs/M3-IMPLEMENTATION-PLAN.md` §Phase F** — three tasks (M3-F1 trace propagation audit + regression test; M3-F2 domain spans with anonymization-of-attributes guarantee; M3-F3 deployment recipes + `docs/observability.md`) keyed to the mini-PRD's three PRs.
3. **`docs/PRD.md` §9** — DE-299 through DE-303, the five OTel-adjacent surfaces the mini-PRD's DE-A through DE-E identified (SQLAlchemy + ARQ instrumentation; log-trace correlation; OTel MeterProvider for metrics; OpenWebUI-fork OTel reconciliation; browser RUM with opt-in + CSP review).

Phase F is positioned **after M3-E1 + M3-E2** (fresh-install verification + docs finalization) so:
- E1's fresh-install verification gets the OTel deployment recipes as a test target.
- E2's docs finalization absorbs `docs/observability.md` as one of the M3-close documents.

## Effort + sequencing

| | Tasks | Effort |
|---|---|---|
| M3 active (pre-F) | 16 | ~161–218 hours |
| Phase F | 3 | ~32–42 hours |
| **M3 active (with F)** | **19** | **~193–260 hours** |

Pushes the M3 full-time build from ~5 weeks to ~5–6 weeks; part-time from ~8–9 weeks to ~9–10 weeks.

## Why this is its own PR

Phase F is non-trivial scope (~6 engineering days) with three deliverables and explicit acceptance criteria; folding the planning into PR #62 would conflate M3-C3's substrate work with M3-F's planning. Reviewer routing is also distinct: Phase F's mini-PRD touches observability + security CODEOWNERS (the anonymization-of-attributes guarantee in PR 2; the CSP review for DE-303 browser RUM).

## Base

Based on `m3-phase-c-tabular-review` (PR #62). After PR #62 merges, the base flips to `main` automatically.

## Test plan

- [ ] Reviewer reads `docs/proposals/opentelemetry-deepening.md` end-to-end and signs off on the three-PR breakdown, the anonymization-of-attributes guarantee for PR 2, and the opt-in posture for DE-303 browser RUM.
- [ ] Reviewer cross-references DE-299..303 from `docs/M3-IMPLEMENTATION-PLAN.md §Phase F` and confirms the M3 plan correctly anchors each task to its corresponding mini-PRD section.
- [ ] CLAUDE.md routing table change is uncontroversial (a docs-only addition; no behavioral impact).
- [ ] CI passes (docs-only PR; should be fast).

entries.title: #61: Sync m3-development → main — M3-A6 Easy Playbook wizard
entries.text:
Sync m3-development → main — M3-A6 Easy Playbook wizard ## Summary

Restores single-trunk M3 development on `main`. After PR #59 (M3 Phase B plumbing) shipped to `main`, the `m3-development` branch carried the only remaining unmerged M3 work: PR #57's M3-A6 Easy Playbook wizard. This PR brings that work onto `main` so Phase C / D / E can branch off `main` directly per the precedent set in Phase B.

**Path chosen.** Cherry-picked the M3-A6 squash commit (`a7aa719`) onto a fresh sync branch off `main` instead of a literal `m3-development` → `main` merge. Rationale:

| Path | Conflicts | Side effects |
|---|---|---|
| Literal `m3-development` → `main` merge | 12 files (many `add/add` from squash-merge history divergence) | Drags `37aadac` stale pre-PR-#58 handoff doc onto `main`; PRs #56/#58/#59/#60 handoff docs would land on `m3-development` from `main`'s side |
| Cherry-pick `a7aa719` onto sync branch (this PR) | 4 files (clean) | Pure M3-A6 delta; `m3-development` stays at `37aadac` untouched on `origin` as a historical archive of the M3 Phase A arc |

The cherry-pick path honors the branch-preservation policy more strictly (zero writes to `m3-development`) and gives `main` a single clean commit when squash-merged.

## Conflict resolutions (the only places where judgment was exercised)

1. **`api/tests/test_endpoints.py`** — merged the IMPLEMENTED_ROUTES list to include both PR #59's Word add-in routes AND M3-A6's 5 new playbook routes (POST/PATCH/DELETE `/playbooks` + 2 `/playbooks/easy`). M3-A6 routes listed immediately after the M3-A4 playbook block.

2. **`api/tests/test_openapi.py`** — kept both PR #59 and M3-A6 explanatory comments; updated `assert len(actual)` from 81 → 86 (M3-A6 adds 5 new paths on top of PR #59's 81).

3. **`docs/PRD.md` §9** — inserted DE-284/285/286 (M3-A6 follow-on DEs) numerically before DE-287/.../DE-295 (PR #58 + PR #59's descope DEs). No content lost from either side.

4. **`docs/architecture.md` §M3** — kept HEAD's plumbing-only Slack/Teams framing and the Word add-in delivery-flow paragraph (both from PRs #58/#59); added the `arq-worker` infrastructure bullet from M3-A6 (genuinely new substrate that needs documentation).

The other conflict-zone candidate from the handoff — `api/app/api/__init__.py` and `docs/M3-IMPLEMENTATION-PLAN.md` — auto-merged cleanly. Several `add/add` candidates avoided entirely by using cherry-pick.

## What this PR brings to main

The full M3-A6 Easy Playbook wizard backend + frontend per PR #57 (70 files, +10,758 / -56 lines):
- ARQ worker infrastructure (`arq-worker` sister to `ingest-worker` on its own `arq:m3a6` queue)
- Playbook CRUD endpoints (POST/PATCH/DELETE on `/api/v1/playbooks`)
- `playbook-easy-extract` skill + Python extractor
- Centroid-based clustering + assembly
- Easy Playbook generation endpoint + ARQ worker + migrations 0034/0035
- Frontend wizard (4-step, full inline editor in Step 3)
- 109 new passing tests
- Cypress E2E

## Test plan

- [ ] CI passes (ruff format, ruff check, mypy strict on gateway, pytest on api + gateway + web vitest)
- [ ] After merge: `m3-development` stays on `origin` per branch-preservation policy
- [ ] After merge: branch Phase C off the new `main` head

Closes the m3-development reconciliation step. Phase C (Tabular Review) opens next on a fresh branch off the post-merge `main`.

entries.title: #59: M3 Phase B plumbing: Word add-in scaffold + OAuth + version handshake
entries.text:
M3 Phase B plumbing: Word add-in scaffold + OAuth + version handshake ## Summary

Lands three of the four M3 Phase B plumbing tasks — Word add-in **scaffold** (M3-B1), **OAuth** (M3-B2), and **version handshake + update-needed overlay** (M3-B8). The fourth — **M3-B7 (signed manifest + enterprise distribution package)** — is descoped to a **community-led effort** per [PRD §9 DE-295](https://github.com/LegalQuants/lq-ai/blob/main/docs/PRD.md#de-295--word-add-in-code-signing-certificate--signed-manifest-ci-community-led) (filed in this PR).

At v0.3.0 this puts an installable, authenticated Word add-in into operators' hands via the **unsigned-manifest sideload path**: sideload the manifest via Microsoft 365 Admin Center (will show "unsigned add-in" warning, which is expected at v0.3.0 until DE-295 closes), sign in to your LQ.AI deployment via an Office.js dialog, see the empty task pane with deep-link cards to the web app. The user-facing feature surface inside the tabs (chat / skills / playbook execution / Inference Tier badge) stays descoped to M4 / community contribution per [DE-287](https://github.com/LegalQuants/lq-ai/blob/main/docs/PRD.md#de-287--word-add-in-feature-surface-chat-skills-playbooks-tier-badge--deferred-to-m4--community-contribution).

## What's in this PR

Five commits — one prep doc + three feature commits + one M3-B7 descope:

* **`c2318fd` docs(m3-phase-b): prep notes — Word add-in plumbing (B1 + B2 + B7 + B8)**
  Locks 8 design decisions at Phase B kickoff. (B-5 and B-6 are later resolved by the M3-B7 community descope in the final commit; the execution log captures the resolution.)
* **`c17223e` feat(m3-b1): Word add-in scaffold (manifest + task pane shell + admin manifest generator)**
  New `word-addin/` directory with Office.js manifest template + React 18 task pane (Header, TabStrip, DeepLinkCard) + commands stub + placeholder icons. Backend admin endpoint `GET /api/v1/admin/word-addin/manifest` renders the templated manifest with the operator's deployment URL + a fresh GUID per download. SvelteKit admin UI at `/lq-ai/admin/word-addin` for the manifest generation form.
* **`70dc009` feat(m3-b2): Word add-in OAuth (Office.js Dialog API + LQ.AI JWT)**
  Office.js Dialog API helper + token storage in `localStorage` (not `Office.context.document.settings` — auth spans documents, not per-doc) + `authenticatedFetch` with single-flight 401-refresh-retry + SignInGate component. SvelteKit dialog page at `/lq-ai/word-addin/oauth-start` (with layout reset) posts the JWT back via `Office.context.ui.messageParent`. Reuses the existing `/api/v1/auth/login` + `/auth/refresh` — no new backend auth endpoint required.
* **`aad9ced` feat(m3-b8): Word add-in version handshake + update-needed overlay**
  Unauthenticated `GET /api/v1/word-addin/version` returns deployment version + compatible add-in version range + bundle URL + nullable `taskpane_bundle_hash` (populated later by M3-B7's signing CI). `webpack.config.js` injects `__ADDIN_VERSION__` from `package.json` at build time. Task pane runs the version check on mount and renders one of three states: blocking update overlay (out-of-range), soft "version unknown" banner (handshake failed), or normal sign-in / authenticated layout (compatible). Backend router split into `admin_router` (manifest, AdminUser-gated) + `public_router` (version, unauthenticated).
* **`0e8ea52` chore(m3-b7): descope to community-led effort per DE-295**
  Files DE-295 with the procurement plan, three credible community paths (SignPath open-source sponsorship recommended; community-funded DigiCert EV / Sectigo OV alternatives), the five operator-UX implications gated until the cert lands, and the acceptance criteria. Updates PRD §3.9 + the M3 implementation plan + Decision M3-5 + the prep doc's Decision B-5/B-6 + the prep doc execution log to reflect the community handoff.

## M3-B7 community handoff in one paragraph

Code-signing cert procurement is real-world purchase + ongoing annual renewal with multi-week lead time and per-vendor identity verification. Treating it as maintainer work would couple the project's release cadence to a procurement clock the maintainer team doesn't otherwise need to run. **SignPath's open-source sponsorship is the recommended first path** — it's free for qualifying OSS, comes with the signing CI integration and HSM-managed private key, and ships EV-tier SmartScreen reputation on the same timeline as a paid cert. Community-funded DigiCert EV (~$500–700/yr) or Sectigo OV (~$200–300/yr) are alternatives. The cert is issued to **LegalQuants, Inc.** regardless of path — a community member organizes procurement / funding; LegalQuants signs the application materials and holds the legal cert artifact. See DE-295 for the full procurement plan + the five operator-UX implications gated until the signed distribution lands.

## What's NOT in this PR

* **The feature surface inside the task pane.** M3-B3 (chat) / M3-B4 (skills) / M3-B5 (playbook execution) / M3-B6 (Inference Tier badge) are descoped to M4 / community contribution per [DE-287](https://github.com/LegalQuants/lq-ai/blob/main/docs/PRD.md#de-287--word-add-in-feature-surface-chat-skills-playbooks-tier-badge--deferred-to-m4--community-contribution). Each tab renders a deep-link card pointing at the equivalent web-app surface so the add-in is usable at v0.3.0; community contributors fork against this plumbing to land the in-Word surfaces.
* **M3-B7 (signed manifest + enterprise distribution package).** Community-led per DE-295 (filed in this PR's final commit). Lands as a community PR (likely v0.3.1 or v0.3.2 release tag) once the cert procurement closes.
* **JSON manifest migration.** XML manifest 1.1+ is the production path for Word as of early 2026; JSON manifest is still in preview. A DE follow-on migrates once JSON GA's for Word.
* **Add-in-scoped JWT (`aud: word-addin`).** The prep doc's M3-B2 scope originally listed this; reconsidered during implementation because every other endpoint would need to accept the new audience (or a deferred refactor) without any v0.3.0 use case that benefits. The add-in uses the same JWT shape as the web app. Future DE if endpoint-level revocation becomes load-bearing.
* **Live `taskpane_bundle_hash` value.** Schema field exists in M3-B8 and ships nullable; the field is populated when M3-B7's signing CI lands (computed from the build manifest).

## Decisions logged in the prep doc

The prep doc (`docs/superpowers/plans/2026-05-21-m3-phase-b-word-addin-plumbing.md`) locks 8 design decisions at Phase B kickoff and its execution log captures side-channel decisions made during implementation:

* Router split (`admin_router` + `public_router`) for the unauthenticated version endpoint
* `localStorage` for token storage rather than `Office.context.document.settings`
* Skipped the `POST /api/v1/word-addin/exchange-token` endpoint
* Compatibility range as module constants, not env vars (for v0.3.0)
* `taskpane_bundle_hash` nullable in M3-B8; populated when M3-B7 community PR lands
* **Decision B-5 (cert vendor) — RESOLVED**: M3-B7 community-led per DE-295. SignPath recommended; community-funded DigiCert EV / Sectigo OV alternatives.
* **Decision B-6 (PR strategy) — UPDATED**: PR #2 becomes a community PR rather than a maintainer-team follow-up.

## Test plan

Automated coverage (all green):

- [x] **Backend pytest** — 468 passed (+7 over the M3-A6 baseline of 465; 6 new from this PR plus 1 from PR #58), 14 skipped (integration tests skip without Postgres), no regressions.
- [x] **Backend ruff** — `ruff format --check` + `ruff check` clean across `api/app/` (95 files).
- [x] **Backend mypy** — strict mode, 0 issues across `api/app/` (96 source files).
- [x] **Web vitest** — 488 passed, no regressions.
- [x] **Web svelte-check** — 0 errors (6 pre-existing warnings in unrelated components).
- [x] **Add-in vitest** — 39 tests (22 auth + 17 version); test runner first introduced in `word-addin/` in this PR. Designed against jsdom; runs when `npm install` lands on a contributor / CI machine.
- [x] **OpenAPI conformance** — `test_openapi_paths_match_sketch` updated for the two new paths (manifest + version), expected count bumped 79 → 81.

Manual verification path (operator-side, lands in M3-E1 fresh-install):

- [ ] `cd word-addin && npm install && npm run build` — populates `web/static/word-addin/`. Needs Node 18+ on the contributor machine.
- [ ] `docker compose up --build` — web container picks up the static bundle via the existing SvelteKit `COPY . .` step.
- [ ] In LQ.AI admin UI: navigate to **Admin → Word add-in** → click **Generate manifest** → download the XML file.
- [ ] In Microsoft 365 Admin Center: sideload the manifest via **Settings → Integrated apps → Upload custom apps → Office Add-in**. Admin Center will warn about the unsigned add-in (expected at v0.3.0 until DE-295 closes).
- [ ] In Word for Microsoft 365 desktop: open any document → see **LQ.AI** on the Home ribbon → click **Open LQ.AI** → task pane opens with the empty layout.
- [ ] Click **Sign in** → Office.js dialog opens against the deployment → log in → dialog closes and the task pane renders the authenticated layout.
- [ ] Each tab (Chat / Skills / Playbooks) shows a deep-link card; clicking the CTA opens the equivalent web-app surface in a new browser tab.
- [ ] Sign-out clears the session; reload the task pane → SignInGate shows again.

Verification deferred to M3-E1 fresh-install (no Word client in the dev environment):

- Cross-platform: Word for Microsoft 365 desktop (Windows + macOS) + Word for the web.
- Stale-version scenario: install an "older" add-in build, confirm the update-needed overlay surfaces.

## Refs

* M3 Phase B prep doc — `docs/superpowers/plans/2026-05-21-m3-phase-b-word-addin-plumbing.md`
* PRD §3.9 (Word Add-In, updated in this PR), §9 DE-287 (feature-surface descope rationale), §9 DE-295 (community-led signed-distribution plan, filed in this PR)
* M3 Implementation Plan — `docs/M3-IMPLEMENTATION-PLAN.md` (Phase B tasks, Decision M3-5 revised in this PR)

entries.title: #58: docs: boundary-register restraint catalog + M3-A6 close-out descope cleanup
entries.text:
docs: boundary-register restraint catalog + M3-A6 close-out descope cleanup ## Summary

- **Boundary-register catalog adopted as the framework for LQ.AI's restraint work** (Greenwood, May 2026 — "The Most Interesting Thing in Claude for Legal Is the Lawyer/Agent Boundary"). PRD §1.8 gains a posture subsection citing Greenwood once on first use; PRD §3.10 names the Tier 2 obligations the M4 design must discharge; a new posture document at `docs/security/boundary-registers.md` tracks each register's state with line-level source citations, refreshed at every milestone close.
- **Six new DEs filed** (DE-289 through DE-294) for the implementation work the catalog surfaces: Lavern as design reference, the posture document itself (✓ closed in this PR), R1 codification, Playbook executor retrofit, autonomous-layer Tier 2 implementation, and cross-agent handoff validation.
- **M3-A6 close-out descope cleanup** (separate commit): files DE-287 (Word add-in feature surface) and DE-288 (Slack/Teams `/lq` slash command) as deferred to M4 / community contribution per the 2026-05-21 scope-reduction decision; updates `docs/M3-IMPLEMENTATION-PLAN.md` with inline "Descoped to M4" markers on the affected tasks; revises the total-effort estimate (~227–302 hr down to ~174–235 hr).

## Framing decisions adjudicated (2026-05-21)

Per the parked handoff doc's §0:

1. **Vocabulary attribution.** "Registers of restraint" (Greenwood's coinage) is **cited once on first use in §1.8, then used as project vocabulary**. Same posture as the PRD's existing reference to ABA Formal Opinion 512.
2. **"Six is not load-bearing."** The catalog is treated as a **living artifact**, not a fixed enumeration. PRD §1.8 explicitly names this: "The catalog is expected to grow as community practice matures … the goal is not to ship 'six of six' as a marketing claim but to make every register's state … verifiable in source." Matches Greenwood's own framing ("two codebases is not a survey").

## Honest count today (verifiable in `docs/security/boundary-registers.md`)

| Register | Tier | State |
|---|---|---|
| R1 — prompt/workflow | how | Fully (codification via DE-291) |
| R2 — capability | how | Fully, adapted (Inference Tier model; agent-tool-grant retrofit via DE-292) |
| R3 — code | how | Partial (gateway + Citation Engine + Anonymization + Playbook typed transitions; closed-intent + audit retrofit via DE-292 / DE-294) |
| R4 — economic | what else | Partial (per-call + ensemble pre-flight; per-execution + per-session caps via DE-292 / DE-293) |
| R5 — temporal | what else | Not yet (ARQ `job_timeout` is a hard-kill; graceful halt via DE-293) |
| R6 — contextual | what else | Not yet (via DE-293) |
| Orthogonal | Inference Choice Spectrum | Fully (PRD §1.5.2 / §3.13 / §4.4) |

Net: **2 fully, 2 partial, 2 deferred-with-architectural-commitment.**

## Reclassification

The prior CC's roadmap-enhancement proposal §2.4 sequenced Playbook updates (declared tool grants + schema-validated step handoffs + per-execution cost cap) as in-PR edits to §3.7 to land *before* the Playbook executor merged. The executor shipped in M3-A2 (`d08bd51`) and M3-A6 PR #57 added more surface on top, so the sequencing is moot. Reclassified as **DE-292** — a retrofit of the existing executor, not an in-PR §3.7 edit.

## Renumbering

The prior CC's DE numbering (DE-265 through DE-269) is fully stale — DE-265 through DE-286 are all claimed on `m3-development` after M3 Phase A shipping. This PR uses **DE-287 → DE-294**. The `de265.patch` from the parked branch is not applied; the content was rewritten with the new numbers.

## Test plan

This is a docs-only PR. Verification steps:

- [ ] `grep -n DE-289 docs/PRD.md docs/security/boundary-registers.md` — six new DEs cross-reference cleanly
- [ ] `grep -n boundary-register docs/PRD.md` — §1.8 + §3.10 additions present
- [ ] `cat docs/security/boundary-registers.md` reads cleanly end-to-end
- [ ] M3-IMPLEMENTATION-PLAN.md descope markers — M3-B3/B4/B5/B6 have "Descoped to M4" status; M3-D2 has same; M3-D3/D4 carry inline plumbing-only scope clarifications; M3-E1 + M3-E2 reflect the v0.3.0 shipping state
- [ ] Total-effort table updated to ~174–235 hr range

No code changes, no migrations, no test deltas.

## What this PR does NOT do (so a reviewer can verify scope was held)

- **No implementation.** DE-291 (R1 codification + golden tests) is mid-M3 work, not in this PR. DE-292 (Playbook executor retrofit) is post-M3-A6 follow-on work, not in this PR. DE-293 / DE-294 are M4 specifications, not in this PR.
- **No re-scoping of existing milestones beyond the M3-A6 close decisions already taken.** M3 Phases B and D are descoped per Kevin's 2026-05-21 call (filed as DE-287 / DE-288); Phase C remains in M3; Phase E remains in M3.
- **No marketing claims.** The honest count is 2/2/2 with deferred-with-commitment framing. The PRD does not state "LQ.AI implements six registers" or similar.
- **No new vocabulary outside Greenwood's catalog.** Adding "boundary registers" extends a small vocabulary; no other new terms.
- **The parked branch (`roadmap-enhancements-parked`) is preserved** per the branch-preservation policy. The source article `draft_05.docx` and the prior CC's two handoff docs remain there as the historical record of the analysis.

## After this lands

Next session work resumes M3 Phase B plumbing on a new branch off the resulting state. Start the **code-signing certificate procurement** immediately when Phase B begins (multi-week lead time per the M3 plan).

entries.title: #57: feat(m3-a6): Easy Playbook wizard — generate playbooks from prior agreements (7 phases)
entries.text:
feat(m3-a6): Easy Playbook wizard — generate playbooks from prior agreements (7 phases) ## Summary

Ships M3-A6: a 4-step wizard that turns a corpus of prior contracts into a draft playbook. The operator uploads PDFs, picks a contract type, waits while the backend extracts + clusters + assembles a draft, edits the assembled positions inline, and saves the result as a regular playbook. Lands the 3 deferred-from-M3-A4 CRUD endpoints (POST/PATCH/DELETE) along the way.

Closes the M3-A6 7-phase plan (`docs/superpowers/plans/2026-05-19-m3-a6-easy-playbook-wizard.md`) **and** the post-smoke iteration arc that surfaced + fixed a load-bearing extraction bug, a worker timeout misconfiguration, and a clustering-quality regression. Cross-corpus validation against synthetic NDAs + MSAs confirms the algorithm generalizes.

## What this PR delivers

### Phases 1–7 (the original 7-phase plan)

| Phase | Commit | What |
|---|---|---|
| 1 | `1feec2e` | ARQ worker infrastructure (queue-isolated `arq:m3a6` pool) |
| 2 | `c8b65a9` | Playbook CRUD endpoints + soft-delete migration 0034 |
| 3 | `88dadb6` | `playbook-easy-extract` skill + extractor module |
| 4 | `d19cfe2` | Clustering + draft assembly (modal + farthest-N neighbors per label) |
| 5 | `af65598` | Easy Playbook endpoints + ARQ worker + migration 0035 |
| 5a | `d7c2909` | **Phase 6 prereq:** surface `document_id` on `FileMetadata` |
| 6 | `5e4ea7c` | **Frontend wizard** — 4-step state machine + full inline editor + 3 new components |
| 7 | `bb26cac` | Cypress E2E spec + suite-bookkeeping fix for Phases 2 + 5 |
| 7a | `0138446` | DE-284 filed for pre-existing api/tests/ mypy debt + carry to session handoff |

### Post-smoke iteration (load-bearing fixes + clustering improvement)

Live-stack testing with real Opus + real embeddings surfaced 4 distinct issues, each fixed in a focused commit:

| Commit | What | Why it matters |
|---|---|---|
| `4aa3a8c` | `fix(api): omit temperature for Opus 4.x reasoning models (M3-A2 + M3-A6)` | Without this, every wizard extraction silently failed (and the M3-A2 executor would too against Opus). The wizard produced 0-position drafts before the fix. |
| `e0de295` | `fix(web,m3-a6): hardcode accent-color fallbacks on wizard surfaces` | "3. Review" pill was invisible (white text on white) because `--lq-accent` isn't always defined on the OpenWebUI theme. Matches the fallback pattern from `PlaybookExecuteModal`. |
| `681376d` | `fix(api,m3-a6): raise arq job_timeout to 900s + catch BaseException on cancel` | ARQ's 300s default cut NFR-conformant runs off mid-assembly; orphan "running" rows resulted because `CancelledError` bypassed the `except Exception` clause. |
| `76ad689` | `feat(api,m3-a6): centroid-based clustering merge to dedupe semantically-similar labels` | First-cut label-embedding merge fired on essentially nothing (a similarity probe showed label-only cosine is the wrong signal). Switched to clause-text centroids — NDA-corpus output went from 35 positions with many duplicates to 18 well-merged positions. |
| `daa9a5a` | `docs(m3-a6): bundle 5 synthetic mutual NDAs for the Easy Playbook wizard` | 5-NDA corpus at `docs/quickstart/sample-ndas/` for wizard testing. Variant matrix covers term, definition scope, standard of care, trade-secret survival, permitted disclosures. |
| `1752b04` | `docs(m3-a6): bundle 5 synthetic mutual MSAs for cross-corpus wizard validation` | 5-MSA corpus at `docs/quickstart/sample-msas/` for cross-corpus validation. Variant matrix covers payment terms, IP ownership, warranty scope, termination triggers, indemnification scope. |
| `1c3d250` | `docs(m3-a6): file DE-286 — cross-document label normalization on richer contracts` | Captures the secondary finding from MSA validation. P2 follow-on. |

## Verification

| Check | Result |
|---|---|
| Backend full suite | **1226/1226 pass** |
| Frontend vitest | **513/513 pass** |
| Cypress E2E spec | **1/1 pass** (~3s headless) |
| svelte-check on `tsconfig.lq-ai.json` | **0 errors** |
| ruff format + check on `api/` | clean |
| mypy on `app/` (CI gate) | clean |

### Live cross-corpus validation against real provider keys (Anthropic Opus + OpenAI embeddings)

| Corpus | Documents | Positions produced | Cross-doc fallback coverage | Runtime |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| NDA (`docs/quickstart/sample-ndas/`) | 5 mutual NDAs | **18** | 12 of 18 with 2 fallback tiers; 5 with 1 tier | 235s |
| MSA (`docs/quickstart/sample-msas/`) | 5 mutual MSAs | **26** | 0 (all singletons — see below) | 805s |

**On the MSA result.** The 26 positions correctly cover all 5 variant axes built into the corpus (payment / IP / warranties / termination / indemnification). The legal sub-concepts within each axis are kept as distinct positions ("Customer Indemnification" vs "Vendor Indemnification" vs "Indemnification Cap and Exceptions"), which is arguably more useful to a user-attorney than collapsing them. The cross-document fallback mechanism didn't activate because the extractor produces document-specific labels on richer contract types that don't exact-match across docs, and the 0.85 centroid threshold isn't low enough to bridge them. Filed as **DE-286** for follow-on tuning; Decision F's "user-attorney edit is the safety net" authorization covers the current behavior.

## Design decisions locked

Per the M3-A6 kickoff (2026-05-19):

| # | Decision |
|---|---|
| §3.1 | ARQ on dedicated `arq:m3a6` queue (queue isolation from document-ingest) |
| §3.2 | CRUD endpoints ship in this PR |
| §3.3 | Uploaded contracts persist to user's library by default |
| §3.4 | **Full** inline editor (every editable field per position + per fallback tier) |
| F | Wizard output is itself a starting point; user-attorney validates in Step 3 inline editor, not the maintainer team |

## DEs filed during this PR

- **DE-284** (P3, M) — Tighten api/tests/ mypy coverage (211 pre-existing errors as of m3-a6; CI gate scoped to `mypy app`). Mechanical sweep + CI gate flip.
- **DE-285** (P2, M) — First-run sample-NDA knowledge base for the Easy Playbook wizard (in-app "Try with sample NDAs" CTA backed by a system-managed KB seeded at bootstrap). Generalizes to sample MSAs / DPAs for other built-in playbooks.
- **DE-286** (P2, M) — Cross-document label normalization on richer contract types. Two parallel paths (extractor-side constrained vocabulary OR clustering-side per-contract-type threshold tuning). Acceptance criteria tied to the 5-NDA + 5-MSA bench shipped in this PR.

## Architectural notes worth carrying forward

- **`document_id` now surfaces on `GET /files/{id}`** (commit `d7c2909`). The Easy Playbook backend takes Document UUIDs, not File UUIDs; before this PR that field was only exposed via the `KnowledgeBaseFile` join. Now any file endpoint can hand the wizard a parsed-document handle.
- **Per-phase commits ALSO need to update `IMPLEMENTED_ROUTES` + `EXPECTED_PATHS`.** Phases 2 + 5 didn't, and the Phase 7 full-suite sweep caught the drift. Fixed in `bb26cac`; tracked as a process gap in the session handoff §9 item 9.
- **Centroid-based clustering merge** (commit `76ad689`) replaces the original exact-label-match grouping. The default 0.85 threshold was tuned against the NDA corpus; DE-286 documents the per-contract-type tuning need for richer document types.
- **`temperature` is omitted from all playbook LLM calls** (commit `4aa3a8c`) — the gateway only forwards non-None values to providers, so this is the correct posture for both reasoning models (which reject the parameter) and sampled models (which keep the provider default).

## Test plan

```bash
# Pull the branch and rebuild — doc_id schema change is in the api image
git checkout m3-a6-easy-playbook-wizard
docker compose up -d --build api arq-worker ingest-worker web
```

### Smoke against the NDA corpus (matches the live-test result above)

- [ ] Log in at <http://localhost:3000/lq-ai/login>
- [ ] Navigate to `/lq-ai/playbooks` → confirm new **"Generate from prior agreements"** CTA in the header
- [ ] Click the CTA → land on `/lq-ai/playbooks/easy`, Step 1 visible
- [ ] Type `NDA` in contract type
- [ ] Drag all 5 PDFs from `docs/quickstart/sample-ndas/` into the dropzone
- [ ] Click **Generate playbook** → expect ~4 min runtime against the `smart` alias (`claude-opus-4-7`)
- [ ] Step 3 inline editor renders with ~18 positions; ~12 carry 2 fallback tiers each
- [ ] Edit one position's `issue`; reorder positions via ↑/↓; remove + re-add a fallback tier
- [ ] Click **Save playbook** → Step 4 success screen
- [ ] Back to `/lq-ai/playbooks` → confirm the new playbook is listed; click Apply on it to confirm it round-trips through the executor

### Cross-corpus validation against the MSA corpus

- [ ] Repeat the above with the 5 PDFs from `docs/quickstart/sample-msas/`
- [ ] Expect ~26 singleton positions covering all 5 MSA variant axes — payment / IP / warranties / termination / indemnification — but no cross-document fallback tiers
- [ ] Confirm this matches DE-286's documented behavior

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

entries.title: #55: docs(playbooks,m3-a3): retro-update NDA descriptions to match M3-A5 starting-point framing
entries.text:
docs(playbooks,m3-a3): retro-update NDA descriptions to match M3-A5 starting-point framing ## Summary

Small docs alignment: retro-update the M3-A3 NDA — Mutual and NDA — Unilateral playbook descriptions to use M3-A5's stronger "starting point, not a vetted template" framing.

The M3-A3 descriptions used implicit framing ("operator's responsibility to apply professional judgment") that was technically consistent with Decision F but read as softer than M3-A5's explicit framing. The 2026-05-19 posture clarification (see `feedback_no_maintainer_legal_review.md`) locked in: the maintainer team does NOT review legal content; the in-house attorney is the validator; built-in playbooks are starting points / placeholders.

This commit makes the NDA descriptions explicit on that posture, matching the M3-A5 playbooks. Also removes one inadvertently-inaccurate line from the NDA-Mutual header comment ("drafted by the maintainer team and reviewed before merge") that conflicted with the actual posture.

## What changed

| File | Change |
|---|---|
| `skills/playbooks/nda/playbook.yaml` | Header comment + `description` updated to explicit "starting point, not a vetted template" framing |
| `skills/playbooks/nda-unilateral/playbook.yaml` | Same update; the unilateral variant had similar implicit framing |

**No legal content modified** — descriptions only. Positions, fallback tiers, redline strategies, severities, detection keywords/examples all unchanged.

## Tests

Both descriptions retain "professional judgment" alongside the new "starting point" phrasing, so:
- `test_description_includes_not_legal_advice_disclaimer` (M3-A3; strict on "professional judgment") still passes
- M3-A5's test (accepts either idiom) still passes

Verified: 50/50 tests pass across `test_builtin_nda_playbooks.py` + `test_builtin_msa_dpa_playbooks.py`.

## Migration drift caveat

Migration 0032's idempotency check (skip-if-(name, version)-exists) means existing operator stacks that have already run 0032 will NOT automatically see the new description — the v1.0.0 DB rows are locked. The YAML is now source-of-truth-correct for:
- Fresh installs (operators running 0032 for the first time)
- Operators who wipe + reapply

For description-only changes (no semantic content), the YAML-truth update is sufficient. A v1.0.1 backport migration is possible but not pursued here. Filed as a candidate follow-on if anyone wants existing stacks to surface the updated framing without a wipe.

## Test plan

- [x] Both NDA YAMLs parse and validate against `PlaybookCreate`
- [x] M3-A3 disclaimer test passes (description contains "not legal advice" AND "professional judgment")
- [x] M3-A5 disclaimer test passes (description contains "not legal advice" AND ("professional judgment" OR "starting point"))
- [x] Migration round-trip test passes (DB description matches YAML after fresh seed)
